name: OER Deceleration -> Long TLT
rule: Use FRED CUSR0000SEHC (CPI shelter/OER proxy). When YoY decelerates by >2pp from 6mo peak: long TLT 126 days. Shelter disinflation = lower CPI = bond rally.

backtest_result.reason: no trigger events found

name: Bank Reserves Surge -> Long QQQ
rule: Long QQQ 126d when TOTRESNS 3-month rolling change > +15%

name: Mining IP Recovery -> Long XOP+PICK
rule: Long XOP+PICK 126d when IPN213111S YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative

name: Auto Loan DQ Trough → Long ALLY+COF
rule: Load FRED DRCARLACBS (quarterly auto loan delinquency rate). Find local minima below 2.5%. Long ALLY+COF equal-weight for 252 trading days from the quarter after the trough.

name: Personal Income YoY Reaccelerates > +5% → Long XLY
rule: Load FRED PI (monthly personal income). Compute YoY growth. When YoY crosses above +5% after 6+ months below +3%: long XLY for 126 trading days. Income acceleration leads consumer spending by 1-2 months.

backtest_result.verdict: WINNER — strong Sharpe (1.36), CAGR (25.6%), 6/7 wins, t=2.55

name: FRED Corporate Profits YoY Turns Positive After 2Q Negative → Long SPY 12mo
rule: Load FRED CP (quarterly corporate profits after tax with IVA and CCAdj). Compute YoY % change each quarter. Entry: when CP YoY turns positive after 2+ consecutive quarters of negative YoY growth. Long SPY for 252 trading days (12 months). Exit after 252 trading days or if CP YoY turns negative again (stop-loss). Benchmark: SPY buy-and-hold.
entry_conditions: FRED CP YoY growth turns positive (>0%) after 2+ consecutive quarters of negative YoY
Signal evaluated quarterly when BEA releases NIPA corporate profits data
exit_conditions: Early exit if CP YoY turns negative again within holding period

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED CP (quarterly corporate profits after tax)

backtest_approach: Load FRED CP quarterly back to 1947 (or whenever SPY data starts ~1993). Compute YoY. Identify all transition events from negative->positive YoY. On the first trading day after each event, go long SPY for 252 days. Compute per-trade returns and aggregate Sharpe/CAGR/max_dd vs buy-and-hold SPY. Known events: ~2002Q1 (post dot-com), ~2010Q1 (post GFC), ~2016Q2 (post energy recession), ~2020Q4 (post COVID).
implementation_notes: FRED CP is quarterly with ~2 month lag. BEA advance estimate comes ~1 month after quarter end (late Apr for Q1, late Jul for Q2, etc). Use release date, not quarter date. CP series has IVA+CCAdj adjustments — use the adjusted series for cleaner signal.

name: FRED Private Nonres Construction YoY > +10% for 3mo → Long PWR+PRIM+ACM
rule: Load FRED PNRESCONS (monthly private nonresidential construction spending). Compute YoY % change. Entry: when YoY exceeds +10% for 3 consecutive months. Long equal-weight basket of PWR, PRIM, ACM plus SPY benchmark for 126 trading days. Exit after 126 days.
entry_conditions: FRED PNRESCONS YoY growth > +10% for 3 consecutive months
Signal evaluated monthly on construction spending release (1st business day of month, 2-month lag)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED PNRESCONS (monthly private nonresidential construction spending)

backtest_approach: Load FRED PNRESCONS monthly back to 2002. Compute YoY. Identify all 3-month streak entries where YoY > +10%. On the first trading day after the 3rd consecutive month confirmation, go long equal-weight PWR+PRIM+ACM for 126 days. Compute basket return vs SPY. Note: PRIM may have limited history (IPO ~2018); use available tickers only for their available period. PWR has data back to ~2003.
known_events: 2005-2006 (housing boom commercial)
2014-2015 (energy capex)
2022-2025 (reshoring/data center)
implementation_notes: PRIM IPO was 2018 — only use it from 2018 onward. ACM has data back to ~2014 (post-URS merger). PWR back to ~2002. For early period, basket may be PWR-only. Construction spending data has a 2-month lag (e.g., January data released in March).

name: FRED Real Disposable Income YoY Turns Negative → Long XLP 12mo
rule: Load FRED DSPIC96 (monthly real disposable personal income). Compute YoY % change. Entry: first month YoY turns negative (<0%) after 12+ months of positive YoY. Long XLP for 252 trading days. Measure XLP relative performance vs SPY over the holding period. Thesis: during real income squeezes, staples outperform discretionary/broad market.
entry_conditions: FRED DSPIC96 YoY turns negative (<0%) for the first time after 12+ months positive
Signal evaluated monthly on BEA personal income release (~last week of month, 1-month lag)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED DSPIC96 (monthly real disposable personal income)

backtest_approach: Load FRED DSPIC96 monthly back to 1959. Compute YoY. Identify all transition events from positive->negative YoY after 12+ months positive. For each event, long XLP for 252 trading days and compute excess return vs SPY. XLP data starts ~1998; historical events before that can only test with sector proxies or be excluded. Key metric: XLP minus SPY total return over holding period.
known_events: 2001 (post dot-com)
2022 (inflation squeeze)
implementation_notes: Real disposable income can turn negative due to inflation surges (2022) OR income declines (2008). Both trigger trade-down behavior. COVID stimulus checks created a massive YoY distortion in 2020-2021 — may want to flag that event as anomalous. XLP inception ~1998.

name: FRED Advance Retail Sales 3 Consecutive Positive MoM → Long XRT 60d
rule: Load FRED RSAFS (monthly advance retail sales, seasonally adjusted). Compute MoM % change. Entry: when MoM is positive for 3 consecutive months. Long XRT (SPDR S&P Retail ETF) for 60 trading days. Exit after 60 days. Benchmark: SPY over same period.
entry_conditions: FRED RSAFS MoM change positive (>0%) for 3 consecutive months
Signal evaluated monthly on Census advance retail sales release (mid-month, ~2 week lag)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED RSAFS (monthly advance retail sales, seasonally adjusted)

backtest_approach: Load FRED RSAFS monthly back to 1992. Compute MoM pct change. Identify all 3-month positive streak completions. On the next trading day after the 3rd month's data release, long XRT for 60 trading days. Compute return vs SPY. XRT inception ~2006; use only events from 2006 onward. Count events, win rate, avg return, Sharpe.
known_events: Multiple per cycle — consumer momentum streaks happen regularly
implementation_notes: XRT inception June 2006. For pre-2006 events, could substitute XLY as proxy but XRT is better (broader retail coverage, equal-weighted). RSAFS is seasonally adjusted — no need for seasonal detrending. The 3-month streak filter avoids whipsaws from single-month noise.

name: FRED Mortgage Apps Surge >20% from 13-Week Low → Long LEN+DHI+NVR (1mo lag)
rule: Use a mortgage application proxy: FRED MORTGAGE30US (30yr mortgage rate) declining >50bps from 13-week high as a proxy for application surge (lower rates = more applications). If FRED has a direct MBA purchase index (MORTAPPW or similar), use that instead. Entry: 21 trading days (1 month) after the rate decline threshold is crossed. Long equal-weight LEN+DHI+NVR for 126 trading days. Exit after 126 days. Benchmark: SPY.
entry_conditions: FRED MORTGAGE30US declines >50bps from trailing 13-week high (proxy for mortgage application surge)
OR if FRED MORTAPPW available: purchase index rises >20% from trailing 13-week low
Entry delayed by 21 trading days (1-month lag for applications-to-closings pipeline)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED MORTGAGE30US (weekly 30yr mortgage rate) or FRED MORTAPPW (MBA purchase applications if available)

backtest_approach: Load FRED MORTGAGE30US weekly back to 1971. Compute 13-week trailing high. Entry signal when current rate is >50bps below that high, with 21 trading day delay. Long equal-weight LEN+DHI+NVR for 126 days. Compute basket return vs SPY. LEN/DHI have data back to ~2000; NVR to ~1993. If MORTAPPW not on FRED, the mortgage rate decline proxy captures the same dynamic (rate drops drive application surges with 0.85+ correlation).
known_events: 2003 (refi boom)
2012 (post-GFC recovery)
2019 (mid-cycle cut)
2020 (COVID rate cuts)
2024 (rate peak rollover)
implementation_notes: The 1-month entry delay is critical — it allows the applications-to-closings pipeline to build before entering. MORTAPPW may not be on FRED (MBA data is semi-proprietary). The mortgage rate proxy is a reasonable substitute with strong historical correlation to purchase applications. NVR has limited float and can be illiquid — consider substituting PHM or TOL if NVR data is spotty.

name: Tanker Stock 2-Week Momentum Spike >15% → Long FRO+STNG+INSW 20d
rule: Since BDTI is not directly available on FRED or yfinance, proxy tanker rate spikes via tanker equity momentum: when equal-weight FRO+STNG+INSW basket 10-day return exceeds +15%, the tanker rate spike is already visible in equity prices but continuation is likely. Long equal-weight FRO+STNG+INSW for 20 additional trading days from the signal. Benchmark vs SPY. Alternative: use crude oil futures curve backwardation as tanker demand proxy (FRED DCOILWTICO spot vs CL=F 6mo).
entry_conditions: Equal-weight FRO+STNG+INSW basket 10-day return exceeds +15%
Signals tanker rate spike underway with momentum continuation expected

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: none (BDTI not available on FRED; using equity momentum proxy)

backtest_approach: Load yfinance FRO, STNG, INSW daily back to max available. Compute equal-weight basket. Identify all 10-day windows where basket return > +15%. Long basket for next 20 trading days. Compute per-event returns and aggregate metrics vs SPY. FRO data from ~2001, STNG from ~2014, INSW from ~2014. Use FRO-only for earlier period.
known_events: 2008 (VLCC rate spike)
2020 (contango storage play)
2022 (Russia rerouting)
2024 (Red Sea disruption)
implementation_notes: BDTI is not on FRED or yfinance. Equity momentum proxy is imperfect but captures the same dynamic. STNG and INSW are product tankers (dirty = crude, clean = products); FRO is VLCC crude. Basket captures both segments. Could also test using DCOILWTICO 1-month return > 10% as entry proxy.

name: FRED DRTSCLCC Crosses Zero from Negative → Long XLY 63d
rule: Load FRED DRTSCLCC (net % of banks tightening standards for credit card loans, quarterly SLOOS). Entry: when DRTSCLCC crosses from negative (tightening) to zero or positive (easing). Long XLY for 63 trading days (1 quarter). Benchmark vs SPY. The zero-crossing signals credit availability inflection for consumers.
entry_conditions: FRED DRTSCLCC crosses from negative territory to zero or positive
Signal evaluated quarterly on SLOOS release (typically late Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED DRTSCLCC (SLOOS consumer installment/credit card willingness, quarterly)

backtest_approach: Load FRED DRTSCLCC quarterly back to 1996 (series start). Identify all negative-to-zero/positive crossings. Long XLY for 63 trading days from the first trading day after SLOOS release. Compute XLY excess return vs SPY per event and aggregate. XLY inception 1998; use events from 1998 onward.
known_events: 2003 (post dot-com easing)
2010 (post GFC easing)
2014 (mid-cycle easing)
2021 (post COVID easing)
implementation_notes: DRTSCLCC is quarterly with limited observations. Verify FRED series name -- may also be SUBLPDRCSC or DRTSCLCC. Check for correct series ID. Small sample size is the main risk for this backtest.

name: Corn Futures Jul 15 Seasonal Entry When Crop Conditions Suggest Yield Risk
rule: Without direct access to USDA Crop Progress data in backtester, use a seasonal proxy: long ZC=F from Jul 15 to Aug 15 each year (peak pollination stress window). Compare returns in years where corn conditions were poor (known events) vs all years. If ZC=F not available or illiquid on yfinance, use CORN ETF as alternative. Benchmark vs SPY.
entry_conditions: July 15 each year (peak corn pollination risk window)
Ideally conditioned on USDA crop progress showing silking delay, but seasonal entry as fallback

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED PCORN (corn price proxy for validation) or none

backtest_approach: Load yfinance ZC=F daily. Compute Jul 15 to Aug 15 return each year. Compare all-year average vs known stressed years (2012 drought, 2019 delayed planting, 2021 drought). If ZC=F has gaps, use CORN ETF (inception 2010). Small sample for stressed years -- present as event study rather than systematic strategy.
known_events: 2012 (severe drought)
2019 (record delayed planting)
2021 (Western drought)
implementation_notes: ZC=F on yfinance may have liquidity gaps or contract roll issues. CORN ETF is a more reliable proxy (uses front-month futures). The unconditioned seasonal trade (always long Jul 15-Aug 15) captures the weather risk premium but has weaker Sharpe than conditioned version. USDA Crop Progress data is not on FRED.

name: FRED JTSJOL Declines >15% from Peak → Long RHI+MAN 126d
rule: Load FRED JTSJOL (job openings level, monthly JOLTS). Compute rolling 12-month peak. Entry: when JTSJOL declines >15% from its trailing 12-month peak. Long equal-weight RHI+MAN for 126 trading days. Thesis: labor market cooling drives companies back to temp staffing agencies. Benchmark vs SPY.
entry_conditions: FRED JTSJOL (job openings) declines >15% from trailing 12-month peak
Signal evaluated monthly on JOLTS release (first week of month, 2-month lag)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED JTSJOL (JOLTS job openings level, monthly)

backtest_approach: Load FRED JTSJOL monthly back to 2000 (series start). Compute trailing 12-month peak. Identify all months where level is >15% below peak. Long equal-weight RHI+MAN for 126 days from the first qualifying month. Avoid overlapping trades. RHI data from ~2000, MAN from ~1990. Compute basket vs SPY.
known_events: 2001 (dot-com bust)
2020 (COVID)
2023-24 (post-pandemic normalization)
implementation_notes: JOLTS data starts Dec 2000. RHI and MAN are the two largest publicly traded staffing firms. HAYS is London-listed and may not be on yfinance US. The thesis is counterintuitive -- openings decline = GOOD for staffing firms -- so careful framing needed. Distinct from PL081 (quits rate recovery) in that this uses openings decline as the trigger.

name: FRED DGORDER 3mo MoM Acceleration → Long AMAT+LRCX+KLAC 63d
rule: Load FRED DGORDER (monthly durable goods new orders). Compute MoM % change. Entry: when MoM is positive AND increasing for 3 consecutive months (accelerating). Long equal-weight AMAT+LRCX+KLAC for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and SOXX. The durable goods acceleration signals capex cycle turn that benefits semi equipment disproportionately.
entry_conditions: FRED DGORDER MoM positive and increasing for 3 consecutive months
Signal evaluated monthly on Census durable goods release (~26th of month)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, SOXX, SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED DGORDER (monthly durable goods new orders)

backtest_approach: Load FRED DGORDER monthly back to 1992. Compute MoM changes. Identify 3-month acceleration streaks (each MoM > prior MoM AND positive). Long equal-weight AMAT+LRCX+KLAC for 63 days. All three have data back to ~1995. Compute basket excess return vs SPY and vs SOXX (semi benchmark).
known_events: 2003 (post dot-com capex recovery)
2010 (post GFC)
2017 (memory capex boom)
2021 (post COVID)
implementation_notes: DGORDER includes volatile Boeing orders -- consider also testing with NEWORDER (ex-transport) for cleaner signal. The 3-month acceleration filter is strict and may fire infrequently. SOXX is the relevant sector benchmark, not SPY.

name: FRED NGTRSTUS Aug 1 Storage < 5yr Avg by >10% → Long NG=F Aug-Nov
rule: Load FRED NGTRSTUS (weekly natural gas storage). On August 1 each year, compute current storage level vs 5-year average for that week. If current storage is >10% below the 5-year average: long NG=F from Aug 1 through Nov 30. This targets the late-injection concern before winter. Benchmark vs SPY. Distinct from PL058 (Oct deficit, general winter) -- this is specifically summer injection shortfall, earlier entry.
entry_conditions: August 1 each year
FRED NGTRSTUS current level >10% below 5-year average for that calendar week
backtest_approach: Load FRED NGTRSTUS weekly. For each year, compute 5-year trailing average for the week containing Aug 1. If actual < 0.9 * avg: long NG=F Aug 1 to Nov 30. Compute annual trade P&L. NG=F may have contract roll issues on yfinance; UNG ETF (inception 2007) as backup. Test 2007-2025.
known_events: 2012 (hot summer, low storage)
2014 (polar vortex recovery)
2018 (production lag)
2022 (record exports to EU)
implementation_notes: NGTRSTUS on FRED may only have total, not the 5-year average. Compute the 5-year average manually from historical data. NG=F on yfinance rolls continuously which introduces roll return distortion. UNG ETF is a reasonable proxy but decays due to contango. For accuracy, note that storage levels are reported with a 1-week lag.

name: FRED PERMIT1 YoY Improving for 3 Consecutive Months → Long SHW+VMC+MLM+BLDR 63d
rule: Load FRED PERMIT1 (single-family building permits, monthly SAAR). Compute YoY % change. Entry: when the YoY rate improves (becomes less negative or more positive) for 3 consecutive months. Long equal-weight SHW+VMC+MLM+BLDR for 63 trading days. The YoY improvement signals the housing trough even if absolute YoY is still negative. Benchmark vs SPY and XHB.
entry_conditions: FRED PERMIT1 YoY rate of change improves (delta of YoY is positive) for 3 consecutive months
Signal evaluated monthly on Census permits release (mid-month, 1-month lag)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance SHW, VMC, MLM, BLDR, XHB, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED PERMIT1 monthly back to 1959. Compute YoY, then compute month-over-month change in YoY (second derivative). Entry when 3 consecutive months of positive second derivative. Long basket for 63 days. SHW from ~1980, VMC from ~1990, MLM from ~1994, BLDR from ~2016 (IPO). For early period use available tickers only. Compare basket vs XHB and SPY.
known_events: 2009 (post GFC trough)
2011 (housing recovery)
2019 (rate cut relief)
2023 (post-rate-shock recovery)
implementation_notes: BLDR IPO ~2016 -- use only from then onward. The second-derivative signal (rate of change of rate of change) is more sensitive than absolute levels and fires earlier in the cycle. SHW is a mega-cap paint company -- lower beta than VMC/MLM but more liquid. XHB is the sector benchmark for relative performance comparison.

name: FRED DTWEXBGS 6-Month Return < -5% → Long EEM 63d
rule: Load FRED DTWEXBGS (broad trade-weighted dollar index, daily). Compute rolling 6-month (126 trading day) return. Entry: when 6-month return crosses below -5%. Long EEM for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Distinct from PL046 (DXY spike → EM reversal) -- that was a mean-reversion trade after sharp dollar STRENGTH; this is momentum following dollar WEAKNESS.
entry_conditions: FRED DTWEXBGS 6-month rolling return crosses below -5% (dollar weakening >5% over 6mo)
Signal evaluated daily

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED DTWEXBGS (broad trade-weighted dollar index, daily)

backtest_approach: Load FRED DTWEXBGS daily back to 2006. Compute 126-day rolling return. Identify all dates where it crosses below -5%. Long EEM for 63 days from each signal (avoid overlapping trades -- skip signals within 63 days of prior entry). EEM data from 2003. Compute excess return vs SPY per trade and aggregate. Also test with VWO as alternative.
known_events: 2006-2007 (pre-GFC dollar weakness)
2017 (synchronized global growth)
2020 (post-COVID dollar decline)
2023 (dollar peak rollover)
implementation_notes: DTWEXBGS is daily but has some gaps (holidays/weekends). Use business day alignment with yfinance data. The signal may cluster (multiple daily crossings) -- use first crossing only and skip 63 days. Distinct from PL046 which was short-term DXY spike reversal; this is medium-term dollar weakness momentum.

name: FRED TLRESCONS 3 Consecutive Positive MoM → Long WSO+WMS 63d
rule: Load FRED TLRESCONS (total residential construction spending, monthly). Compute MoM % change. Entry: when MoM is positive for 3 consecutive months after a period of decline (at least 2 prior negative MoM months). Long equal-weight WSO+WMS for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and XHB. Plumbing/HVAC distributors benefit first from construction restart as they supply materials before framing/finishing trades.
entry_conditions: FRED TLRESCONS MoM positive for 3 consecutive months
Must follow at least 2 months of negative MoM (confirms inflection, not continuation)
Signal evaluated monthly on Census C30 release (1st business day, 2-month lag)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED TLRESCONS (total residential construction spending, monthly)

backtest_approach: Load FRED TLRESCONS monthly back to 2002. Compute MoM changes. Identify sequences of 2+ negative MoM followed by 3+ positive MoM (inflection). Long WSO+WMS for 63 days from the 3rd positive month. WSO (Watsco, HVAC distributor) data from ~1990. WMS (Advanced Drainage Systems) data from ~2014 (IPO). For pre-2014, WSO-only basket. Compute vs SPY.
known_events: 2009 (post GFC trough)
2012 (housing recovery)
2020 (post COVID)
2023 (post rate shock)
implementation_notes: WMS (Advanced Drainage Systems) IPO was 2014. SWK (Stanley Black & Decker) from original thesis could substitute for broader tool exposure but is less pure-play plumbing/HVAC. WSO is the purest HVAC distribution play in US equities. Construction spending data has a 2-month lag. Verify FRED series name is TLRESCONS (may also be filed under PRRESCONS for private only).

name: FRED WTREGEN Treasury General Account Rebuild → Long Short-Duration Bonds
rule: Load FRED WTREGEN (Treasury General Account, weekly). Compute 4-week change. Entry: when TGA increases by >$100B in 4 weeks (debt ceiling rebuild). Wait 4 weeks for funding stress to peak, then long SHV for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs BIL.
backtest_approach: Load FRED WTREGEN weekly. Identify all 4-week windows where TGA increases >$100B. After a 4-week wait, long SHV for 42 days. Compute excess return vs BIL. Known events: 2019 (post-ceiling), 2021 (post-ceiling), 2023 (post-ceiling). Small sample.

name: FRED TOTCI Total Commercial & Industrial Loans YoY Trough → Long Regional Banks
rule: Load FRED TOTCI or BUSLOANS (C&I loans, weekly). Compute YoY change. Entry: when YoY reaches trough and turns upward for 4 consecutive weeks. Long KRE for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.
backtest_approach: Load FRED BUSLOANS weekly. Compute 52-week YoY. Identify troughs where YoY turns positive for 4 consecutive weeks after declining. Long KRE for 63 days. KRE inception 2006. Compute vs SPY.

name: FRED FRGSHPUSM649NCIS Real Government Spending Acceleration → Long Defense Prime
rule: Load FRED FGCE (federal government consumption expenditures, quarterly). Entry: when QoQ growth accelerates for 2 consecutive quarters. Long equal-weight LMT+RTX+NOC+GD for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and ITA.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance LMT, RTX, NOC, GD, ITA, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED FGCE quarterly. Compute QoQ growth. Entry when 2 consecutive quarters of accelerating growth. Long defense basket for 63 days. All tickers have long history (LMT from ~1995, NOC from ~1994). Compute basket vs SPY and ITA.

name: NOAA Palmer Drought Severity Index Extreme → Long Water Utilities
rule: Use FRED CUSR0000SEHG (water/sewer CPI) as drought-impact proxy since PDSI not on FRED. When water CPI YoY exceeds +5% for 3 months (utility surcharges kicking in), long AWK+WTR for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs XLU and SPY.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance AWK, WTR, SJW, XLU, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED CUSR0000SEHG01 monthly. Compute YoY. Entry when YoY > +5% for 3 months. Long AWK+WTR for 63 days. AWK from ~2008 IPO. Alternative: known drought years (2012, 2015, 2021, 2022) as event study.

name: USDA World Ag Supply & Demand (WASDE) Soybean Ending Stocks Surprise → Long Soyb
rule: Without WASDE consensus estimates in backtester, use seasonal WASDE release dates. Long ZS=F for 5 trading days around each WASDE release (published ~10th of each month). Test whether WASDE months show systematically different soybean returns. Benchmark vs SPY.
backtest_approach: Load yfinance ZS=F daily. Identify WASDE release dates (typically 10th-12th of each month). Compute 5-day return starting WASDE release date. Compare to non-WASDE-week 5-day returns. If ZS=F unavailable, use SOYB ETF (inception 2011).

name: FRED PCEPILFE Core PCE 3mo Annualized Below 2% → Long Growth/Tech (QQQ)
rule: Load FRED PCEPILFE (core PCE, monthly). Compute 3-month annualized rate. Entry: first time 3mo annualized drops below 2.0% after being above 3.0% for 6+ months. Long QQQ for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and VTV.
backtest_approach: Load FRED PCEPILFE monthly. Compute 3-month annualized change. Identify events where it crosses below 2.0% after 6+ months above 3.0%. Long QQQ for 42 days. QQQ inception 1999. Historical events rare -- 2003, 2012, 2023 possible. Small sample event study.

name: FRED RAILFRGCARLOADSD11 Railroad Carloads Chemical Group Surge → Long Specialty
rule: Load FRED RAILFRTCARLOADSD11 (railroad carloads total, monthly). Compute YoY. Entry: when YoY crosses above +5% after 3+ months negative. Long equal-weight DD+LYB+EMN for 63 trading days as chemical demand proxy. Benchmark vs SPY and XLB.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance DD, LYB, EMN, XLB, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED RAILFRGCARLOADSD11 monthly. Compute YoY. Entry on positive crossing above +5% after negative period. Long DD+LYB+EMN for 63 days. DD data from ~2019 (DowDuPont split), LYB from ~2010 (IPO), EMN from ~1994. Use available tickers per period.

name: FRED ISRATIO Total Business Inventory/Sales Ratio Decline → Long Small Cap Value
rule: Load FRED ISRATIO (total business inventory/sales ratio, monthly). Entry: when ISRATIO declines for 3 consecutive months from a level above 1.40. Long IWN for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs IWF and SPY.
backtest_approach: Load FRED ISRATIO monthly. Identify all 3-month declining streaks starting from >1.40. Long IWN for 63 days. IWN inception 2000. Compute excess return vs IWF (growth) and SPY.

name: FRED CPIAUCSL CPI Monthly Print Below Consensus → Long TLT Same-Day
rule: Load FRED CPIAUCSL (CPI, monthly). Compute MoM % change. Since we cannot access consensus estimates in backtester, proxy the surprise as: MoM < prior month MoM by >0.1pp (decelerating inflation). Long TLT for 5 trading days on CPI release date when MoM decelerates. Benchmark vs SPY.
backtest_approach: Load FRED CPIAUCSL monthly. Compute MoM. For each month where MoM decelerates by >0.1pp vs prior month, long TLT for 5 trading days starting CPI release date (typically ~13th of month). Compute per-event and aggregate returns.

name: FRED BOGZ1FL073164003Q Household Net Worth to GDP Trough → Long SPY Multi-Month
rule: Load FRED TNWBSHNO (household net worth, quarterly) and FRED GDP (quarterly). Compute ratio. Entry: when ratio shows 2 consecutive quarterly increases after a decline. Long SPY for 126 trading days. Benchmark: SPY buy-and-hold.

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED TNWBSHNO (household net worth quarterly), FRED GDP

backtest_approach: Load FRED TNWBSHNO and GDP quarterly. Compute ratio. Identify 2-quarter recovery signals after decline. Long SPY for 126 days. Z.1 data has long history but is quarterly with ~3 month lag. Known events: 2003, 2009, 2020.

name: FRED REVOLSL Revolving Consumer Credit Deceleration → Long XLP Defensive Rotatio
rule: Load FRED REVOLSL (revolving consumer credit, monthly). Compute YoY. Entry: when YoY decelerates by >3pp in 2 months (e.g., from +8% to +5% in 2 months). Long XLP for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs XLY and SPY.
backtest_approach: Load FRED REVOLSL monthly. Compute YoY. Compute 2-month change in YoY rate. Entry when 2-month change < -3pp. Long XLP for 42 days. Compute XLP excess return vs XLY (rotation signal) and vs SPY.

name: FRED PPIACO Producer Price Index Crude Materials Surge → Long Commodity Miners (
rule: Load FRED PPICRM or PPIACO (PPI crude materials/all commodities, monthly). Compute MoM. Entry: when MoM exceeds +5%. Long XME for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.
backtest_approach: Load FRED PPICRM monthly back to 1926 (limited use pre-2006 for XME). Identify all months with MoM > +5%. Long XME for 42 days. XME inception 2006. Compute vs SPY.

name: FRED IPMAN Manufacturing IP 3mo Rate of Change Inflection → Long Factory Automat
rule: Load FRED IPMAN (manufacturing IP, monthly). Compute 3-month rate of change (3mo annualized). Entry: when 3mo RoC crosses above 0 after 3+ months below 0. Long equal-weight ROK+AME+EMR for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and XLI.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance ROK, AME, EMR, XLI, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED IPMAN monthly. Compute 3-month annualized rate of change. Identify zero-crossings from below after 3+ months negative. Long ROK+AME+EMR for 63 days. All have data back to ~1995+. Compute basket vs XLI and SPY.

name: FRED DSPIC96 Real Disposable Personal Income 3mo Acceleration → Long Luxury/Prem
rule: Load FRED DSPIC96 (real DPI, monthly). Compute MoM. Entry: when 2nd derivative (change in MoM) is positive for 3 consecutive months. Long equal-weight RL+TPR+RH for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs XRT and SPY.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance RL, TPR, RH, XRT, SPY

backtest_approach: Load FRED DSPIC96 monthly. Compute MoM changes, then compute month-over-month acceleration (2nd derivative). Entry when 3 consecutive months of positive acceleration. Long RL+TPR+RH for 63 days. RL from ~1997, TPR from ~2017 (Tapestry rebrand), RH from ~2012. Use available tickers.

name: FRED AWHMAN Average Weekly Hours Manufacturing Inflection → Long Temp Staffing A
rule: Load FRED AWHMAN (avg weekly hours mfg, monthly). Entry: when AWHMAN rises for 3 consecutive months after a period of decline (at least 2 prior declining months). Long equal-weight RHI+ASGN for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED AWHMAN (monthly avg weekly hours mfg)

backtest_approach: Load FRED AWHMAN monthly. Identify 3-month rising streaks following 2+ month declines. Long RHI+ASGN for 63 days. RHI from ~2000, ASGN from ~2012 (formerly On Assignment). Compute vs SPY.

name: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Gasoline Days of Supply <22 → Long Refiner Margins (
rule: Load FRED GASDESW (gasoline days of supply estimate, weekly) if available. When days of supply drops below 22 between May-September: long equal-weight VLO+MPC+PSX for 21 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and XLE.

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance VLO, MPC, PSX, XLE, SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED GASDESW (gasoline days of supply) or EIA weekly data

backtest_approach: Load FRED GASDESW weekly if available (may need to verify series ID). Identify weeks May-Sep where days of supply < 22. Long VLO+MPC+PSX for 21 days. All refiners have data from ~2000+. MPC from ~2011 (Marathon spinoff), PSX from ~2012 (Phillips 66 spinoff). Compute vs XLE and SPY.

name: FRED DCOILWTICO WTI Crude Below $50 for 3 Months → Long E&P Recovery (XOP) Contr
rule: Load FRED DCOILWTICO (WTI crude spot, daily). Compute monthly average. Entry: when monthly avg stays below $50 for 3 consecutive months AND then bounces >15% from 3-month low. Long XOP for 126 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and XLE.
backtest_approach: Load FRED DCOILWTICO daily. Compute monthly averages. Identify 3-month sub-$50 periods followed by >15% bounce from trough. Long XOP for 126 days. XOP inception 2006. Known events: 2015-16, 2020. Small sample.

name: FRED MSPUS Median Home Sale Price YoY Deceleration → Long Mortgage REITs (NLY, A
rule: Load FRED MSPUS (median existing home sale price, quarterly). Compute YoY. Entry: when YoY decelerates from >10% to below 3% over 2 quarters. Long equal-weight NLY+AGNC for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY and REM.
backtest_approach: Load FRED MSPUS quarterly. Compute YoY. Identify deceleration events from >10% to <3% in 2Q. Long NLY+AGNC for 63 days. NLY from ~1997, AGNC from ~2008. Compute vs SPY and REM.

name: FRED EXPGS Real Exports of Goods & Services Recovery → Long Industrial Exporters

name: FEMA Major Disaster Declaration in Populous Area -> Long Building Materials (OC+BLDR+UFPI)
rule: Fetch FEMA disaster declarations via OpenFEMA API (https://www.fema.gov/api/open/v2/DisasterDeclarationsSummaries). Filter for declarationType=DR (major disaster) in states with large populations (FL, TX, CA, LA, NC, SC, NY, NJ). On each qualifying declaration date, go long OC+BLDR+UFPI equal-weight for 60 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Use requests or urllib to fetch JSON from FEMA API. Known major events: 2005-08-29 (Katrina), 2012-10-30 (Sandy), 2017-08-25 (Harvey), 2017-09-20 (Maria/Irma), 2018-10-10 (Michael), 2020-08-27 (Laura), 2021-08-29 (Ida), 2022-09-28 (Ian).
entry_conditions: FEMA declares a major disaster (DR type) in a high-population state (FL, TX, CA, LA, NC, SC, NY, NJ)
Go long on the trading day following the declaration date
exit_conditions: Hold for 60 trading days from entry
Equal-weight basket of OC + BLDR + UFPI

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FEMA OpenFEMA API v2 DisasterDeclarationsSummaries (free, no key needed)

backtest_approach: Event study: fetch all DR-type disaster declarations from FEMA API for major states. For each event, enter long OC+BLDR+UFPI equal-weight on next trading day, hold 60 days. Compute per-event returns and aggregate metrics vs SPY. If FEMA API is unavailable, use hardcoded known disaster dates as fallback.
implementation_notes: BECN (Beacon Roofing Supply) is delisted on yfinance; replaced with UFPI (UFP Industries). FEMA API returns JSON at https://www.fema.gov/api/open/v2/DisasterDeclarationsSummaries with filters. Filter: declarationType eq DR, state in (FL,TX,CA,LA,NC,SC,NY,NJ). Deduplicate by keeping only the first declaration per state per disaster incident. Use urllib.request for API calls.

backtest_result.verdict: Duplicate - see other PL249 entry. Sharpe 0.20, CAGR -1.2%

name: FEMA Major Disaster Declarations -> Rebuilding Materials Demand
rule: Using yfinance and FEMA OpenFEMA data: When FEMA issues a Major Disaster Declaration (type DR) for a state with population > 5M, go long an equal-weight basket of building materials stocks (OC, BLDR, BLD) on the declaration date. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Filter: only declarations that include 'Hurricane', 'Severe Storm', 'Tornado', or 'Flood' as incident type (categories that drive physical rebuilding). Require at least 30 days between signals to avoid overlap.
entry_conditions: FEMA Major Disaster Declaration (type DR) issued for state with population > 5M
Incident type is one of: Hurricane, Severe Storm(s), Tornado, Flood
At least 30 calendar days since last signal entry

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FEMA OpenFEMA DisasterDeclarations API (bulk CSV at https://www.fema.gov/api/open/v2/DisasterDeclarations.csv) - fields: declarationDate, incidentType, state, declarationType

backtest_approach: Event study: For each qualifying FEMA Major Disaster Declaration (2000-2025), compute cumulative return of equal-weight OC+BLDR+BLD basket over 40 trading days post-declaration vs SPY over same window. Calculate average excess return, win rate, and t-statistic across all events. Use FEMA bulk CSV download (free, no API key needed) to get historical declaration dates.

name: BLS QCEW County Employment Surge -> Sunbelt Industrial REITs
rule: Using yfinance and BLS QCEW data: When BLS QCEW quarterly release shows aggregate warehouse/logistics employment (NAICS 493 Warehousing and Storage) grew > 5% YoY nationally, go long an equal-weight basket of Sunbelt industrial REITs (STAG, PLD, REXR) on the QCEW release date. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. QCEW data releases quarterly with ~6 month lag (Q1 data released ~September). Use national aggregate to avoid sparse county-level data issues.
entry_conditions: BLS QCEW quarterly release shows NAICS 493 (Warehousing & Storage) national employment grew > 5% YoY
Use national-level aggregate (area_fips=US000, own_code=5 private, industry_code=493)
Signal fires on QCEW release date (approximately 6 months after quarter end)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: BLS QCEW API: https://data.bls.gov/cew/data/api/{year}/{qtr}/area/US000.csv - filter industry_code=493, own_code=5. Release schedule at https://www.bls.gov/cew/schedule.htm

backtest_approach: Event study: For each QCEW quarterly release (2010-2025) where NAICS 493 national employment grew > 5% YoY, compute cumulative return of equal-weight STAG+PLD+REXR basket over 30 trading days post-release vs SPY. QCEW release dates: Q1 data ~Sept, Q2 data ~Dec, Q3 data ~March, Q4 data ~June. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. If fewer than 8 signal events, relax threshold to > 3% YoY growth.
implementation_notes: BLS QCEW API is free and requires no key. QCEW has ~6 month publication lag which is the edge (market may not process this data promptly). STAG IPO was 2011, REXR IPO was 2013 - backtest start limited by earliest REIT availability. NAICS 493 is specifically warehousing/storage; broader logistics includes 484 (trucking) and 492 (couriers) which could be added if 493 alone yields too few signals. bt_feasibility is 3 due to potentially sparse signal count at >5% threshold.

name: SBA 504 Loan Approval Surge -> Community Bank Net Interest Income
rule: Using yfinance and SBA loan data: When SBA monthly 504 loan approval volume (dollar amount) exceeds its trailing 12-month average by > 20%, go long KRE (SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF) on the first trading day after the SBA monthly data release. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. SBA publishes monthly approval data on its website with ~1 month lag. Use dollar volume rather than count to account for loan size trends.
entry_conditions: SBA monthly 504 loan approval dollar volume exceeds trailing 12-month average by > 20%
Enter on first trading day after SBA monthly data release (~1 month lag)
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals to avoid overlap

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: SBA 504/7(a) loan approval data from SBA.gov open data (FOIA dataset, downloadable CSV). Historical data available back to 2006. Monthly frequency with ~30 day publication lag.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2006-2025) where SBA 504 dollar volume exceeded trailing 12-month average by >20%, compute KRE return over 30 trading days post-release vs SPY. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. SBA release dates can be approximated as end of following month (e.g., January data released ~end of February). If signal is too frequent, raise threshold to >30%.
implementation_notes: KRE ETF inception was June 2006, so backtest starts mid-2006. SBA data may need to be scraped from SBA.gov FOIA datasets or downloaded as bulk CSV. The 504 program is specifically for fixed-asset lending (real estate, equipment), making it a cleaner signal for economic activity than 7(a) which includes working capital. bt_feasibility is 3 due to potential difficulty in automating SBA data retrieval and approximating exact release dates.

name: USDA Crop Progress Soybean Blooming Poor Condition -> Soybean Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA Crop Progress data: When USDA weekly Crop Progress report shows soybean good+excellent condition rating drops below 55% during July 1 - August 15 (R1-R3 blooming/pod-setting stages), go long SOYB (Teucrium Soybean Fund ETF) on the Monday following the report. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Signal fires only once per growing season (first week condition drops below threshold). Use USDA NASS QuickStats API for historical crop progress data.
entry_conditions: USDA weekly Crop Progress report shows soybean good+excellent condition < 55%
Report date falls between July 1 and August 15 (blooming/pod-setting stage)
Signal fires only on the first week threshold is breached each growing season
Enter on Monday following report release (reports released Monday afternoon)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: SOYB, DBA, ZS=F (soybean futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA NASS Crop Progress via QuickStats API (free, API key required but free registration). Weekly data available back to 1986 for soybeans. Parameters: source_desc=SURVEY, commodity_desc=SOYBEANS, statisticcat_desc=CONDITION, unit_desc=PCT GOOD + PCT EXCELLENT.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each growing season (2012-2025, SOYB inception), identify weeks in July-August where soybean good+excellent first dropped below 55%. Compute SOYB return over 20 trading days post-signal vs SPY. Also test with ZS=F (soybean futures) for longer history (1986-2025). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Expected ~6-10 events where condition dropped below 55% during critical reproductive stages.
known_events: 2012 (severe drought, conditions crashed to ~29% G+E)
2019 (late planting, wet conditions degraded quality)
2023 (flash drought in August)
implementation_notes: SOYB ETF inception was September 2011. For longer history, use ZS=F (CBOT soybean futures continuous contract) on yfinance which has data back further. DBA included as broader agriculture ETF alternative. USDA NASS QuickStats API requires free registration for API key. The 55% threshold is based on historical analysis showing that condition below this level during R1-R3 stages correlates with >5% yield reduction. Reports are released Monday afternoon; entry is Tuesday open to avoid stale pricing.

name: NFIP Claims Surge -> Flood Mitigation Infrastructure Spending
rule: Using yfinance and FEMA OpenFEMA NFIP claims data: When monthly NFIP paid claims exceed $500M in a single month (indicating a major flood event), go long an equal-weight basket of water infrastructure stocks (AWK, ACM, MWA) on the first trading day of the month following the claims surge. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The $500M threshold captures only major events (roughly top-decile months). Use FEMA OpenFEMA NFIP claims bulk CSV which has payment dates and amounts going back to 1970s.
entry_conditions: FEMA NFIP monthly paid claims total exceeds $500M
Enter on first trading day of the following month
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals to avoid overlap from multi-month events

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FEMA OpenFEMA NFIP Claims dataset (bulk CSV: https://www.fema.gov/api/open/v2/FimaNfipClaims.csv). Fields: dateOfLoss, amountPaidOnBuildingClaim, amountPaidOnContentsClaim, totalAmountPaid. Aggregate monthly totalAmountPaid to get monthly claims volume.

backtest_approach: Event study: Aggregate NFIP claims by month. For months exceeding $500M in paid claims (2008-2025, constrained by AWK IPO 2008), compute equal-weight AWK+ACM+MWA basket return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Key events: Hurricane Sandy (2012), Harvey (2017), Florence (2018), Ida (2021), Ian (2022). If fewer than 6 signal events at $500M, lower threshold to $300M.
known_events: 2012-10 Hurricane Sandy NFIP claims spike
2016-08 Louisiana floods major NFIP payouts
2017-08 Hurricane Harvey record NFIP claims
2017-09 Hurricane Irma NFIP surge
2018-09 Hurricane Florence claims
2021-08 Hurricane Ida claims
2022-09 Hurricane Ian claims
implementation_notes: AWK IPO was 2008, ACM IPO was 2007, MWA IPO was 2006. Backtest limited to 2008+. The FEMA NFIP dataset is very large (millions of claims); pre-aggregate by month before analysis. MWA (Mueller Water Products) focuses on water infrastructure products. ACM (AECOM) is engineering/construction. AWK (American Water Works) is the largest US water utility. The causal chain has a lag: mitigation spending takes 2-4 quarters to materialize, but stock prices may anticipate. bt_feasibility is 3 due to potentially low signal count.

name: BLS PPI Construction Inputs Deflation -> Homebuilder Margin Expansion
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When FRED series WPUIP2300001 (PPI: Inputs to Construction Industries) shows 3-month annualized rate of change below -5%, go long an equal-weight basket of homebuilders (DHI, LEN, TOL) on the PPI release date. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. PPI is released monthly by BLS, typically around the 13th-15th of the month. Calculate 3-month annualized change as: ((current / 3-months-ago)^4 - 1) * 100.
entry_conditions: FRED WPUIP2300001 (PPI Inputs to Construction) 3-month annualized rate of change < -5%
Enter on PPI release date (typically 13th-15th of month)
No minimum gap between signals (can fire consecutive months during deflation)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: DHI, LEN, TOL, ITB (iShares US Home Construction ETF for robustness check), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED series WPUIP2300001 (PPI: Inputs to Construction Industries, Goods). Monthly frequency, not seasonally adjusted. Alternative: WPUIP2300 if 2300001 unavailable. BLS PPI release schedule at https://www.bls.gov/schedule/news_release/ppi.htm

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2000-2025) where WPUIP2300001 3-month annualized change < -5%, compute equal-weight DHI+LEN+TOL return over 30 trading days post-release vs SPY. Also test with ITB ETF (inception 2006) as single-ticker alternative. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Expected events: 2008-2009 (GFC commodity crash), 2015 (commodity deflation), 2019 (trade war), 2022-2023 (lumber price normalization).
known_events: 2008-2009 GFC commodity crash caused sharp construction input deflation
2015-2016 global commodity deflation impacted construction costs
2019 lumber price decline amid trade uncertainties
2022-2023 lumber normalization after pandemic spike
implementation_notes: DHI, LEN, TOL all have data back to 2000+. ITB ETF inception was 2006 for robustness check. The key insight is that homebuilders lock in sales prices 3-9 months before delivery, so falling input costs flow directly to margin on backlog. This is a well-understood mechanism in the industry but the PPI signal timing may offer an edge. FRED data is free but requires API key (free registration). The 3-month annualized rate smooths noise while being responsive enough to capture meaningful deflation episodes.

name: USPTO Patent Grant Acceleration in Oncology CPC -> Biotech M&A Signal
rule: Using yfinance and USPTO PatentsView data: When quarterly USPTO patent grants in CPC class A61P35 (antineoplastics/oncology) exceed trailing 4-quarter average by >30%, go long XBI (SPDR S&P Biotech ETF) on the first trading day of the following quarter. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USPTO patent grant data available via PatentsView API (free, no key needed) with weekly updates. Aggregate to quarterly frequency. Signal fires at most once per quarter.
entry_conditions: Quarterly USPTO patent grants in CPC class A61P35 (oncology) exceed trailing 4-quarter average by >30%
Enter on first trading day of the following quarter
At most one signal per quarter

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USPTO PatentsView API (free): https://api.patentsview.org/patents/query - filter by CPC subclass A61P35. Alternatively, bulk download from https://patentsview.org/download/data-download-tables. Count patent grants per quarter in this CPC class.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each quarter (2006-2025, XBI inception Jan 2006) where oncology patent grants exceeded trailing 4-quarter average by >30%, compute XBI return over 40 trading days post-quarter-start vs SPY. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. The hypothesis is that patent acceleration signals an innovation wave that leads to M&A activity 1-3 quarters later. If fewer than 6 events at 30% threshold, lower to 20%.
implementation_notes: PatentsView API is free and well-documented. CPC A61P35 covers antineoplastic agents specifically. The backtester should query patents by grant date and CPC classification. XBI is equal-weighted so it better captures small-cap M&A premium than IBB (cap-weighted). bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) patent grant data may show secular growth trends requiring detrending, (b) the causal link from patent grants to M&A is indirect, and (c) quarterly frequency gives few data points per year.

name: USDA NASS Citrus Forecast Down-Revision -> FCOJ Futures Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA NASS citrus data: When USDA NASS monthly citrus crop production forecast revises Florida orange production down > 15% from the initial October estimate (first forecast of the season), go long OJ=F (FCOJ futures) on the report release date. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USDA NASS publishes citrus crop forecasts monthly October through July. Compare current month estimate to the initial October estimate for the same crop year.
entry_conditions: USDA NASS Florida orange production forecast revised down > 15% from initial October estimate
Enter on USDA citrus report release date
Signal fires only once per crop year (on first month threshold is breached)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: OJ=F (FCOJ continuous futures contract), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA NASS Citrus Fruits Summary and monthly Crop Production reports. Reports released around 10th-12th of each month.

When cumulative revision exceeds -15%, compute OJ=F return over 20 trading days post-report vs SPY. Expected significant events: 2004-2005 (multiple hurricanes), 2006-2010 (citrus canker), 2013-present (citrus greening disease causing persistent declines). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.

2016-2017 crop year: Hurricane Irma damaged citrus groves
2018-2025: Citrus greening disease causing progressive production decline
2022-2023: Hurricane Ian further reduced production
implementation_notes: OJ=F on yfinance provides FCOJ continuous futures contract. Citrus greening (HLB) has caused a structural decline in Florida production since ~2005, so the 15% revision threshold must be measured within-season (vs initial October estimate), not YoY. USDA QuickStats is free. The NASS citrus report schedule varies; approximate release as 10th-12th of each month. FCOJ is a thin market -- position sizing should be small. Consider also tracking Brazil crop news as it affects global supply.

rule: Using yfinance and FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile data: When FDIC QBP shows aggregate unrealized losses on investment securities narrowing by > 20% QoQ (i.e., losses becoming less negative by at least 20%), go long KRE (SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF) on the first trading day after FDIC QBP publication. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. FDIC QBP published quarterly, typically ~8 weeks after quarter end (e.g., Q1 data released late May/early June).
entry_conditions: FDIC QBP aggregate unrealized losses on investment securities narrow by > 20% QoQ
Enter on first trading day after FDIC QBP publication date
At most one signal per quarter

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile (free): https://www.fdic.gov/analysis/quarterly-banking-profile - Table III-A 'Estimated Insured Deposits and Unrealized Gains (Losses) on Investment Securities'. Also available via FDIC Statistics At A Glance. Quarterly data, published ~8 weeks after quarter end.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each FDIC QBP release (2008-2025, KRE inception 2006) where aggregate unrealized losses narrowed >20% QoQ, compute KRE return over 30 trading days post-release vs SPY. FDIC QBP publication dates are on the FDIC website. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. implementation_notes: This signal became especially relevant post-SVB (March 2023) when bank unrealized losses became a major market focus. Before 2022, unrealized losses were smaller and less market-moving. The FDIC QBP is freely available but must be manually scraped or downloaded as PDF/Excel. KRE inception was June 2006. The 20% improvement threshold should be applied to the absolute value of unrealized losses (e.g., going from -$600B to -$480B = 20% improvement). This is a relatively clean signal with well-defined data and clear mechanism.

name: USDA Feeder Cattle Price Collapse -> Feedlot Margin Expansion -> Packers Long
rule: Using yfinance: When GF=F (feeder cattle futures front month) drops > 15% below its 252-trading-day (1 year) moving average while LE=F (live cattle futures) remains within 5% of its 252-day moving average, go long TSN (Tyson Foods) on the next trading day. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The feeder-to-fed spread widening signals feedlot margin expansion. Use yfinance continuous futures for both feeder and live cattle. Check condition weekly (every Friday close).
entry_conditions: GF=F (feeder cattle futures) closes > 15% below its 252-day simple moving average
LE=F (live cattle futures) closes within +/- 5% of its 252-day simple moving average
Check condition on Friday close each week
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: TSN (Tyson Foods), GF=F (CME feeder cattle futures continuous), LE=F (CME live cattle futures continuous), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: none - all signal data derived from yfinance futures prices

backtest_approach: Event study: For each Friday (2000-2025) where GF=F was >15% below 252-day MA while LE=F was within 5% of its 252-day MA, compute TSN return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Group overlapping signals into single events with 60-day minimum gap. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. This is a pure price-based signal so no external data sources needed. The spread between feeder and fed cattle is the key driver of packer margins.
known_events: 2008-2009 GFC: feeder cattle crashed while fed cattle held up better initially
2015-2016: feeder cattle collapsed from cycle highs while boxed beef stable
2020 Q1-Q2: COVID disrupted feedlot placements causing feeder price collapse
implementation_notes: This is one of the cleanest signals since all data comes from yfinance (no external APIs needed). TSN is the largest US protein company with significant beef segment (~35% of revenue). GF=F and LE=F continuous futures on yfinance may have roll artifacts; the backtester should be aware of potential price discontinuities at contract rolls. The 15% threshold is aggressive; if fewer than 5 events, relax to 12% or 10%. Alternative targets: SAFM (Sanderson Farms, acquired 2022), PPC (Pilgrim's Pride) for chicken exposure during beef margin cycles.

name: Treasury STRIPS 2s10s Inversion Depth -> Utilities Outperformance
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When FRED series SVENY10 minus SVENY02 (Svensson zero-coupon 10yr minus 2yr Treasury yields) drops below -100bp, go long an equal-weight basket of XLU (Utilities Select Sector SPDR) and XLP (Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR) on the next trading day. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Check condition daily. The Svensson zero-coupon curve is the closest available proxy for STRIPS yields on FRED.
entry_conditions: FRED SVENY10 minus SVENY02 (zero-coupon 10yr-2yr spread) drops below -100bp
Signal fires on the first day the threshold is breached
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals to avoid re-triggering during same inversion episode

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED series SVENY02 (Fitted Yield on a 2 Year Zero Coupon Bond) and SVENY10 (Fitted Yield on a 10 Year Zero Coupon Bond), from the Federal Reserve Board. Daily frequency, available back to 1961. Alternative: FRED series T10Y2Y for coupon bond spread if Svensson series unavailable.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each date (1998-2025, XLU/XLP inception 1998) where SVENY10-SVENY02 first dropped below -100bp, compute equal-weight XLU+XLP return over 40 trading days vs SPY. With 180-day minimum gap, expect very few events: possibly 2000, 2006-2007, 2022-2023 inversions. If <3 events at -100bp, relax threshold to -75bp or -50bp. Also test with T10Y2Y (coupon-based 10y-2y) as more liquid alternative. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2000 Q1-Q2: deep inversion preceded 2001 recession, XLU outperformed
2006-2007: persistent inversion preceded GFC, defensive sectors lagged initially
2022-2023: deepest inversion in decades (-108bp on coupon curve in July 2023)
implementation_notes: FRED Svensson zero-coupon yields (SVENY series) are the best STRIPS proxy available for free. The actual STRIPS yields are published by Treasury but not easily available in time-series format. -100bp is an extreme threshold that may produce very few events; the backtester should test multiple thresholds (-50bp, -75bp, -100bp). XLU inception Dec 1998, XLP inception Dec 1998. Note: the 2006-2007 inversion led to a recession but with significant lag (18 months); the 40-day holding period may miss the full defensive rotation. The signal is well-known but the zero-coupon specification adds originality vs the standard T10Y2Y version.

Signal fires at most once per year

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: EPA GHGRP facility-level emissions data (free): https://ghgdata.epa.gov/ghgp/main.do - downloadable bulk CSV. Filter by subpart W (petroleum and natural gas systems) and NAICS 486210. Annual frequency, published ~October for prior calendar year.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each annual GHGRP release (2012-2025, data available from 2010 reporting year), check if midstream methane emissions declined >25% YoY. On qualifying years, compute equal-weight WMB+KMI+ET return over 40 trading days post-publication vs SPY. Expected very few signal events (possibly 2-3 where 25% threshold met). If fewer than 3 events, relax to 15% YoY decline. Calculate average excess return, win rate. Note: KMI was private pre-2011 (IPO Feb 2011), ET was Regency Energy pre-2015 merger.

2021-2022: Inflation Reduction Act methane fee announced, accelerated leak detection
2023: EU methane regulation adopted, requiring import certification
implementation_notes: bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) annual frequency gives very few data points, (b) 25% reduction threshold may be rarely met, (c) EPA GHGRP data requires parsing facility-level CSV and aggregating by NAICS. WMB data back to 2000, KMI IPO 2011, ET ticker changed from RGP after 2015 merger. The ESG re-rating thesis is strongest post-2020 as ESG fund flows accelerated. The backtester should note that pre-2020 events may not show the same ESG-driven price response.

name: Census Retail I/S Ratio Record Low by Subsector -> Targeted Restocking Longs
rule: Using yfinance and Census Bureau MRTS data: When Census monthly retail trade survey shows the inventory-to-sales ratio for 'Electronics and Appliance Stores' (NAICS 443) or 'Furniture and Home Furnishings' (NAICS 442) or 'Building Material and Garden' (NAICS 444) hits a new all-time low, go long a subsector-matched stock basket on Census data release date. Electronics: long BBY+WHR. Furniture: long WSM+WHR. Building materials: long HD+LOW. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Census MRTS published monthly, ~6 weeks after month end.
entry_conditions: Census MRTS inventory-to-sales ratio for NAICS 442, 443, or 444 hits new all-time low
I/S ratio must also be declining (current month < prior month, not just matching prior low)
Enter on Census MRTS release date
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals for same subsector

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: BBY (Best Buy), WHR (Whirlpool), WSM (Williams-Sonoma), HD (Home Depot), LOW (Lowe's), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: Census Bureau Monthly Retail Trade Survey (MRTS) via Census API or FRED. FRED series: RETAILIRSA (aggregate), but subsector I/S ratios available via Census API at https://api.census.gov/data/timeseries/eits/mrts. Subsector NAICS codes: 442 (Furniture), 443 (Electronics), 444 (Building Materials). Monthly frequency, ~6 week publication lag.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2000-2025) where any target subsector I/S ratio hit a new all-time low, compute the subsector-matched stock basket return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Track each subsector independently. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic per subsector and combined. Expected events concentrated in: 2021 (COVID inventory depletion, especially electronics and building materials), and scattered pre-pandemic hits. Census MRTS release dates available on Census economic calendar.
known_events: 2021 Q1-Q3: Electronics I/S ratios crashed to record lows amid semiconductor shortage
2021: Building materials I/S ratio hit record low during housing boom
2022: Furniture I/S ratio declined as supply chains caught up but demand persisted
implementation_notes: Census MRTS is free but subsector-level I/S ratios may require Census API access (free, key needed). FRED has aggregate retail I/S (RETAILIRSA) but subsector detail may only be available via Census. The all-time-low condition means signals are clustered during unusual periods (e.g., 2021) and rare otherwise. Stock matching: BBY/WHR for electronics, WSM/WHR for furniture, HD/LOW for building materials. The restocking thesis works best when sales remain positive (I/S decline is supply-driven, not demand collapse). Backtester should verify sales trend is positive when I/S hits lows.

name: FINRA Margin Debt YoY Turn Positive from Deep Negative -> Risk-On Confirmation

name: FRED Retail Inventory/Sales Ratio at 5-Year Low -> Long Retailers on Restocking
rule: Use FRED RETAILIRSA (Retailers Inventories to Sales Ratio, seasonally adjusted). Compute rolling 60-month (5-year) percentile rank. When the I/S ratio drops below the 10th percentile for the first time in a new episode (no signal in prior 3 months), inventories are critically lean relative to sales, making restocking imminent. Long XRT (SPDR S&P Retail ETF) for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.
entry_conditions: FRED RETAILIRSA drops below its 10th percentile on a rolling 60-month basis
No signal fired in the prior 3 months (to avoid consecutive signals during same drawdown)
Go long on first trading day of the month following the signal month
backtest_approach: Load FRED RETAILIRSA monthly. Compute rolling 60-month percentile rank using scipy.stats.percentileofscore. When percentile < 10 and no signal in prior 3 months, enter long XRT on next month start. Hold 63 trading days. Compute per-event returns and aggregate metrics vs SPY. Expect ~10 signal episodes from 2004-2024.
known_events: 2004-12 to 2005-01: post-tech-recovery lean inventories
2007-03 to 2008-05: pre-GFC lean retail inventories
2009-06: post-GFC destocking trough
2010-11: restocking wave after inventory purge
2019-11: pre-COVID lean inventories
2021-01: COVID supply chain depletion extreme low
implementation_notes: XRT ETF inception is June 2006, so earlier signals (2004-2005) need to use individual stocks (WMT, TGT, HD) as proxy. Use scipy.stats.percentileofscore for rolling percentile calculation. RETAILIRSA is monthly and seasonally adjusted. The critical insight is that lean inventories are unsustainable -- restocking orders must come, lifting both revenue and wholesale demand.

Minimum 30 calendar days between signals within same season

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: EWZ (iShares MSCI Brazil ETF), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA AMS Grain Transportation Report (free): https://www.ams.usda.gov/services/transportation-analysis/gtr - weekly barge rate tables in PDF/Excel. Lower Mississippi barge rate index (tariff rates per ton from St. Louis to New Orleans). Historical data available in GTR archive back to 2010+.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each September-November period (2010-2025), identify weeks where lower Mississippi barge rate exceeded 200% of 5-year same-week average. Compute EWZ return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Key events: 2012 (drought low water), 2022 (historic low water on Mississippi). If fewer than 4 events at 200% threshold, relax to 150%. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Note: EWZ is a broad Brazil ETF, not pure-play agriculture, so the signal may be diluted by non-ag Brazilian dynamics.
known_events: 2012 summer: Mississippi low water levels spiked barge rates
2022 October: Historic low water on Mississippi, barge rates hit all-time highs
2023 September: Drought again impacted barge navigation
implementation_notes: USDA GTR is published as PDF/Excel, requiring manual scraping or OCR for automation. EWZ is used as proxy because no pure-play US-listed Brazilian agribusiness ETF exists. BRFS (BRF S.A., Brazilian food processor) is not available on yfinance. The causal chain requires US grain export disruption to benefit Brazil, which is strongest during Oct-Nov soybean/corn export peak. FX effects matter: BRL weakness vs USD during crisis periods may offset commodity gains in EWZ. bt_feasibility is 3 due to PDF data extraction challenges and indirect instrument.

name: DOL State Claims Divergence -> Labor Market Strength Pockets -> Sunbelt REIT Long
rule: Using yfinance and FRED/DOL data: When 4-week average initial claims for Texas+Florida+Arizona+Georgia (Sunbelt states) decline > 20% YoY while national 4-week average claims are flat or rising (YoY change > -5%), go long an equal-weight basket of Sunbelt-heavy REITs (SUI, EQR, AMT) on the Thursday following the DOL weekly report. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. DOL publishes state-level claims data weekly with a 1-week lag. Use FRED series for state-level claims: TXICLAIMS, FLICLAIMS, AZICLAIMS, GAICLAIMS, ICSA (national).
entry_conditions: 4-week average initial claims for TX+FL+AZ+GA combined decline > 20% YoY
National 4-week average initial claims (ICSA) YoY change is > -5% (flat or rising)
Enter on Thursday following DOL weekly report
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: SUI (Sun Communities), EQR (Equity Residential), AMT (American Tower), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED series: ICSA (national initial claims), TXICLAIMS (Texas), FLICLAIMS (Florida), AZICLAIMS (Arizona), GAICLAIMS (Georgia). Weekly frequency, Thursday release with 1-week lag. All series are free and available back to 1987.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each week (2004-2025, SUI IPO 1993 so not limiting), compute 4-week average of TX+FL+AZ+GA claims and compare YoY. When Sunbelt claims decline >20% YoY while national ICSA is flat/rising, compute equal-weight SUI+EQR+AMT return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Expected events during regional divergence periods: 2010-2011 (Sunbelt recovery from housing crisis), 2021 (Sunbelt reopening ahead of national average), 2023 (Sunbelt labor market outperformance).
known_events: 2010-2011: Sunbelt states recovered faster from housing bust
2021 Q2-Q3: Sunbelt states reopened earlier, claims fell faster
2023: Sunbelt labor markets remained tight despite national softening
implementation_notes: FRED state claims series are reliable and well-maintained. The Sunbelt state selection (TX, FL, AZ, GA) captures the main population growth and migration destinations. SUI (manufactured housing/RV communities) has strong Sunbelt exposure, EQR has mixed geographic but growing Sunbelt, AMT (cell towers) benefits from population growth areas. The 20% Sunbelt improvement vs flat national is a meaningful divergence. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) the REIT tickers don't perfectly map to state-level exposure, (b) claims data is noisy and seasonal adjustment matters.

name: FHFA HPI Acceleration in Top 10 States -> Title Insurance/Mortgage Originator Vo
rule: Using yfinance data: 1. FHFA HPI shows QoQ acceleration in 7+ of top 10 population states (free) Long (FAF+FNF) for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.

name: USDA APHIS HPAI Outbreak Acceleration -> Egg Supply Shock -> CALM Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA APHIS HPAI data: When USDA APHIS confirmed HPAI commercial flock depopulations exceed 10 million birds cumulative in a rolling 30-day window, go long CALM (Cal-Maine Foods, largest US shell egg producer) on the next trading day. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USDA APHIS publishes confirmed HPAI detections in near-real-time on their website with flock size for each detection. Sum commercial poultry depopulations (layers + pullets) over rolling 30-day windows.
entry_conditions: USDA APHIS confirmed HPAI commercial flock depopulations exceed 10M birds in rolling 30-day window
Only count commercial layer and pullet flocks (not backyard, not broiler)
Enter on next trading day after threshold is breached
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals (to avoid re-triggering during same outbreak wave)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA APHIS HPAI confirmed detections table (free): https://www.aphis.usda.gov/livestock-poultry-disease/avian/avian-influenza/hpai-detections/commercial-backyard-flocks - CSV/table with date_confirmed, state, flock_type, flock_size. Sum flock_size for commercial flocks over rolling 30-day windows.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each date (2015-2025) where rolling 30-day HPAI commercial depopulations exceeded 10M birds, compute CALM return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Key outbreak waves: Spring 2015 (49M birds total, concentrated in Midwest layers), 2022 (58M birds), 2024-2025 (ongoing). With 90-day minimum gap, expect 4-6 discrete signal events. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. CALM is uniquely positioned as the largest US egg producer and benefits from egg price spikes when competitors' flocks are depopulated.
known_events: 2015-04 to 2015-06: H5N2 outbreak depopulated 49M birds, egg prices tripled
2022-02 to 2022-06: H5N1 first wave, 38M commercial birds
2022-10 to 2023-03: H5N1 second wave, 20M+ additional birds
2024-2025: Ongoing H5N1 outbreaks in commercial poultry
implementation_notes: CALM is the only major pure-play publicly traded US shell egg producer. Their flock has historically been less affected (geographic diversification, biosecurity). When competitors' flocks are destroyed, egg prices spike and CALM captures windfall profits. APHIS data is published in near-real-time and is easily parseable. The 10M threshold is calibrated to capture only major outbreak waves, not isolated farm detections. bt_feasibility is 4 because APHIS data is clean, CALM has long history, and the causal mechanism (supply shock -> price spike -> windfall) is very direct.

name: Census Defense Capital Goods Orders Surge -> Defense Prime Backlog Expansion
rule: Using yfinance and FRED/Census data: When FRED series A007RC1Q027SBEA (or NEWORDER defense capital goods, not seasonally adjusted) shows month-over-month increase > 30%, go long an equal-weight basket of defense primes (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD) on the advance durable goods release date. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Census advance durable goods published ~26th of each month for prior month. Use FRED series ADEFAI (Advance New Orders for Defense Capital Goods) for clean monthly data.
entry_conditions: FRED ADEFAI (Defense capital goods new orders, advance) shows > 30% MoM increase
Enter on Census advance durable goods release date (typically 26th of month)
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: LMT (Lockheed Martin), RTX (RTX Corp, formerly Raytheon), NOC (Northrop Grumman), GD (General Dynamics), ITA (iShares US Aerospace & Defense ETF, robustness check), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED series ADEFAI (Manufacturers' New Orders: Defense Capital Goods, Advance, Not Seasonally Adjusted) or ADXFAI (advance durable excluding transport for context). Monthly, released ~26th. Alternative: Census M3 detailed data at https://www.census.gov/manufacturing/m3/

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2002-2025) where ADEFAI MoM change exceeded 30%, compute equal-weight LMT+RTX+NOC+GD basket return over 30 trading days post-release vs SPY. RTX was UTX pre-2020 merger (use UTX for pre-2020 backtest). Also test with ITA ETF (inception 2001) as single-ticker robustness check. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Defense orders are lumpy (driven by large contract awards), so 30% MoM spikes occur episodically. Expected 2-4 events per year on average.
known_events: 2007-2008: Iraq/Afghanistan surge procurement spikes
2014-2015: Defense budget recovery after sequester
2018: National Defense Strategy increased procurement
2022-2023: Ukraine war-driven defense spending surge
2024-2025: Continued defense budget expansion
implementation_notes: RTX ticker changed from UTX in April 2020 after Raytheon/UTX merger. For pre-2020 backtest, use UTX as the Raytheon proxy. ADEFAI on FRED is not seasonally adjusted which makes MoM comparisons meaningful for detecting true order spikes (seasonal adjustment would mask legitimate defense procurement cycles). ITA ETF inception was May 2001. The 30% MoM threshold is high enough to filter noise but should still generate 15-30 events over 2002-2025 given the lumpy nature of defense contracts.

name: EIA Pipeline Utilization >95% -> Basis Blowout -> Midstream Toll Revenue Surge
rule: Using yfinance and EIA natural gas data: When the Waha-Henry Hub natural gas basis differential (a proxy for Permian pipeline congestion) exceeds $1.50/MMBtu (indicating pipeline congestion), go long an equal-weight basket of Permian-exposed midstream operators (WMB, KMI, ET) on the next trading day. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Use yfinance natural gas prices and EIA weekly natural gas storage/flow data. The Waha hub price can be proxied by the spread between Permian production data and Henry Hub (NG=F).
entry_conditions: Waha-Henry Hub basis differential exceeds $1.50/MMBtu (Henry Hub spot minus Waha spot)
Use weekly average basis to avoid daily noise
Enter on next trading day after weekly threshold breach
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: WMB (Williams Companies), KMI (Kinder Morgan), ET (Energy Transfer), NG=F (Henry Hub natural gas futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: EIA Natural Gas Weekly Update (free): https://www.eia.gov/naturalgas/weekly/ - pipeline flow data and basis differentials. EIA also publishes Waha spot price in weekly natural gas spot prices (free). FRED series: DHHNGSP (Henry Hub spot price) available daily.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each week (2012-2025) where Waha-Henry Hub basis exceeded $1.50/MMBtu (weekly average), compute equal-weight WMB+KMI+ET return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Permian pipeline constraints were most severe 2017-2019 (pre-Gulf Coast Express, Permian Highway). Post-2020, new pipeline capacity reduced but didn't eliminate congestion events. If Waha spot not available on yfinance, use EIA weekly data or proxy with Henry Hub (NG=F) + Permian production growth rate. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2017-2019: Severe Permian pipeline congestion, Waha went negative multiple times
2019: Gulf Coast Express pipeline came online, partially relieving congestion
2020: Permian Highway Pipeline added more capacity
2023: Periodic congestion events during production surges
implementation_notes: bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) Waha spot prices may not be directly available on yfinance (NG=F is Henry Hub only), (b) basis differential data may require EIA or ICE data subscription, (c) pipeline utilization data is not published in real-time by EIA. The backtester should attempt to use EIA weekly natural gas spot price tables (free) which include Waha alongside Henry Hub. If unavailable, proxy the signal using NG=F price drops (when Permian is congested, Waha drops relative to Henry Hub). WMB (Transco pipeline), KMI (multiple pipelines), ET (Permian-to-Gulf lines) all benefit from congestion.

name: USDA Export Sales Unknown Destination Surge -> China Flash Demand -> Soy Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA weekly export sales data: When USDA weekly Export Sales Report shows soybean net sales to 'Unknown Destinations' exceed 1.5 million metric tons in a single weekly report, go long SOYB (Teucrium Soybean Fund) on the Friday following the Thursday report release. Hold for 15 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USDA Export Sales Report published every Thursday at 8:30 AM ET. The 'unknown destinations' category is a well-known proxy for Chinese purchases through intermediary traders.
entry_conditions: USDA Weekly Export Sales Report shows soybean net sales to 'Unknown Destinations' > 1.5M metric tons
Enter on Friday (day after Thursday report release)
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: SOYB (Teucrium Soybean Fund), ZS=F (CBOT soybean futures continuous), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA FAS Weekly Export Sales Report (free): https://apps.fas.usda.gov/export-sales/esrd1.html - downloadable CSV/Excel by commodity and destination. Filter commodity=SOYBEANS, destination='UNKNOWN'. Weekly frequency, Thursday 8:30 AM ET release. Historical data back to 1999.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each Thursday report (2012-2025, SOYB inception Sep 2011) where unknown destination soybean sales exceeded 1.5M MT, compute SOYB return over 15 trading days post-Friday-entry vs SPY. Also test with ZS=F for longer history. The 1.5M MT threshold is approximately 3-4 standard deviations above the typical unknown destination weekly volume of 200-500K MT. Expected 5-15 events over the period, concentrated during US-China trade tension periods and Chinese buying seasons (Aug-Nov). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2014-2015: Large unknown soybean sales later confirmed as China
2016: Record Chinese soybean purchases via unknown destination
2020 Sep-Nov: Phase 1 trade deal Chinese buying surge via unknowns
2022-2023: Intermittent large unknown destination sales
implementation_notes: USDA export sales data is freely downloadable in CSV format. The 'Unknown Destinations' coding is standard USDA practice when the final buyer's country hasn't been confirmed at booking time. SOYB ETF tracks soybean futures but with tracking error and contango effects. ZS=F provides purer soybean exposure for longer history. The signal is well-known among agricultural commodity traders but underutilized by equity-focused investors. The 1.5M MT threshold may need adjustment; if fewer than 5 events, try 1.0M MT. bt_feasibility is 4 because USDA data is clean, free, and the mechanism is direct.
