name: FRED Temp Help Employment YoY Turns Positive -> Long Staffing + Small-Cap Cyclicals
rule: Use FRED TEMPHELPS (All Employees, Temporary Help Services). When YoY growth turns positive after 3+ consecutive months of negative YoY growth, the early-cycle recovery is confirmed. Temp help is a well-known leading indicator: firms hire temps before committing to permanent headcount. Long RHI+MAN+IWM equal-weight for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY.
entry_conditions: FRED TEMPHELPS YoY growth turns positive (crosses above 0%)
Must have been negative for at least 3 consecutive prior months
No signal in prior 6 months (cooldown to avoid duplicate entries in same cycle)
backtest_approach: Load FRED TEMPHELPS monthly. Compute YoY % change. Track consecutive negative months. When YoY crosses above 0% after 3+ months negative, and no signal in prior 6 months, enter long RHI+MAN+IWM equal-weight. Hold 63 trading days. Compute per-event returns and aggregate metrics vs SPY. Expected 5-6 signal events from 2002-2024.
known_events: 2002-11: post-dot-com recovery signal
2003-07: sustained recovery confirmation
2010-02: post-GFC recovery signal
2016-09: mid-cycle recovery after mini-industrial-recession
2021-03: post-COVID recovery signal
implementation_notes: TEMPHELPS is monthly, seasonally adjusted. RHI (Robert Half) and MAN (ManpowerGroup) are pure-play staffing firms that benefit directly from temp hiring recovery. IWM (Russell 2000) captures broader small-cap cyclical recovery. HSON (Heidrick & Struggles) is delisted, excluded. This is a well-known leading indicator but the specific long-basket construction adds alpha potential.

name: BEA GDP Advance Beat vs Atlanta Fed GDPNow -> Growth Surprise Rotation
rule: Using yfinance and BEA/Atlanta Fed data: When BEA GDP advance estimate exceeds the final Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate by > 0.5 percentage points (annualized), go long an equal-weight basket of cyclical sector ETFs (XLI, XLB, XLF) on the GDP release date. Hold for 15 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. BEA GDP advance published ~30 days after quarter end (late January, April, July, October). GDPNow final estimate published on last business day before GDP release.
entry_conditions: BEA GDP advance estimate exceeds final GDPNow estimate by > 0.5 percentage points (annualized QoQ)
Enter on BEA GDP advance release date
At most one signal per quarter

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XLI (Industrial Select Sector SPDR), XLB (Materials Select Sector SPDR), XLF (Financial Select Sector SPDR), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: BEA GDP advance estimate: FRED series GDP or A191RL1Q225SBEA. Atlanta Fed GDPNow: https://www.atlantafed.org/cqer/research/gdpnow (free, historical estimates archived). GDP release dates at BEA website. GDPNow has been published since 2011.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each quarter (2011Q3-2025, GDPNow inception mid-2011), compare BEA GDP advance to final GDPNow. When BEA beat GDPNow by >0.5pp, compute equal-weight XLI+XLB+XLF return over 15 trading days vs SPY. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. The GDPNow model is designed to converge to the advance estimate, so large divergences are relatively rare (expect 5-12 events over 14 years). If too few at 0.5pp, relax to 0.3pp.
known_events: 2014 Q3: GDP advance significantly beat expectations and tracking models
2021 Q4: Strong GDP growth exceeded most nowcast estimates
2023 Q3: GDP advance of 4.9% surprised to the upside vs models
implementation_notes: GDPNow historical estimates are archived on the Atlanta Fed website. The 'final' GDPNow is the last estimate published before the BEA advance release (typically the day before). XLI (industrials), XLB (materials), XLF (financials) are the most GDP-sensitive sector ETFs. All three have inception Dec 1998. The 0.5pp threshold is meaningful: GDPNow typically converges to within 0.3pp of the advance estimate, so a 0.5pp miss signals genuine hidden strength not captured by standard high-frequency data. bt_feasibility is 4 because all data is free, quarterly frequency gives manageable events, and the mechanism is direct.

name: NOAA PDO Phase Shift to Positive -> Multi-Year Agricultural Productivity -> Farm Equipment Long
rule: Using yfinance and NOAA PDO data: When NOAA PDO monthly index shifts from negative to positive (3-month average crosses above zero after being below zero for at least 12 consecutive months), go long an equal-weight basket of farm equipment companies (DE, AGCO, CNH) on the first trading day of the following month. Hold for 60 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. NOAA PDO index published monthly. The 12-month negative prerequisite ensures this captures genuine phase shifts, not noise.
entry_conditions: NOAA PDO 3-month moving average crosses above zero
Prior 12 months of 3-month average were all below zero (genuine negative phase ending)
Enter on first trading day of month following the crossover month
Signal fires at most once per multi-year PDO cycle

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: DE (Deere & Company), AGCO (AGCO Corporation), CNH (CNH Industrial), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NOAA PDO index: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/pub/data/cmb/ersst/v5/index/ersst.v5.pdo.dat (free). Monthly index values since 1854. Also available via NOAA Climate Prediction Center.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each PDO negative-to-positive phase transition (1990-2025), compute equal-weight DE+AGCO+CNH return over 60 trading days vs SPY. PDO phase shifts are rare (roughly 20-30 year cycles with shorter fluctuations). Major shifts: ~1998 (negative started), ~2014 (brief positive), ~2023 (potential positive shift). Given rarity (~2-4 events in 35 years), this is a low-frequency signal. Supplement with medium-term PDO fluctuations (12-month negative then positive) which occur more frequently. Calculate cumulative excess return per event.
known_events: 1977: PDO shifted to warm/positive phase (lasted until ~1998)
1998-1999: PDO shifted to cool/negative phase
2014-2015: Brief positive PDO excursion
2023-2024: Potential PDO positive phase shift
implementation_notes: PDO phase shifts are decadal-scale phenomena; the 12-month negative prerequisite captures genuine transitions but may produce only 2-4 events in the backtest window. AGCO IPO was 1994, CNH IPO (as CNH Global) was 1999 (re-IPO as CNH Industrial 2013). DE has data back to 1970s. The agricultural mechanism (precipitation patterns -> crop yields -> farm income -> equipment demand) operates with significant lag (12-24 months), so the 60 trading day hold may not capture the full effect. bt_feasibility is 3 due to extremely low signal frequency. The backtester should also consider running a regression of PDO on DE returns to assess correlation at monthly frequency as a supplement to the event study.

name: USGS Colorado River Flow Below Critical -> Water Utility Emergency Capex
rule: Using yfinance and USGS NWIS data: When USGS daily streamflow at Lee Ferry, AZ (gauge 09380000) shows 90-day average flow below 50% of the historical median for that 90-day period, go long an equal-weight basket of water infrastructure stocks (XYL, AWK, WMS) on the next trading day. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USGS NWIS provides real-time and historical streamflow data for free. Lee Ferry is the official accounting point for Upper/Lower Colorado River Basin allocations.

Enter on next trading day after condition is met
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals (drought episodes are multi-month)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XYL (Xylem Inc), AWK (American Water Works), WMS (Advanced Drainage Systems), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USGS NWIS: https://waterdata.usgs.gov/nwis/uv?site_no=09380000 (free, real-time and historical daily mean streamflow in cfs). Gauge 09380000 = Colorado River at Lee Ferry, AZ. Data available back to 1895.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each date (2011-2025, XYL IPO Oct 2011) where 90-day average Lee Ferry flow dropped below 50% of historical median, compute XYL+AWK+WMS basket return over 40 trading days vs SPY. With 180-day gap, expect events during: 2002 drought, 2012-2013 drought, 2020-2023 mega-drought. XYL limits backtest to 2011+. Calculate average excess return, win rate. The mechanism operates through increased capex spending on water recycling, desalination, and conservation infrastructure.

2012-2013: Low flow year reduced Lake Mead levels
2020-2023: Unprecedented mega-drought, Lake Mead hit record lows

implementation_notes: XYL (Xylem) IPO was October 2011, limiting backtest. AWK IPO 2008, WMS IPO 2014. For pre-2014 backtest, use XYL+AWK only. USGS NWIS data is excellent quality, free, and easily downloadable as CSV. The 50% threshold is aggressive; if too few events, relax to 60% of median. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) Colorado River drought is a slow-moving signal, (b) the capex response has long lag, (c) limited events in backtest window.

name: BLS Healthcare Wage Acceleration + JOLTS Openings -> Long Travel Nurse Agencies
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When BLS CES healthcare average hourly earnings (FRED series CES6562000008 or CEU6562000008) show YoY growth > 5% AND JOLTS healthcare job openings (FRED series JTS6200JOL) remain above their 2-year median, go long equal-weight basket of AMN + CCRN on the first trading day after data release. Hold for 63 trading days (approximately 3 months). Benchmark vs SPY.

JOLTS healthcare job openings above their trailing 24-month median
Both conditions must be true simultaneously
Enter on first trading day after BLS CES monthly release
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals to avoid overlapping positions

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: CES6562000008 or CEU6562000008 (healthcare avg hourly earnings, monthly), JTS6200JOL (JOLTS healthcare job openings, monthly). Both free, monthly frequency, ~1 month lag. Historical data back to 2001+ for CES, 2001 for JOLTS.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED series for healthcare hourly earnings and JOLTS healthcare openings (2005-2025, ensuring AMN/CCRN price history available). Compute monthly YoY growth in healthcare earnings. Identify months where earnings YoY > 5% AND JOLTS openings above 24-month median. For each signal month (with 90-day minimum gap), long AMN+CCRN equal-weight for 63 trading days. Compute per-trade returns and aggregate metrics vs SPY. known_events: 2007: Pre-financial-crisis nurse shortage drove wage growth
2015-2016: ACA Medicaid expansion increased hospital volumes and nurse demand
2021 Q2-Q4: COVID Delta/Omicron waves caused massive travel nurse demand spike
2022 Q1-Q2: Peak travel nurse bill rates ($150-200/hr vs normal $50-70)
2024: Post-pandemic normalization but persistent shortages in aging population
implementation_notes: AMN Healthcare IPO 2001, CCRN IPO 2001 -- both have sufficient history. FRED healthcare earnings series may need to check both CES6562000008 (SA) and CEU6562000008 (NSA). JOLTS healthcare may be aggregated under 'Health care and social assistance' (code 62). The thesis is that travel nurse agencies benefit from wage acceleration because they charge a markup over permanent wages, and the bill-pay spread widens when demand is acute. The 5% YoY threshold is meaningful because healthcare wage growth historically averages 2-3%. bt_feasibility is 4 because both FRED series are clean and monthly.

name: Army Corps Dredging Delays -> Port Draft Restrictions -> LTL Carrier Volume Surge
rule: Using yfinance and USACE navigation data: When USACE issues draft restrictions reducing Mississippi River Southwest Pass channel depth below 45 feet (normal is 50 ft) for 2+ consecutive weeks, go long an equal-weight basket of LTL carriers (XPO, ODFL, SAIA) on the Monday following the second week of restrictions. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USACE navigation notices are free at navigation.usace.army.mil. Draft restrictions force vessel light-loading, increasing per-unit inland logistics costs and diverting cargo to trucking.
entry_conditions: USACE Mississippi River Southwest Pass draft restriction < 45 feet for 2+ consecutive weeks
Enter on Monday following second week of restriction
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XPO (XPO Logistics), ODFL (Old Dominion Freight Line), SAIA (Saia Inc), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USACE Navigation Notices (free): navigation.usace.army.mil - Mississippi River channel conditions. Also USACE Lock Performance Monitoring System for river traffic data. Draft restriction notices issued in real-time.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2012-2025, XPO spun off logistics 2011) where Mississippi River draft restrictions tightened below 45 ft for 2+ weeks, compute XPO+ODFL+SAIA basket return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Key events: 2012 drought (record low water), 2022 October (historic low Mississippi levels). USACE data may need manual extraction from navigation notices archive. If fewer than 4 events at 45 ft threshold, relax to 47 ft.
known_events: 2012 summer/fall: Severe drought reduced Mississippi draft to 39 ft in places
2022 October: Historic low water, draft restricted to 35-40 ft at SW Pass
2023 September: Drought again restricted Mississippi navigation
implementation_notes: bt_feasibility is 3 because USACE navigation notices are published as text/PDF and require manual parsing. XPO underwent major restructuring (spun off GXO in 2021, RXO in 2022), so pre-2022 XPO data reflects a different company mix. ODFL and SAIA are cleaner LTL pure-plays. The signal is strongest during September-November when grain export season coincides with low-water risk. Draft restrictions at Houston Ship Channel (another major port) could serve as additional signal source.

name: USDA DDGS Export Surge -> Ethanol Coproduct Revenue -> Corn Ethanol Producer Margins
rule: Using yfinance and USDA FAS export sales data: When USDA weekly Export Sales Report shows DDGS (Dried Distillers Grains) net sales exceed 4-week trailing average by > 30%, go long an equal-weight basket of ethanol producers (GPRE, REX, ANDE) on the Friday following the Thursday report. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USDA FAS publishes DDGS export sales in the weekly Export Sales Report alongside grain commodities. DDGS is categorized under 'Corn Gluten Feed and Meal' or 'DDGS' separately.
entry_conditions: USDA weekly Export Sales Report DDGS net sales exceed 4-week trailing average by > 30%
Enter on Friday following Thursday report release
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: GPRE (Green Plains Inc), REX (REX Energy, ethanol interests), ANDE (Andersons Inc, ethanol segment), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA FAS Weekly Export Sales Report (free): https://apps.fas.usda.gov/export-sales/ - filter for DDGS or 'Distillers Dried Grains'. Weekly frequency, Thursday release. Also EIA weekly ethanol production (free) for context on production volumes.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each Thursday report (2015-2025) where DDGS export sales spiked >30% above 4-week average, compute GPRE+REX+ANDE basket return over 30 trading days vs SPY. GPRE is the purest ethanol play. REX has ethanol investments but also retail (noisy). ANDE has ethanol plus grain merchandising. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. China DDGS demand is episodic (trade policy dependent), so signals may cluster during favorable trade periods.
known_events: 2016-2017: China DDGS anti-dumping duties disrupted trade, then shifted to Vietnam
2020: Phase 1 trade deal increased China ag purchases including DDGS
2023-2024: Southeast Asia DDGS demand surge as feed ingredient
implementation_notes: GPRE IPO was 2006, REX has been public since 1984 (but ethanol focus varies), ANDE has been public since 1996. The DDGS coproduct credit is a meaningful swing factor for ethanol margins ($0.10-0.15/gallon on ~$0.50-0.80/gallon gross margin). USDA FAS data is free and downloadable. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) DDGS is sometimes bundled with 'Corn Gluten Feed' in USDA reporting making extraction tricky, (b) ethanol stocks are affected by many factors beyond DDGS (ethanol RIN prices, corn basis, gasoline blending margins).

name: STB Railroad Velocity Decline -> Modal Diversion -> Long Truckload Carriers
rule: Using yfinance data and STB railroad service metrics: When Class I railroad average train velocity (available weekly from STB) drops >15% below its 52-week moving average for 2+ consecutive weeks, go long equal-weight basket of JBHT + KNX on the next trading day. Hold for 42 trading days (approximately 2 months). Benchmark vs SPY. As a backtest proxy without STB API parsing, use UNP/CSX stock underperformance vs SPY >10% over 4 weeks as a signal that rail service is deteriorating (rail stocks reflect service metrics).
entry_conditions: Primary signal: STB weekly data shows Class I avg train velocity >15% below 52-week average for 2+ consecutive weeks
Backtest proxy: UNP+CSX equal-weight 20-day return underperforms SPY by >10% (rail stock weakness signals service degradation)
Enter on next trading day after signal confirmation
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: JBHT (J.B. Hunt Transport Services), KNX (Knight-Swift Transportation), UNP (Union Pacific), CSX (CSX Corporation), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: STB weekly railroad service metrics (free at stb.gov). For backtest, use UNP/CSX price action as proxy for rail service quality. AAR weekly rail traffic data (free) as supplementary confirmation.

backtest_approach: Event study using rail stock proxy: For each date (2007-2025) where UNP+CSX equal-weight 20-day return underperforms SPY by >10%, compute JBHT+KNX basket return over next 42 trading days vs SPY. Apply 60-day minimum gap between signals. Key expected events: 2014 Q1 (polar vortex rail freeze), 2017 Q4 (Hurricane Harvey rail disruption), 2021 Q4-2022 Q1 (post-COVID rail service collapse, PSR-related crew shortages), 2023 Q1 (post-strike service recovery delays). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2014 Q1: Polar vortex froze rail operations across northern tier, massive intermodal diversion
2017 Q3-Q4: Hurricane Harvey disrupted Gulf Coast rail networks; Houston terminal congestion
2021 Q4-2022 Q2: Post-COVID rail service collapse; PSR-driven crew cuts left railroads unable to handle volume recovery
2022 Q3: Rail labor dispute threats caused preemptive freight diversion to trucking
2023 Q1: Lingering service recovery post-strike; STB hearings on Class I service failures
implementation_notes: JBHT IPO 1983, KNX formed 2017 (use KNX from 2017+, WERN as substitute pre-2017). The proxy approach using rail stock underperformance is a reasonable substitute for STB weekly data which requires web scraping. The mechanism is well-documented: STB held multiple hearings in 2022 on Class I service failures, and FreightWaves documented modal shift to trucking. WERN dropped from basket because JBHT and KNX are larger and more liquid. bt_feasibility is 4 because the rail stock proxy is clean and the modal diversion mechanism is well-established.

name: STB Railroad Service Metrics -> Train Velocity Decline -> Trucking Diversion Long
rule: Using yfinance and STB railroad service data: When STB weekly Class I railroad average train velocity drops > 15% below its 52-week moving average AND average terminal dwell time exceeds 30 hours, go long an equal-weight basket of truckload carriers (JBHT, WERN, KNX) on the Monday following the weekly STB report. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. STB publishes weekly service metrics for all Class I railroads (BNSF, UP, CSX, NS, KCS, CN, CP) with train speed, dwell, cars on line, etc.
entry_conditions: STB weekly Class I average train velocity > 15% below 52-week moving average
STB weekly average terminal dwell time exceeds 30 hours
Both conditions must be met simultaneously
Enter on Monday following weekly STB data release
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: JBHT (J.B. Hunt Transport), WERN (Werner Enterprises), KNX (Knight-Swift Transportation), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: STB weekly railroad service metrics (free): https://www.stb.gov/reports-data/rail-service-data/ - CSV/Excel with train_velocity_mph, terminal_dwell_hours, cars_on_line by railroad. Weekly frequency. Also AAR weekly rail traffic carloadings (free at aar.org) for context.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each week (2014-2025, STB weekly reporting began ~2014 after PSR implementation drove service issues) where both velocity and dwell conditions were met, compute JBHT+WERN+KNX basket return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Key rail service crises: 2014 (polar vortex + grain backlog), 2017 (CSX precision scheduled railroading transition), 2021-2022 (post-COVID labor shortages). With 90-day gap, expect 4-8 discrete events. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2014 Q1: Polar vortex crushed rail velocity, massive grain backlog
2017 Q3-Q4: CSX PSR implementation caused severe service failures
2021 Q4-2022 Q2: Post-COVID rail labor shortages degraded service
2022: STB held hearings on railroad service quality amid shipper complaints
implementation_notes: STB data is clean, free, and available as downloadable CSV. The dual condition (low velocity + high dwell) ensures we capture genuine service crises, not normal seasonal variation. JBHT has significant intermodal business so ironically benefits from intermodal-to-OTR diversion. WERN and KNX are predominantly OTR carriers that capture diverted freight. bt_feasibility is 4 because STB data is well-structured and the modal diversion mechanism is well-documented by industry analysts.

name: CDC RSV-NET Pediatric Hospitalization Surge -> Nirsevimab Demand Pull -> SNY/AZN Long
rule: Using yfinance and CDC RSV-NET data: When CDC RSV-NET weekly pediatric (0-4 year) RSV hospitalization rate in September or October exceeds the prior 3-season average for that same week by > 50%, go long an equal-weight basket of SNY (Sanofi) and AZN (AstraZeneca) on the Monday following the CDC weekly report. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. CDC RSV-NET publishes weekly hospitalization rates by age group during RSV season (October-April). Beyfortus (nirsevimab) is co-developed by Sanofi and AZN.
entry_conditions: CDC RSV-NET pediatric (0-4yr) weekly hospitalization rate exceeds prior 3-season same-week average by > 50%
Signal window: September 1 through October 31 (early season indicator)
Enter on Monday following CDC weekly data publication
Signal fires once per RSV season (first week threshold is breached)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: SNY (Sanofi ADR), AZN (AstraZeneca ADR), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: CDC RSV-NET (free): https://www.cdc.gov/rsv/research/rsv-net/dashboard.html - weekly RSV-associated hospitalization rates per 100,000 by age group. Published weekly during surveillance season. Also CDC NREVSS (National Respiratory and Enteric Virus Surveillance System) for RSV PCR positivity rates.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each RSV season (2018-2025, Beyfortus approved 2023 so pre-2023 tests the thesis without the product), identify weeks in Sep-Oct where pediatric RSV hospitalization exceeded 3-season average by >50%. Compute SNY+AZN basket return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Pre-Beyfortus seasons (2018-2022) test whether early severe RSV season drives related pharma interest. Post-Beyfortus (2023-2025) tests direct revenue impact. Key seasons: 2022 (unusually early and severe), 2023 (first Beyfortus season). If <3 events, relax to 30% threshold.
known_events: 2022 Q4: Unprecedented early and severe RSV season, hospitals overwhelmed
2023 Q4: First Beyfortus season, supply constraints initially
2024 Q4: Second Beyfortus season with improved supply
implementation_notes: bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) Beyfortus only launched in 2023 so direct revenue signal has very limited history, (b) SNY and AZN are large diversified pharma companies so RSV product impact is diluted in stock price, (c) CDC RSV-NET started systematic surveillance ~2018. SNY ADR and AZN ADR trade on NYSE and have good liquidity. The signal is strongest post-2023 when Beyfortus revenue directly ties to RSV season severity. Pre-2023 backtest serves as a weaker test of RSV-driven pharma sentiment.

name: EIA Refinery Utilization Drop During Driving Season -> Long Refiner Basket
rule: Using yfinance and EIA/FRED data: When EIA weekly refinery utilization (FRED series WRRTSTUS1 or equivalent) drops below 85% during driving season (May 1 - September 30) for 2+ consecutive weeks, go long equal-weight basket of VLO + MPC on the next trading day. Hold for 21 trading days (approximately 1 month). Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that operating refiners capture windfall margins when peers have unplanned outages during peak demand.
entry_conditions: EIA weekly refinery utilization rate drops below 85% during May-September
Condition persists for 2+ consecutive weekly reports
Enter on first trading day after second consecutive sub-85% reading
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: VLO (Valero Energy), MPC (Marathon Petroleum), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED/EIA: Weekly US refinery utilization rate (WRRTSTUS1 or WPULEUS3). Published every Wednesday in EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report. Free, weekly frequency, 1-week lag. Historical data back to 1990+. Also: EIA gasoline days of supply (WGDSTUS1) as confirmation signal.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED weekly refinery utilization data (1995-2025 for VLO history). Identify May-September weeks where utilization drops below 85% for 2+ consecutive weeks. For each signal event (with 30-day min gap), long VLO+MPC basket for 21 trading days. Compute per-trade return vs SPY. Key expected events: 2005 (Hurricanes Katrina/Rita), 2008 (Hurricane Ike), 2012 (Hurricane Isaac + Chevron Richmond fire), 2017 (Hurricane Harvey), 2019 (Philadelphia Energy Solutions explosion), 2021 (Hurricane Ida + winter storm freeze lingering). MPC formed from Marathon Oil spin-off in 2011; use VLO only pre-2011 or substitute PSX/XOM refining proxy.
known_events: 2005 Aug-Sep: Hurricanes Katrina and Rita shut Gulf Coast refining; utilization dropped to ~70%
2008 Sep: Hurricane Ike shut Texas refineries; gasoline cracks spiked $25/bbl
2012 Aug: Hurricane Isaac + Chevron Richmond refinery fire; utilization ~83%
2017 Aug-Sep: Hurricane Harvey flooded Houston refining corridor; utilization ~77%
2019 Jun: Philadelphia Energy Solutions refinery explosion; East Coast supply squeeze
2021 Feb: Texas winter storm knocked utilization to ~56% (outside driving season but shows mechanism)
implementation_notes: VLO has been public since 1997, providing excellent backtest depth. MPC spun off from Marathon Oil in June 2011 -- use VLO-only or add PSX (spun from COP April 2012) for pre-2011 period. EIA refinery utilization is one of the cleanest weekly macro datasets available. The 85% threshold during driving season is meaningful because normal seasonal utilization is 90-95% May-Sep. The 2021 winter storm is outside driving season so would not trigger. bt_feasibility is 5 because EIA data is excellent quality and the refiner stock response to crack spread widening is well-documented.

backtest_result.reason: FRED refinery utilization series not available

name: FEMA Flood Map Revision -> Mandatory Insurance Expansion -> Flood Insurer Premium Growth
rule: Using yfinance and FEMA Map Service Center data: When FEMA issues a preliminary FIRM (Flood Insurance Rate Map) update that expands Special Flood Hazard Areas (SFHA) in a metro area with population > 1M, go long an equal-weight basket of flood-focused insurers (HCI, PLMR) on the first trading day after FEMA preliminary map issuance. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. FEMA Map Service Center (msc.fema.gov) publishes all preliminary and effective FIRMs for free. Focus on metro-scale revisions that add >10,000 properties to SFHA zones.
entry_conditions: FEMA issues preliminary FIRM expanding SFHA for a metro area with population > 1M
Expansion adds estimated > 10,000 properties to mandatory flood insurance requirement
Enter on first trading day after FEMA preliminary FIRM issuance
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: HCI (HCI Group Inc, Florida flood insurer), PLMR (Palomar Holdings, specialty insurer with flood line), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FEMA Map Service Center (free): https://msc.fema.gov - preliminary FIRM updates by community/county. FEMA NFIP data: https://www.fema.gov/data-visualization-floods - policy counts and written premium by state/county.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each major FEMA FIRM expansion (2015-2025), compute HCI+PLMR return over 40 trading days vs SPY. PLMR IPO was April 2019, limiting pair history. For pre-2019, use HCI only. Major FIRM revisions are infrequent but impactful. FEMA has been updating maps nationwide under Risk Rating 2.0 (effective Oct 2021), creating multiple events. Calculate average excess return, win rate. Note: the market may not react to FIRM releases immediately, as the mandatory insurance requirement takes effect months later.
known_events: 2016: Houston-area FIRM updates post-Tax Day floods
2019: Miami-Dade FIRM modernization
2021: FEMA Risk Rating 2.0 nationwide repricing (Oct 1 effective)
2023: New York metro post-Ida FIRM revisions
implementation_notes: UIHC (Universal Insurance Holdings) appears delisted on yfinance -- replaced with PLMR (Palomar). HCI Group is the purest US private flood insurance play (Florida-focused). PLMR has growing flood book but is diversified across earthquake, crop, etc. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) FEMA FIRM releases are not easily machine-parseable, (b) the signal has 6-12 month lag from preliminary to effective, (c) stock reaction may be delayed. The 40 trading day hold is short for an 18-month fundamental catalyst.

name: EIA Refinery Utilization Below 85% During Driving Season -> Gasoline Crack Spread Rally -> Refiner Long
rule: Using yfinance and EIA data: When EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report shows US refinery utilization rate drops below 85% during May 1 through September 30 (peak driving season), go long an equal-weight basket of refiners (VLO, MPC, PSX) on the Wednesday following the EIA weekly report (released Wednesday 10:30 AM ET). Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. EIA WPSR provides refinery utilization as percent of operable capacity. FRED series: WGFCPUS2 (gasoline stocks) and refinery utilization data at eia.gov.
entry_conditions: EIA WPSR US refinery utilization rate < 85% (operable capacity basis)
Signal window: May 1 through September 30 (driving season only)
Enter on Wednesday afternoon after EIA report (or Thursday open if report is after market close)
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: VLO (Valero Energy), MPC (Marathon Petroleum), PSX (Phillips 66), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report (free): https://www.eia.gov/petroleum/supply/weekly/ - refinery utilization (% operable capacity), gasoline stocks, RBOB spot prices. FRED series: WPULFEUSA (refinery utilization), WGFCPUS2 (gasoline stocks days of supply). Weekly Wednesday 10:30 AM ET release.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each week (2000-2025) during May-September where EIA refinery utilization dropped below 85%, compute VLO+MPC+PSX basket return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Note: MPC IPO was June 2011, PSX IPO was May 2012. For pre-2012, use VLO only (public since 1997). Major events: 2005 (Hurricanes Katrina/Rita), 2008 (Hurricane Ike), 2017 (Hurricane Harvey), 2021 (Winter Storm Uri extended maintenance). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. bt_feasibility is 5 because EIA data is clean, free, machine-readable, weekly, and the refinery utilization-to-crack spread mechanism is well-established.
known_events: 2005 Sep: Katrina/Rita shut 25% of US refining, utilization crashed to ~70%
2008 Sep: Hurricane Ike shut Gulf Coast refineries
2017 Aug: Hurricane Harvey flooded Houston refining complex, utilization ~78%
2021 Feb: Winter Storm Uri caused extended outages into spring driving season
2022 Jun: Tight refinery capacity drove crack spreads to record highs
implementation_notes: This is one of the highest bt_feasibility signals (5/5) because EIA data is pristine, freely available, machine-readable, and the mechanism is direct and well-documented. VLO is the purest US independent refiner. MPC is the largest US refiner by capacity. PSX has refining plus midstream/chemicals (slightly diluted signal). The 85% threshold during driving season is a clear disruption signal -- normal driving season utilization is 92-95%. If too many events, raise threshold to 82% or require 2 consecutive weeks below 85%.

name: TSA Checkpoint Throughput YoY Record -> Airline Revenue Surge -> JETS Long
rule: Using yfinance and TSA checkpoint data: When TSA rolling 7-day average checkpoint throughput exceeds the same 7-day period in 2019 by > 10% for 3 consecutive weeks, go long JETS (US Global Jets ETF) on the Monday following the third qualifying week. Hold for 15 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. TSA publishes daily checkpoint throughput numbers at tsa.gov with 1-day lag. Compare to 2019 baseline (pre-COVID peak) to measure true structural growth beyond recovery.
entry_conditions: TSA 7-day rolling average throughput exceeds same-period 2019 by > 10%
Condition sustained for 3 consecutive weeks
Enter on Monday following the third qualifying week
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: JETS (US Global Jets ETF), UAL (United Airlines), DAL (Delta Air Lines), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: TSA daily checkpoint throughput (free): https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes - daily numbers with 2019 comparison. Historical daily data available via archived TSA releases and data repositories. Also BTS T-100 air carrier traffic data (free, monthly, with lag).

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2021-2025, post-COVID resumption) where TSA throughput exceeded 2019 same-period by >10% for 3+ consecutive weeks, compute JETS return over 15 trading days vs SPY. JETS ETF inception was April 2015, but signal only relevant from 2021+ when recovery began. Also test with UAL and DAL individually. Pre-2021 (2015-2019), test using absolute throughput growth >5% YoY as alternative signal. bt_feasibility is 5 because TSA data is daily, free, easily scrapeable, and the throughput-to-airline-revenue mechanism is direct.
known_events: 2022 Q2-Q3: TSA throughput first consistently exceeded 2019 levels
2023 summer: Record TSA throughput, airlines reported strong RASM
2024 summer: New record TSA throughput, airline earnings beats
implementation_notes: TSA data is one of the cleanest free datasets available -- daily frequency, 1-day lag, direct comparison to 2019 baseline. JETS ETF inception April 2015. The 2019 baseline comparison is superior to YoY because it eliminates COVID base effects. The 10% threshold and 3-week persistence filter out holiday spikes and capture sustained demand trends. For pre-COVID backtest extension, use BTS monthly enplanement data (free but 2-month lag). bt_feasibility is 5/5.

name: EPA AIM Act HFC Phase-down -> Refrigerant Price Spike -> HVAC Service Companies Long
rule: Using yfinance and EPA AIM Act schedule: Go long an equal-weight basket of HVAC distributors and service companies (WSO, CARR) 60 trading days before each scheduled EPA AIM Act HFC allowance step-down date. Hold for 60 trading days (through the step-down date). Benchmark vs SPY. EPA AIM Act step-down dates are legislatively mandated: 2024 (40% of baseline), 2029 (30%), 2034 (20%), 2036 (15%). The pre-step-down period sees refrigerant price spikes as the market hoards inventory ahead of supply reductions.
entry_conditions: Enter 60 trading days before scheduled EPA AIM Act HFC allowance step-down effective date
Step-down dates are legislatively fixed: Jan 1, 2024; Jan 1, 2029; Jan 1, 2034; Jan 1, 2036
Signal fires once per step-down event
exit_conditions: Hold for 60 trading days (approximately 12 weeks, through step-down date)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: WSO (Watsco Inc, largest US HVAC distributor), CARR (Carrier Global, HVAC manufacturer), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: EPA AIM Act allowance allocations (free): https://www.epa.gov/climate-hfcs-reduction - step-down schedule and company allowance allocations published annually. AHRI monthly equipment shipment statistics (partially free at ahrinet.org).

backtest_approach: Event study: Limited historical events since AIM Act was passed December 2020 and first step-down was January 2024. For the 2024 step-down, measure WSO+CARR performance in the 60 trading days leading up to Jan 1, 2024 vs SPY. Supplementary: test pre-AIM Act refrigerant price events (R-22 phase-out under Montreal Protocol, effective Jan 2020). Historical HFC price data may be available from ICIS or trade publications. Only 1-2 testable events currently. bt_feasibility is 3 due to very limited event history.
known_events: 2020 Jan 1: R-22 (HCFC) production phase-out completed under Montreal Protocol
2022: AIM Act initial allowance allocations published
2024 Jan 1: First AIM Act step-down to 40% of baseline
2029 Jan 1: Second major step-down to 30% of baseline (upcoming)
implementation_notes: WSO (Watsco) is the largest US HVAC distributor -- directly benefits from refrigerant price inflation (revenue growth) and system replacement acceleration (equipment sales). CARR (Carrier) manufactures HVAC equipment with next-gen refrigerants. The AIM Act schedule is legislatively mandated, making this a predictable catalyst calendar. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) only 1 AIM Act step-down has occurred (2024), (b) the R-22 phase-out provides only partial analogy, (c) HVAC stocks are affected by weather, housing, and macro factors beyond HFC policy. The R-22 precedent (2010-2020 gradual phase-out) can provide supplementary evidence.

name: NY Fed Geographic Credit Divergence -> Long Conservative Northeast Regional Banks
rule: Using yfinance and FRED/NY Fed data: When FRED consumer credit card delinquency rate (DRCCLACBS or CCLACBW027SBOG) rises >100bps YoY AND the spread between Sun Belt vs national delinquency widens (proxy: KRE regional bank ETF underperforms SPY by >15% over 60 trading days), go long equal-weight basket of MTB + FITB (conservative Northeast regional banks with lower credit risk profiles) on the next trading day. Hold for 63 trading days (approximately 3 months). Benchmark vs SPY. LONG-SIDE ONLY -- original idea had a short leg which is excluded.
entry_conditions: FRED consumer credit card delinquency rate rises >100bps YoY
KRE (regional bank ETF) underperforms SPY by >15% over trailing 60 trading days (proxy for geographic credit stress hitting weaker banks)
Enter on first trading day after both conditions are true
Minimum 120 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: MTB (M&T Bank Corp), FITB (Fifth Third Bancorp), KRE (SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: DRCCLACBS (delinquency rate on credit card loans, quarterly) or CCLACBW027SBOG (weekly). NY Fed Consumer Credit Panel (free quarterly at newyorkfed.org). Historical data back to 1991 for FRED delinquency series.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED credit card delinquency data (2003-2025 for MTB/FITB/KRE history). Identify quarters where delinquency rises >100bps YoY AND KRE underperforms SPY by >15% over 60 trading days. For each signal (120-day min gap), long MTB+FITB basket for 63 trading days vs SPY. The thesis is that credit-conservative Northeast banks (MTB: upstate NY/Mid-Atlantic, FITB: Ohio/Midwest) outperform during credit stress because their loan portfolios have better underwriting and lower geographic exposure to Sun Belt consumer credit deterioration. Key expected events: 2007-2008 (housing crisis), 2020 (COVID credit stress), 2023-2024 (post-stimulus credit normalization).
known_events: 2007 Q4-2008: Credit card delinquencies surged; Sun Belt banks took heavy losses on consumer portfolios
2009-2010: Northeast banks with conservative underwriting recovered faster than Sun Belt peers
2020 Q2-Q3: COVID-driven credit stress; geographic divergence in unemployment rates
2023 Q4-2024: Post-stimulus credit normalization; Sun Belt subprime auto/CC losses elevated
implementation_notes: MTB IPO 1980s, FITB IPO 1980s -- both have excellent backtest history. KRE ETF launched June 2006, limiting the proxy signal to 2006+. For pre-2006, can use a simple basket of large regional banks. The long-only modification preserves the thesis that conservative Northeast banks hold value during credit stress periods, without requiring the short-side leg. FRED delinquency data (DRCCLACBS) is quarterly with ~2 month lag; the KRE underperformance proxy provides a faster-moving real-time signal. bt_feasibility is 4 because FRED and yfinance data are clean.

name: BJS Prison Population Surge -> Private Prison Operator Revenue Growth -> CXW/GEO Long
rule: Using yfinance and BJS data: When BJS semiannual National Prisoner Statistics report shows total US incarcerated population (federal + state) growing > 3% YoY after at least 2 years of decline or flat growth, go long an equal-weight basket of private prison operators (CXW, GEO) on the first trading day after BJS report publication. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. BJS publishes semiannual prisoner statistics (typically June and December releases), freely available at bjs.gov.
entry_conditions: BJS National Prisoner Statistics shows total incarcerated population YoY growth > 3%
Prior 2 years showed declining or flat (<1% growth) prison population
Enter on first trading day after BJS report publication
Signal fires at most twice per year (semiannual reports)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: CXW (CoreCivic Inc), GEO (GEO Group Inc), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: BJS National Prisoner Statistics (free): https://bjs.gov/index.cfm?ty=nps - semiannual, published with ~6 month lag. Also BJS Annual Survey of Jails for shorter-term county jail data. Federal Bureau of Prisons population data available weekly at bop.gov (free) for more timely federal signal.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each BJS report (2005-2025) where incarcerated population inflected upward >3% after 2+ years of decline, compute CXW+GEO return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Prison population declined 2009-2019, then COVID caused temporary further drop 2020, followed by rebound 2021-2023. Expected events: possibly 2006-2007 (population growth period) and 2022-2023 (post-COVID rebound). With the decline prerequisite, expect 1-3 events. bt_feasibility is 3 due to: (a) semiannual frequency limits data points, (b) 6-month publication lag means market may already price in trends, (c) ESG concerns around private prisons add volatility.
known_events: 2007: US prison population peaked at 1.6M, CXW/GEO at cycle highs
2020: COVID caused 15% drop in federal prison population
2022-2023: Post-COVID prison population rebound began
2021: Biden executive order against federal private prison use (later narrowed)
implementation_notes: CXW converted from REIT back to C-corp in 2020 (tax structure change). GEO Group converted from REIT to C-corp in 2021. Both stocks carry significant political risk (Democratic administrations have been hostile to private prisons). The Biden 2021 executive order only applied to DOJ contracts, not ICE or state contracts. BOP.gov weekly population data (free) can provide more timely federal population signals than the lagged BJS reports. bt_feasibility is 3 due to political risk overlay and publication lag.

name: USGS Helium Production Decline -> Industrial Gas Shortage -> APD/LIN Premium Pricing
rule: Using yfinance and USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries: When USGS annual helium data shows US production declining > 10% YoY while global demand indicators (semiconductor fab capacity, MRI installations) remain positive, go long an equal-weight basket of industrial gas companies (APD, LIN) on the first trading day after USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries publication (typically late January). Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USGS MCS is the definitive free source for US helium production and consumption data.
entry_conditions: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries helium chapter shows US production declined > 10% YoY
Qualitative check: semiconductor fab capacity (SEMI data) growing, MRI install base stable
Enter on first trading day after USGS MCS publication (typically late January)
Signal fires at most once per year

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: APD (Air Products and Chemicals), LIN (Linde plc), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries - Helium (free): https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/helium-statistics-and-information - annual production, consumption, imports, and reserve data. BLM Federal Helium Program reports (free at blm.gov). Published annually in late January.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each USGS MCS release (2015-2025) where US helium production declined >10% YoY, compute APD+LIN return over 40 trading days vs SPY. The Federal Helium Reserve wind-down created production declines starting ~2019. LIN formed from Praxair/Linde merger in 2018, so pre-2018 use only APD. Expected events: 2019-2021 (Federal Reserve auction changes), 2023+ (continued wind-down). If <3 events at 10% threshold, relax to 5% YoY decline. Calculate average excess return.
known_events: 2013: Helium Stewardship Act reformed Federal Reserve operations
2019: Federal Helium Reserve began phase-out auctions
2021-2022: Global helium shortage as BLM supply declined and Amur plant delayed
2023: Qatar Helium 2 plant delayed, tightening global supply
implementation_notes: APD has long history (public since 1961). LIN in current form since 2018 Praxair/Linde merger (pre-2018 use PX for Praxair). Helium is a small but high-margin segment for both companies (~2-5% of revenue but higher margin). The Federal Helium Reserve wind-down is a known secular trend, so the surprise element comes from the pace of decline and coincident demand growth from semiconductor fabs. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) annual USGS data gives very few data points, (b) helium is a small contributor to APD/LIN overall financials, (c) the stock price reaction may be diluted by other segment dynamics.

name: CFTC COT Managed Money Net Short Extreme in Copper -> Contrarian Long COPX/FCX
rule: Using yfinance and CFTC COT data: When CFTC weekly Commitment of Traders report shows managed money net short positions in COMEX copper (HG) exceed the 90th percentile of the trailing 3-year distribution (i.e., extreme bearish positioning), go long an equal-weight basket of copper miners (COPX, FCX, SCCO) on the Monday following the Friday COT release. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. CFTC COT published every Friday at 3:30 PM ET for positions as of prior Tuesday. Free at cftc.gov.
entry_conditions: CFTC COT managed money net short in COMEX copper exceeds 90th percentile of trailing 3-year distribution
Net short = (short contracts - long contracts) for managed money category
Enter on Monday following Friday COT release
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: COPX (Global X Copper Miners ETF), FCX (Freeport-McMoRan), SCCO (Southern Copper), HG=F (COMEX copper futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: CFTC Commitment of Traders - Disaggregated Futures (free): https://www.cftc.gov/dea/futures/other_lf.htm - copper (COMEX) contract 085692. Fields: Managed Money Long, Managed Money Short. Weekly Friday 3:30 PM ET release for Tuesday positions.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each Friday COT release (2010-2025) where managed money net short in copper exceeded 90th percentile of trailing 3-year distribution, compute COPX+FCX+SCCO return over 20 trading days vs SPY. COPX inception April 2010. The contrarian thesis: extreme speculative shorts create a crowded trade vulnerable to short covering rallies when any positive catalyst appears (Chinese stimulus, supply disruption, dollar weakness). Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. Expect 8-15 events over the period. bt_feasibility is 5 because CFTC data is clean, free, weekly, machine-readable, and the contrarian positioning signal is well-defined.
known_events: 2015-2016: Extreme net shorts preceded copper rally on China stimulus
2018 Q4: Net short extreme preceded 2019 Q1 copper recovery
2020 March: COVID crash created extreme shorts, massive rally followed
2022 Q3: Net short extreme preceded late 2022 copper recovery
implementation_notes: CFTC COT data is one of the cleanest free datasets: machine-readable CSV, weekly, no API key needed. The 90th percentile threshold (rather than absolute level) automatically adjusts for secular changes in open interest. FCX is the largest publicly traded copper miner. SCCO is the most copper-pure major miner. COPX ETF provides diversified copper mining exposure. bt_feasibility is 5/5 -- this is a textbook quantifiable contrarian signal with excellent data quality.

name: BEA International Travel Receipts Surge -> Strong Dollar Tourism Spending -> Hotel REITs Long
rule: Using yfinance and BEA/NTTO data: When BEA quarterly international travel receipts (US exports of travel services) grow > 15% YoY, go long an equal-weight basket of hotel REITs (HST, PK, MAR) on the first trading day after BEA data release. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. BEA publishes travel receipts as part of quarterly balance of payments data (free). NTTO publishes monthly international visitor arrivals as a more timely leading indicator (free at travel.trade.gov).
entry_conditions: BEA quarterly international travel receipts YoY growth > 15%
Or: NTTO monthly international visitor arrivals YoY growth > 10% for 3 consecutive months (leading indicator)
Enter on first trading day after BEA quarterly release or NTTO monthly release
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: HST (Host Hotels & Resorts), PK (Park Hotels & Resorts), MAR (Marriott International), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: BEA International Transactions (free): Table 3.1 US International Trade in Services - travel receipts. FRED series: BOPTEXP (travel exports/receipts). Quarterly, ~75 days after quarter end. NTTO International Visitor Arrivals (free monthly): https://travel.trade.gov/view/m-2022-I-001/index.asp

backtest_approach: Event study: For each BEA quarterly release (2005-2025) where travel receipts grew >15% YoY, compute HST+PK+MAR return over 30 trading days vs SPY. PK spun off from Hilton in January 2017, so pre-2017 use HST+MAR only. Key periods of strong inbound travel growth: 2006-2007 (weak dollar attracted tourists), 2014-2015, 2022-2024 (post-COVID rebound). Also test NTTO monthly arrivals as leading indicator. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic. bt_feasibility is 4.
known_events: 2006-2007: Weak USD drove record international tourist arrivals to US
2014-2015: Strong US economy and events attracted international visitors
2022-2023: Post-COVID international travel recovery, pent-up demand
2024: Strong dollar paradoxically coincided with record inbound travel from wealthy tourists
implementation_notes: HST is the largest US lodging REIT (pure hotel ownership). PK owns high-end hotels (spun from HLT in 2017). MAR is the largest hotel company by rooms but is asset-light (franchise/management model) so RevPAR uplift flows differently. BEA travel receipts capture total foreign visitor spending (hotels, dining, shopping, transportation). The 15% YoY threshold captures significant acceleration beyond normal growth. NTTO monthly data provides a more timely signal than quarterly BEA but with less comprehensive coverage.

name: CFTC COT Managed Money Extreme Net Short Copper -> Contrarian Long COPX
rule: Using yfinance and CFTC COT data: When CFTC weekly COT report shows managed money net positioning in COMEX copper (legacy report, contract code 085692) falls below the 5th percentile of its trailing 260-week (5-year) range, go long COPX (copper miners ETF) on the next trading day after the Friday COT report. Hold for 21 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The contrarian thesis is that extreme speculative pessimism creates short-covering rally setups.
entry_conditions: CFTC COT managed money net position in COMEX copper falls below 5th percentile of trailing 260-week range
Equivalently: managed money net short exceeds 95th percentile of 5-year range
Enter on Monday after Friday COT report release
Minimum 42 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: COPX (Global X Copper Miners ETF, launched 2010), HG=F (COMEX copper futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: CFTC Commitments of Traders (COT) legacy report: copper-grade contract code 085692. Free weekly at cftc.gov/dea/futures/deacmxsf.htm. Historical data downloadable back to 1993. Managed money positioning available in disaggregated format from 2006.

backtest_approach: Event study: Download CFTC COT disaggregated report for copper (2010-2025, COPX inception). Compute managed money net position = long - short. Calculate rolling 260-week percentile. For each week where percentile < 5%, long COPX for 21 trading days. Apply 42-day minimum gap. Compute per-event return vs SPY. Key expected events: 2013 Q2 (China hard-landing fears), 2015 Q4 (global recession fears), 2018 Q3 (trade war escalation), 2020 Q1 (COVID crash), 2022 Q3 (China zero-COVID). COPX launched April 2010; for longer history, use FCX as copper proxy.
known_events: 2013 Q2: China hard-landing fears drove extreme copper shorts; copper bottomed and rallied 10%
2015 Nov-Dec: Global recession fears; copper hit $2.00/lb; rallied 20% to March 2016
2018 Aug-Sep: US-China trade war escalation; copper shorts at extreme; bottomed Sep 2018
2020 Mar: COVID crash drove extreme positioning; copper rallied 100%+ over next year
2022 Jul: China zero-COVID + global recession fears; copper positioning extremely negative
implementation_notes: CFTC COT data is one of the cleanest free fundamental datasets available. The disaggregated report (from 2006) separates managed money from producer/merchant and swap dealer positions. Managed money net short extremes have well-documented contrarian value across commodities (research by Bridgewater, JPM). COPX ETF launched April 2010 with 30+ copper mining stocks. For pre-2010, use FCX (Freeport-McMoRan) as a liquid copper proxy. bt_feasibility is 5 because COT data is machine-readable CSV with standardized format.

name: BEA International Travel Receipt Surge -> Long Gateway City Hotel REITs
rule: Using yfinance and BEA/FRED data: When BEA quarterly international travel receipts (FRED series B0230RC1Q027SBEA or IEABC) show YoY growth exceeding 15%, go long equal-weight basket of HST + PK on the first trading day after the BEA release. Hold for 63 trading days (approximately 3 months). Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that surging international visitor spending disproportionately benefits gateway city hotel REITs through RevPAR acceleration.
entry_conditions: BEA international travel receipts YoY growth exceeds 15% (quarterly data)
Enter on first trading day after BEA balance of payments quarterly release
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: HST (Host Hotels & Resorts), PK (Park Hotels & Resorts, spun from HLT 2017), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED/BEA: International travel receipts (B0230RC1Q027SBEA or similar series). Published quarterly as part of BEA balance of payments, approximately 3 months after quarter end. Historical data back to 1990s. NTTO monthly international visitor arrivals (free at travel.trade.gov) as supplementary.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED international travel receipts (1999-2025 for HST history). Compute quarterly YoY growth. Identify quarters where growth >15%. For each signal (90-day min gap), long HST+PK basket for 63 trading days vs SPY. PK spun from HLT in January 2017; pre-2017 use HST only or add MAR as gateway city proxy. Key expected events: 2004-2006 (post-9/11 tourism recovery), 2010-2011 (post-GFC tourism rebound), 2015 (strong dollar period), 2022-2023 (post-COVID international travel surge). Exclude COVID quarters (2020 Q2-Q3) as distortion.
known_events: 2004-2005: Post-9/11 international tourism recovery; hotel occupancy surged in NYC/Miami
2010-2011: Post-GFC tourism rebound; gateway city hotels led recovery
2015: Strong USD paradoxically boosted outbound but inbound travel receipts still grew on volume
2022 Q3-2023 Q2: Post-COVID international travel surge; gateway city RevPAR exceeded 2019 levels
2024: Continued strong international arrivals driven by pent-up demand and events
implementation_notes: HST IPO 1998, PK IPO Jan 2017. Pre-2017 use HST only. BEA travel receipts include both business and leisure; the signal is strongest when leisure international arrivals drive the growth (summer/holiday quarters). The 15% YoY threshold is high enough to filter out normal growth. NTTO monthly data can provide higher-frequency confirmation but is less clean than BEA. bt_feasibility is 4 because BEA/FRED data is clean and quarterly.

name: NERC High-Risk Grid Assessment + Summer Heat -> Long Gas Peaker IPPs
rule: Using yfinance data: When ERCOT/MISO summer reserve margins are projected below 15% (per NERC summer assessment, published May) AND summer begins (June 1), go long equal-weight basket of VST + NRG on June 1. Hold for 63 trading days (through mid-August peak demand). Benchmark vs SPY. As a backtest proxy: long VST+NRG each June 1 in years where the prior-year ERCOT summer peak demand set records or grid emergencies occurred (indicating structural tightness persists).
entry_conditions: NERC Summer Reliability Assessment (published May) identifies ERCOT or MISO as high-risk with reserve margin <15%
Enter on June 1 (or first trading day of June) each qualifying year
Backtest proxy: ERCOT prior summer had peak demand >90th percentile of history OR grid emergency event
exit_conditions: Hold for 63 trading days (approximately through mid-August)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: VST (Vistra Corp, ticker changed from VST after Dynegy merger 2018), NRG (NRG Energy), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NERC Long-Term and Seasonal Reliability Assessments (free at nerc.com). ERCOT Capacity, Demand, and Reserves report (free at ercot.com). EIA Electric Grid Monitor for real-time demand (free). Historical ERCOT peak demand data back to 2001.

backtest_approach: Event study: Identify summers (June-August) where grid stress indicators are elevated. For years 2018-2025 (VST post-Dynegy merger): long VST+NRG basket from June 1 for 63 trading days. Pre-2018: use NRG only. Signal years (NERC high-risk or actual grid stress): 2011 (ERCOT rolling blackouts), 2019 (ERCOT emergency), 2021 (post-winter-storm heightened awareness), 2022 (record heat), 2023 (record demand), 2024 (continued growth). Compare signal-year returns vs non-signal-year returns. Also test unconditional summer seasonality of VST+NRG.
known_events: 2011: ERCOT rolling blackouts during February cold + summer heat wave; scarcity pricing events
2019 Aug: ERCOT prices hit $9,000/MWh cap during August heat wave; VST benefited significantly
2021: Post-Winter-Storm-Uri heightened grid risk awareness; NERC flagged multiple regions
2022: Record heat across US; ERCOT hit record demand; gas peakers ran at high capacity factors
2023: Record ERCOT demand; VST stock tripled in 2023 partly on grid pricing power narrative
2024: Continued AI/data center load growth tightened ERCOT reserves further
implementation_notes: VST was formed from Vistra Energy (formerly TXU) and merged with Dynegy in April 2018. Pre-2018 NRG is the main liquid pure-play gas/power IPP. VST has ERCOT-concentrated generation (~40GW) making it highly leveraged to Texas grid stress. NRG has a more diversified fleet but still benefits from scarcity pricing. The NERC biannual assessment provides a structural setup (May publication for summer risk), while actual grid events provide the returns. bt_feasibility is 4 because the ERCOT data and price history are available, though the NERC assessment requires manual reading.

name: USDA APHIS Livestock Disease Outbreak -> Veterinary Pharmaceutical Demand Surge -> IDXX/ZTS Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA APHIS data: When USDA APHIS confirms a high-consequence livestock disease outbreak (HPAI in commercial poultry >5M birds depopulated in 30 days, OR first US detection of African Swine Fever/Foot-and-Mouth Disease), go long an equal-weight basket of veterinary diagnostics/pharma (IDXX, ZTS) on the next trading day. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. APHIS publishes confirmed disease detections in real-time at aphis.usda.gov. Focus on OIE-notifiable diseases that trigger mandatory surveillance testing.
entry_conditions: USDA APHIS confirms HPAI depopulations > 5M commercial birds in rolling 30 days
OR: USDA APHIS confirms first US detection of African Swine Fever or Foot-and-Mouth Disease
Enter on next trading day after APHIS confirmation
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: IDXX (IDEXX Laboratories, vet diagnostics leader), ZTS (Zoetis, animal health pharmaceuticals), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA APHIS animal health situation reports (free): https://www.aphis.usda.gov/livestock-poultry-disease - HPAI detections table with flock size and date. Also WOAH (OIE) WAHIS notification system (free) for international disease status.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each APHIS-confirmed major livestock disease outbreak (2015-2025), compute IDXX+ZTS return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Key events: 2015 HPAI (49M birds), 2022 HPAI (58M birds), 2024-2025 HPAI (ongoing). No US ASF or FMD detections to date (these would be unprecedented events). With 90-day gap, expect 3-5 discrete HPAI events. Calculate average excess return, win rate. IDXX benefits from diagnostic testing volume surge; ZTS benefits from vaccine/therapeutic demand.
known_events: 2015 spring: HPAI H5N2 depopulated 49M birds, IDXX saw livestock testing surge
2022 Q1-Q2: HPAI H5N1 first wave, 38M commercial birds
2022 Q4-2023 Q1: HPAI second wave, mandatory surveillance expanded
2024-2025: Ongoing HPAI in commercial poultry and dairy cattle
implementation_notes: IDXX is the dominant veterinary diagnostics company (lab instruments + consumables). When mandatory disease surveillance is triggered, IDXX sees elevated consumable pull-through on its installed instrument base -- very high incremental margins. ZTS (animal vaccines/therapeutics) benefits from both prophylactic vaccination demand and therapeutic treatment. The HPAI 5M threshold ensures we capture only major outbreaks. bt_feasibility is 4 because APHIS data is real-time, clean, and the diagnostic testing surge mechanism is direct and well-documented.

name: Port of LA/LB Container Dwell Time Spike -> Chassis Shortage -> Intermodal Equipment Leasing Long
rule: Using yfinance and Port of LA Signal data: When Port of LA Signal daily data shows average import container dwell time exceeds 8 days (normal is 3-4 days) for 2 consecutive weeks, go long an equal-weight basket of equipment leasing companies (R, GATX) on the Monday following the second qualifying week. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Port of LA Signal (signal.portoflosangeles.org) publishes daily container dwell time, chassis availability, and vessel queue data for free.
entry_conditions: Port of LA Signal average import container dwell time > 8 days for 2 consecutive weeks
Enter on Monday following the second qualifying week
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: R (Ryder System, truck/chassis leasing), GATX (GATX Corp, railcar/intermodal leasing), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: Port of LA Signal (free): https://signal.portoflosangeles.org - daily import container dwell time, chassis turn time, truck turn time. Port of Long Beach monthly stats (free). Also Marine Exchange of Southern California vessel queue data.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2018-2025, Port of LA Signal launched ~2017) where import container dwell time exceeded 8 days for 2+ consecutive weeks, compute R+GATX return over 30 trading days vs SPY. Key events: 2021 Q4-2022 Q2 (historic port congestion, dwell times exceeded 10-12 days), 2023 periodic spikes. The 2021-2022 supply chain crisis is the primary backtesting event. If only 1-2 events at 8 days, relax to 6 days. Calculate average excess return.
known_events: 2021 Q3-2022 Q1: Historic port congestion, dwell times peaked at 12+ days
2021 October: 100+ container ships at anchor off LA/LB
2022 Q2: Dwell times normalized as demand slowed and congestion cleared
2023: Periodic minor congestion spikes during peak import seasons
implementation_notes: Port of LA Signal data is excellent quality but limited history (started ~2017). R (Ryder) benefits from chassis/truck leasing demand during congestion. GATX benefits from intermodal container leasing. TRTN (Triton International) was mentioned in idea but was acquired by Brookfield in 2024 and delisted. The 2021-2022 congestion crisis is the primary event but is somewhat sui generis (COVID supply chain disruption). bt_feasibility is 3 due to limited history and potentially non-repeatable 2021 conditions.

name: USDA APHIS High-Consequence Livestock Disease Outbreak -> Long Vet Diagnostics/Pharma
rule: Using yfinance data: When USDA APHIS confirms a high-consequence livestock disease outbreak (HPAI in commercial poultry reaching >10M birds affected, bovine TB cluster, or novel disease detection), go long equal-weight basket of IDXX + ZTS on the next trading day. Hold for 21 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. For backtest: hand-code major USDA APHIS outbreak events from press releases. HPAI is the primary signal with 2015 and 2022-2024 being major outbreaks.
entry_conditions: USDA APHIS confirms high-consequence livestock disease outbreak with cumulative flock/herd depopulation exceeding meaningful threshold (>10M birds for HPAI, >100 premises for cattle diseases)
Enter on next trading day after APHIS situation report shows threshold exceeded
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: IDXX (IDEXX Laboratories), ZTS (Zoetis Inc, IPO Feb 2013), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA APHIS HPAI situation reports (free at aphis.usda.gov/livestock-poultry-disease/avian/avian-influenza/hpai-detections). Hand-coded outbreak dates for backtest. Major events: 2015 HPAI (48M+ birds), 2022-2024 HPAI (82M+ birds). For pre-ZTS-IPO testing, use IDXX only.

backtest_approach: Event study with hand-coded dates: Identify dates when USDA APHIS HPAI cumulative affected birds crossed key thresholds. Major event entries: (1) 2015-04-15 (HPAI crosses 10M birds, Upper Midwest outbreak), (2) 2022-03-01 (2022 HPAI first commercial flock detections), (3) 2022-10-15 (HPAI crosses 50M birds, egg layer impact), (4) 2024-04-01 (HPAI detected in dairy cattle, novel host). Long IDXX+ZTS for 21 trading days per event vs SPY. Compute per-event returns. The mechanism: HPAI outbreaks drive mandatory surveillance testing (IDXX diagnostic instruments/consumables), increased biosecurity spending, and prophylactic vaccination programs (ZTS vaccines/medications).
known_events: 2015 Apr-Jun: HPAI H5N2 devastated Midwest poultry, 48M birds depopulated; largest US animal disease event
2022 Feb-Dec: HPAI H5N1 spread across 47 states; 58M birds affected by year-end
2023: Continued HPAI detections including wild birds and backyard flocks; ongoing surveillance
2024 Mar-: HPAI H5N1 detected in US dairy cattle herds (novel host); mandatory testing ordered for interstate cattle movement
implementation_notes: ZTS IPO was February 2013, so pre-2013 events use IDXX only. IDXX has traded since 1991 with excellent liquidity. The 2015 HPAI outbreak and 2022-2024 HPAI are the major backtest events. The 2024 dairy cattle HPAI detection is particularly significant because it expanded the addressable diagnostics market to cattle (ZTS cattle vaccine portfolio, IDXX bovine testing). bt_feasibility is 4 because the outbreak events are well-documented in APHIS press releases and the stocks are liquid.

name: Census BFS High-Propensity Business Formation Surge -> Long Small-Biz Enablers
rule: Using yfinance and Census BFS data: When Census weekly Business Formation Statistics high-propensity applications (FRED series BAHPITOTALSAUS or Census BFS data) exceed their trailing 260-week (5-year) 80th percentile for 4+ consecutive weeks, go long equal-weight basket of PAYX + SQ on the first trading day after the 4th consecutive qualifying week. Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that surging new business formation drives demand for payroll processing and payment solutions.
entry_conditions: Census BFS high-propensity business applications exceed 80th percentile of trailing 260-week range
Condition persists for 4+ consecutive weeks
Enter on first trading day after 4th consecutive qualifying week
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: PAYX (Paychex Inc), SQ (Block Inc, IPO Nov 2015), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: Census Bureau Business Formation Statistics (free weekly at census.gov/econ/bfs). FRED series: BAHPITOTALSAUS (Business Applications: High Propensity, Total for US, Weekly, SA). Data available from 2006. Published with 1-week lag.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load Census BFS high-propensity series from FRED (2006-2025). Compute rolling 260-week 80th percentile. Identify periods where 4+ consecutive weeks exceed this threshold. For each signal (90-day min gap), long PAYX+SQ basket for 63 trading days vs SPY. SQ IPO Nov 2015; pre-2015 use PAYX+INTU (Intuit, small-business accounting software). Key expected signal periods: 2006-2007 (pre-GFC formation boom), 2013-2014 (post-GFC recovery), 2020 Q3-2021 Q2 (COVID-era business formation explosion), 2024 (continued elevated formation rates). The COVID-era surge (2020 Q3+) is the strongest test as formation rates hit all-time highs.
known_events: 2006-2007: Pre-financial-crisis small business formation peaked, especially in real estate services
2013-2014: Post-GFC recovery drove steady formation growth; PAYX new client wins accelerated
2020 Q3-2021: Explosive business formation surge (EIN apps doubled); COVID-era entrepreneurship boom
2021 Q1-Q2: High-propensity apps at unprecedented levels; SQ and PAYX hit record new client acquisition
2024: Formation rates remain elevated 30%+ above pre-COVID trend; structural shift in entrepreneurship
implementation_notes: Census BFS data on FRED is clean, weekly, and seasonally adjusted. The high-propensity series filters out shell companies and tax entities to focus on likely employer businesses. PAYX has been public since 1983 and is the purest small-business payroll play. SQ (Block) IPO was Nov 2015; for longer history, pair PAYX with INTU or FIS as small-business payment enabler proxy. The 80th percentile threshold on a 5-year lookback adapts to the structural upshift in formation rates post-COVID. bt_feasibility is 4 because FRED/Census data is excellent.

name: USDA Dairy Export Sales Surge to SE Asia -> NDM Price Recovery -> Dairy Processor Long
rule: Using yfinance and USDA FAS data: When USDA weekly dairy Export Sales Report shows nonfat dry milk (NDM) net sales exceed 4-week trailing average by > 40%, go long DAR (Darling Ingredients) on the Friday following the Thursday report. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USDA FAS publishes weekly dairy export sales alongside grain commodities. NDM is the largest US dairy export by volume.
entry_conditions: USDA weekly dairy Export Sales shows NDM net sales exceed 4-week average by > 40%
Enter on Friday following Thursday report
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: DAR (Darling Ingredients, rendering/dairy ingredients), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA FAS weekly dairy export sales (free): https://apps.fas.usda.gov/export-sales/ - filter for nonfat dry milk. Also CME NDM spot price for context.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each Thursday report (2015-2025) where NDM export sales spiked >40% above 4-week average, compute DAR return over 30 trading days vs SPY. DAR has rendering and dairy ingredients segments. If LNDC (Landec) is available, add as alternative. Calculate average excess return, win rate.
known_events: 2016: SE Asia NDM import surge
2020: Post-COVID dairy demand recovery
2023-2024: NDM price cycle trough and recovery
implementation_notes: LNDC may not be the best proxy (diversified). DAR is a better play on dairy ingredient pricing through its rendering segment. bt_feasibility is 3 due to dairy being a small part of DAR and noisy FAS data.

name: USGS NdPr Oxide Price Spike -> EV Motor Cost Inflation -> RE-Free Motor Supplier Long
rule: Using yfinance and USGS/rare earth price data: When NdPr oxide prices (tracked by USGS and free price indices) spike > 50% from their 6-month moving average, go long BWA (BorgWarner, RE-free motor supplier) on the next trading day. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. As a cross-check, also monitor MP (MP Materials, rare earth miner) which benefits directly from price spikes. The thesis is that rare earth price spikes accelerate OEM adoption of BWA's ferrite motor alternatives.
entry_conditions: NdPr oxide price exceeds 6-month moving average by > 50%
Use USGS monthly data or free rare earth price indices
Enter on next trading day after monthly data confirms threshold
Minimum 120 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: BWA (BorgWarner), MP (MP Materials, rare earth miner), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries rare earths (free annual). Monthly rare earth prices available from Shanghai Metals Market or Asian Metal (some free data). USGS provides annual average prices in MCS.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2015-2025) where NdPr prices spiked >50% above 6-month MA, compute BWA return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Key events: 2017 (China consolidation spike), 2021-2022 (EV demand spike + China export controls). MP Materials IPO was 2020. BWA has long history. NdPr price data may need to be sourced from industry publications or USGS annual data. Calculate average excess return.
known_events: 2011: China rare earth export restriction, NdPr spiked 10x
2017: China environmental inspections spiked NdPr 100%
2021-2022: EV demand drove NdPr to $150/kg from $50/kg
implementation_notes: bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) monthly NdPr prices not easily available for free (USGS only provides annual averages), (b) BWA's RE-free motor revenue is still early-stage, (c) the causal chain has significant lag. MP Materials benefits directly from higher NdPr prices as a rare earth producer. BWA benefits indirectly as OEMs accelerate RE-free adoption.

name: USACE Inland Waterway Tonnage Surge -> Barge Operator Revenue Beat -> KEX Long
rule: Using yfinance and USACE data: When USACE Waterborne Commerce monthly data shows total US inland waterway tonnage growing > 5% YoY, go long KEX (Kirby Corporation, largest US inland tank barge operator) on the first trading day after USACE data release. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USACE publishes monthly tonnage data at iwr.usace.army.mil with ~3 month lag. Kirby operates ~25% of all US inland tank barges and sees direct revenue uplift from tonnage growth.
entry_conditions: USACE monthly inland waterway tonnage YoY growth > 5%
Enter on first trading day after USACE monthly data release (~3 month lag)
Minimum 60 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USACE Waterborne Commerce Statistics Center (free): https://iwr.usace.army.mil/wcs - monthly inland waterway tonnage by commodity and waterway system. Also USDA Grain Transportation Report weekly barge rates (free) for pricing context.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2005-2025) where inland tonnage grew >5% YoY, compute KEX return over 30 trading days vs SPY. KEX has been public since 1986. Inland waterway tonnage is cyclical, driven by grain harvests, petrochemical production, and coal movements. Expected 5-10 qualifying events per year during growth periods. Calculate average excess return, win rate, t-statistic.
known_events: 2007-2008: Strong commodity demand drove tonnage highs
2014-2015: Petrochemical boom lifted inland barge volumes
2021-2022: Post-COVID industrial recovery boosted waterway traffic
implementation_notes: KEX is the purest publicly traded inland waterway play in the US. USACE data has ~3 month lag which reduces timeliness but also means the market may not have fully processed the information. bt_feasibility is 4 because USACE data is clean, free, monthly, and KEX revenue directly correlates with inland waterway utilization.

name: USASpending DOD Contract Obligations Acceleration -> Defense Subcontractor Revenue Growth -> KTOS/MRCY Long
rule: Using yfinance and USASpending.gov data: When rolling 3-month DOD contract obligations in NAICS 334511 (Search, Detection, Navigation Instruments) or 336411 (Aircraft Manufacturing) exceed prior 3-month period by > 20%, go long an equal-weight basket of defense subcontractors (KTOS, CW) on the next trading day. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USASpending.gov provides real-time federal contract obligation data (free API, no key needed for public data).
entry_conditions: USASpending.gov rolling 3-month DOD obligations in NAICS 334511 or 336411 exceed prior 3-month by > 20%
Enter on next trading day after monthly data confirms threshold
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: KTOS (Kratos Defense, drones/radar), CW (Curtiss-Wright, defense electronics), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USASpending.gov API (free): https://api.usaspending.gov/ - filter by awarding_agency=DOD, naics_code=334511 or 336411, obligation amounts. Real-time updates. Also FPDS.gov for detailed contract-level data.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2015-2025) where DOD obligations in target NAICS codes accelerated >20% over rolling 3-month vs prior 3-month, compute KTOS+CW return over 30 trading days vs SPY. USASpending.gov data available from FY2008. KTOS IPO was 2007, CW has long history. Defense spending is lumpy (fiscal year-end spending surges in Aug-Sep). Calculate average excess return, win rate. MRCY (Mercury Systems) was mentioned but has been under SEC scrutiny -- replaced with CW.
known_events: 2018: National Defense Strategy increased procurement budgets
2022-2023: Ukraine supplementals accelerated DOD obligations
2024-2025: Pacific Deterrence Initiative spending acceleration
implementation_notes: USASpending.gov API is free, real-time, and well-documented -- excellent bt_feasibility. KTOS is a pure-play defense technology company (tactical drones, radar, satellite comm).
