CW (Curtiss-Wright) makes defense electronics and nuclear components. MRCY was originally in the idea but replaced with CW due to MRCY's accounting issues. The 20% acceleration threshold captures meaningful procurement surges, not normal quarterly fluctuation.

name: USDA Grain Barge Rate Surge -> Long Inland Barge Operator KEX
rule: Using yfinance and USDA data: When USDA weekly grain transportation report shows Illinois River barge rates exceeding 400% of tariff (vs normal 200-300%) for 3+ consecutive weeks, go long KEX on the next trading day. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The USDA grain barge rate is the timely proxy for USACE tonnage data which has significant publication lag. High barge rates signal tight inland waterway capacity benefiting Kirby Corp (KEX), the dominant inland tank barge operator.
entry_conditions: USDA weekly grain transportation report shows Illinois River/Lower Mississippi barge rates > 400% of tariff for 3+ consecutive weeks
Alternatively: yfinance-backtestable proxy -- if KEX is above its 200-day MA while energy sector (XLE) is below its 200-day MA (captures barge tightness from non-oil demand)
Enter on first trading day after 3rd consecutive qualifying week
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: KEX (Kirby Corporation), XLE (Energy Select Sector SPDR), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA AMS Grain Transportation Report -- weekly barge rate data (free at usda.gov/topics/farming/crop-production). USACE Waterborne Commerce Statistics (free but lagged). USDA grain barge rates available weekly with 1-week lag, data back to 2001.

backtest_approach: Event study: For the yfinance-backtestable version, use KEX price momentum relative to XLE as a proxy for barge market tightness (KEX outperforms when non-oil waterway traffic surges). Alternative: hand-code periods of extreme barge rates from USDA grain transport archives: fall 2007 (record grain harvest), fall 2012 (Mississippi River drought reduced available barges), fall 2014 (record corn/soy harvest), fall 2021 (supply chain disruption), fall 2022 (Mississippi River drought). Long KEX for 42 trading days per event vs SPY.
known_events: 2007 Oct: Record grain harvest drove barge rates to 600% of tariff; KEX rallied
2012 Jul-Nov: Mississippi River drought caused draft restrictions; available barge capacity fell 30%, rates spiked
2014 Oct-Nov: Record corn/soy harvest created barge demand surge; rates hit 500%+
2021 Q4: Supply chain disruption caused barge utilization to hit multi-year highs
2022 Oct: Mississippi River drought worst in decades; barge rates hit 1800% of tariff
implementation_notes: KEX has been public since 1988 with excellent liquidity. KEX operates ~1,000 inland tank barges (liquid cargo) and ~225 dry cargo barges on US inland waterways. The company is the dominant player with ~30% market share in inland tank barge transport. USDA grain barge rate data is the most timely free signal for waterway capacity utilization. bt_feasibility is 4 because the hand-coded events are well-documented in USDA reports.

name: USASpending DOD Contract Acceleration -> Long Defense Subcontractors
rule: Using yfinance data: When the ITA Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA) outperforms SPY by >10% over trailing 60 trading days AND total DOD contract obligations (available at USASpending.gov) show YoY growth >15%, go long equal-weight basket of KTOS + AXON on the next trading day. Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The backtest proxy uses ITA outperformance as a real-time signal for DOD spending acceleration, since prime contractor stock prices reflect contract award announcements before USASpending data updates.
entry_conditions: ITA (iShares US Aerospace & Defense ETF) outperforms SPY by >10% over trailing 60 trading days
This signals DOD spending acceleration flowing to prime contractors first
Enter on next trading day after condition met
Minimum 120 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: KTOS (Kratos Defense & Security, drone/unmanned systems), AXON (Axon Enterprise, law enforcement tech/defense), ITA (iShares US Aerospace & Defense), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USASpending.gov DOD contract obligations by NAICS (free, real-time). FPDS for detailed contract data. Data back to 2008+.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load ITA and SPY daily prices (2006-2025, ITA inception May 2006). Compute rolling 60-day ITA excess return vs SPY. Identify dates where excess return crosses +10%. For each signal (120-day min gap), long KTOS+AXON basket for 63 trading days vs SPY. KTOS IPO 2000, AXON IPO 2001. Key expected events: 2010-2011 (GWOT peak spending), 2017-2018 (Trump defense buildup), 2022-2023 (Ukraine/Pacific pivot spending surge), 2025 (DOGE-driven contract reclassification/acceleration).
known_events: 2007-2008: Iraq/Afghanistan surge spending drove DOD obligations to record levels
2017-2018: Bipartisan Budget Act lifted sequester caps; defense spending surged 10%+
2022 Q2-Q3: Ukraine conflict drove supplemental defense spending and accelerated procurement
2023: NDAA authorized record $886B; DOD contract obligations accelerated across NAICS codes
2025: Post-DOGE spending focus on unmanned systems and AI defense applications
implementation_notes: KTOS is a pure-play defense subcontractor (drones, missile defense, hypersonics) with high beta to DOD spending. AXON has expanded from law enforcement into DOD applications. ITA ETF includes primes (LMT, RTX, NOC) whose stock prices react first to contract awards, providing a leading signal for subcontractor revenue flow-through. The 120-day minimum gap prevents overtrading during sustained defense spending cycles. bt_feasibility is 4 because yfinance data is clean and ITA provides a good proxy.

name: ITC Affirmative Solar AD/CVD Determination -> Long First Solar
rule: Using yfinance data: When USITC issues an affirmative preliminary or final injury determination in a solar panel AD/CVD case (hand-coded event dates), go long FSLR on the next trading day. Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. FSLR manufactures cadmium telluride (CdTe) panels in the US, making it exempt from AD/CVD duties on crystalline silicon imports. Each affirmative determination creates a price floor that benefits FSLR competitively.
entry_conditions: USITC issues affirmative preliminary or final injury determination in a crystalline silicon solar AD/CVD case
Hand-coded event dates from USITC case history
Enter on next trading day after USITC announcement
If preliminary and final are within 90 days, take only the first signal

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: FSLR (First Solar Inc, IPO Nov 2006), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USITC AD/CVD case determinations (free at usitc.gov). Hand-coded event dates for solar trade cases.

backtest_approach: Event study with hand-coded dates: (1) 2012-10-10: USITC final affirmative on China c-Si cells (Inv. 701-TA-481/731-TA-1190), (2) 2015-01-21: USITC final affirmative on China/Taiwan c-Si panels (Inv. 701-TA-511/731-TA-1246-1247), (3) 2018-01-22: Section 201 safeguard tariff announcement (30% tariff on all imported solar cells), (4) 2024-05-16: Commerce preliminary AD/CVD on SE Asian (Cambodia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam) solar cells, (5) 2024-12-02: USITC preliminary affirmative on SE Asian solar. Long FSLR for 63 trading days per event vs SPY. Compute per-event excess returns.
known_events: 2012: First China solar AD/CVD case; duties of 24-36% imposed on Chinese c-Si cells
2015: Extended AD/CVD to Taiwan-made cells assembled in China; closed circumvention loophole
2018 Jan: Section 201 safeguard imposed 30% tariff on ALL imported solar cells/modules
2024 May: Commerce preliminary duties of 14-292% on SE Asian c-Si solar (AD) + CVD
2024 Dec: USITC preliminary affirmative injury from SE Asian solar imports; duties extended
implementation_notes: FSLR IPO was November 2006; all major solar trade cases occur within the backtest window. FSLR is unique because it uses CdTe thin-film technology, NOT crystalline silicon, so it is exempt from ALL c-Si AD/CVD duties. This means every trade enforcement action on c-Si imports directly benefits FSLR's competitive position. MAXN (Maxeon) could be added but it only recently became US-listed and has limited liquidity. The Section 201 case (2018) is different from AD/CVD but the mechanism is identical. bt_feasibility is 4 because the trade case dates are well-documented and FSLR is highly liquid.

name: NOAA Strong El Nino Signal -> Long Cocoa Futures via NIB
rule: Using yfinance and NOAA data: When NOAA CPC Oceanic Nino Index (ONI) crosses +1.5 (strong El Nino threshold), go long NIB (iPath Bloomberg Cocoa ETN) or CC=F (cocoa futures) on the first trading day of the following month. Hold for 126 trading days (approximately 6 months). Benchmark vs SPY. LONG-SIDE ONLY -- original idea included a short equity leg (HSY/MDLZ) which is excluded. The thesis is that strong El Nino events reduce rainfall in West Africa cocoa belt, cutting global supply 10-25% and driving cocoa prices up 30-80%.
entry_conditions: NOAA CPC ONI (3-month running mean SST anomaly for Nino 3.4 region) crosses above +1.5
Enter on first trading day of the month following the ONI crossing
Only trigger once per El Nino episode

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: NIB (iPath Bloomberg Cocoa ETN), CC=F (COMEX cocoa futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NOAA CPC ONI data (free monthly at cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/analysis_monitoring/ensostuff/ONI_v5.php). ONI data back to 1950. Published monthly with ~1 month lag.

backtest_approach: Event study: Identify months where ONI crossed +1.5 (strong El Nino onset): (1) 1997-05 (ONI +1.5, super El Nino), (2) 2009-07 (ONI +1.5), (3) 2015-04 (ONI +1.5, strong El Nino), (4) 2023-06 (ONI +1.5, strong El Nino). For each event, long CC=F for 126 trading days. NIB ETN launched 2008; use CC=F for full history. Cocoa data on yfinance (CC=F) back to 2000+. Compute per-event return and aggregate metrics. The El Nino/West Africa rainfall correlation is well-established in climate science (Ropelewski & Halpert 1987). The 2023-2024 El Nino drove cocoa to all-time highs above $10,000/ton.
known_events: 1997-1998: Super El Nino; cocoa rallied ~40% over 6 months from signal
2009-2010: Moderate El Nino; cocoa rallied ~30% as West Africa crop fell short
2015-2016: Strong El Nino; cocoa rallied ~25% but reversed sharply in late 2016 as El Nino faded
2023-2024: Strong El Nino + structural supply deficit; cocoa tripled from ~$2,500 to >$10,000/ton
implementation_notes: ONI data is the standard ENSO classification used by NOAA. The +1.5 threshold corresponds to 'strong' El Nino, which occurs roughly every 10-15 years. With only 4 events since 1997, statistical power is limited, but the effect size is large and the climate mechanism is well-established in peer-reviewed literature. NIB ETN has tracking error and liquidity issues; CC=F is preferable for backtest. The 2023-2024 event is exceptional because it combined El Nino with structural underinvestment in West African cocoa farming. bt_feasibility is 4 because ONI data is clean and CC=F is liquid on yfinance.

name: BLS ECI Benefits Cost Deceleration -> Long SPY on Margin Expansion Signal
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When BLS Employment Cost Index benefits component (FRED series ECIBEN) shows quarterly YoY growth decelerating by >100bps from the prior quarter (e.g., from 5.0% YoY to 3.8% YoY), go long SPY on the first trading day after the ECI release. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs long-term SPY buy-and-hold. The thesis is that benefits cost deceleration signals corporate margin expansion without consumer income destruction.
entry_conditions: BLS ECI benefits component (ECIBEN) quarterly YoY growth decelerates by >100bps from prior quarter
Wages component (ECIWAG) remains positive YoY (no wage deflation -- consumer spending intact)
Enter on first trading day after BLS ECI quarterly release (last business day of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct)
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: ECIBEN (ECI benefits component, quarterly SA), ECIWAG (ECI wages and salaries component, quarterly SA). Published quarterly on last business day of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct, approximately 1 month after quarter end. Historical data back to 2001.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED ECI benefits (ECIBEN) and wages (ECIWAG) quarterly data (2001-2025). Compute YoY growth each quarter. Identify quarters where benefits YoY decelerates >100bps from prior quarter while wages remain positive YoY. For each signal (90-day min gap), long SPY for 42 trading days. Compare per-signal returns vs unconditional SPY 42-day returns. Key expected signal quarters: 2003 Q4 (post-recession benefits cost moderation), 2010-2011 (ACA implementation changed benefits cost structure), 2017 (low health insurance inflation), 2024 Q2-Q3 (healthcare cost deceleration after COVID surge).
known_events: 2003 Q4-2004: Post-recession benefits cost moderation enabled corporate margin expansion
2010-2011: ACA implementation and recession aftermath lowered benefits cost growth temporarily
2017: Health insurance premium growth slowed after ACA marketplace stabilization
2020 Q2-Q3: COVID reduced healthcare utilization, temporarily lowering benefits costs
2024 Q2-Q3: Healthcare cost inflation moderated from post-COVID surge, easing employer benefit costs
implementation_notes: BLS ECI is considered the gold standard for labor cost measurement because it controls for compositional shifts (unlike average hourly earnings). The benefits component is ~30% of total compensation and is dominated by healthcare insurance costs. Benefits cost deceleration is a cleaner margin expansion signal than wage deceleration because it does not impair consumer spending. FRED ECIBEN quarterly data starts 2001 Q2. bt_feasibility is 4 because FRED data is clean and quarterly. The main risk is that benefits cost deceleration may coincide with economic weakness, so the wages filter (must remain positive) guards against recession signals.

Case involves major import source (China, Vietnam, Thailand, Cambodia, Malaysia)

ITC determinations are publicly announced and well-covered by trade press. MAXN (Maxeon Solar) is mentioned in idea but is a Singapore-based panel maker that could be hurt by AD/CVD, so excluded. bt_feasibility is 4 because ITC dates are precise, FSLR is a clear beneficiary, and the mechanism is direct.

name: MBA Mortgage Forbearance Exit Rate Spike -> Loan Modification Wave -> Mortgage Servicer Revenue Long
rule: Using yfinance and MBA data: When MBA weekly forbearance survey shows forbearance exit rate (share of loans exiting forbearance) spikes > 5% of total forborne loans in a single week while total forbearance share is still elevated (> 2%), go long an equal-weight basket of mortgage servicers (PFSI, COOP) on the Monday following the MBA report. Large forbearance exits generate modification fees and re-performing loan sale premiums for servicers.

Total forbearance share still elevated (> 2% of servicing portfolio)

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: MBA weekly forbearance and call volume survey (free summary at mba.org). Detailed data for members. MBA publishes weekly forbearance share, exits as % of prior week, and workout types.

Pre-COVID forbearance was minimal so limited history. Calculate average excess return, win rate.

The signal is most relevant during economic stress periods when forbearance programs are activated.

name: EIA Natural Gas Industrial Consumption Dip -> Ammonia Plant Turnaround -> Nitrogen Fertilizer Price Spike
rule: Using yfinance and EIA data: When EIA monthly natural gas industrial consumption drops > 10% MoM during March-May (spring turnaround season), go long CF (CF Industries, largest US nitrogen fertilizer producer) on the first trading day after EIA monthly data release. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The spring industrial gas consumption dip signals ammonia plant turnarounds which temporarily reduce nitrogen fertilizer supply heading into the peak spring application season, supporting prices. FRED series: N3035US3M (industrial natural gas consumption).
entry_conditions: EIA monthly industrial natural gas consumption drops > 10% MoM during March-May
Enter on first trading day after EIA monthly data release
Signal window: March-May only (spring turnaround season)
At most one signal per spring season

data_sources_concrete.fundamental: EIA monthly natural gas consumption by sector (free): https://www.eia.gov/naturalgas/data.php - industrial consumption. FRED: N3035US3M. Also EIA weekly natural gas storage for context.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each March-May period (2005-2025) where industrial gas consumption dropped >10% MoM, compute CF return over 20 trading days vs SPY. CF has been public since 2005 IPO. Spring turnarounds are a regular industry pattern but the magnitude varies. Calculate average excess return, win rate. Also test with NTR (Nutrien, formed 2018 from PotashCorp/Agrium merger) for post-2018 data.
known_events: 2008: Major turnaround year, nitrogen prices spiked
2014: Polar vortex extended turnarounds into spring
2021: Texas winter storm damaged ammonia plants, extended outages into spring
implementation_notes: CF Industries is the purest US nitrogen fertilizer play. NTR (Nutrien) has broader ag exposure (potash, phosphate, retail). The spring turnaround signal works because: (a) turnarounds reduce supply heading into peak demand, (b) EIA data confirms the magnitude of supply disruption, (c) fertilizer pricing responds with 2-4 week lag. bt_feasibility is 4 because EIA data is clean and monthly.

Enter on first trading day after FAA ATADS monthly data publication

name: EIA Industrial Gas Consumption Spring Dip -> Long Nitrogen Fertilizer Producers
rule: Using yfinance and FRED/EIA data: When EIA monthly industrial natural gas consumption (FRED series N3035US2 or similar) shows MoM decline >5% during March-May (spring turnaround season), go long CF (CF Industries) on the first trading day after the EIA release. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that synchronized ammonia plant turnarounds in spring create temporary nitrogen fertilizer supply squeeze, spiking spot prices and benefiting producers with pre-turnaround inventory.
entry_conditions: EIA monthly industrial natural gas consumption shows MoM decline >5% during March-May
This decline signals synchronized ammonia plant turnaround season
Enter on first trading day after EIA monthly data release
One signal per year maximum (spring turnaround season)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: CF (CF Industries Holdings, IPO Aug 2005), LXU (LSB Industries), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED/EIA: Industrial natural gas consumption (N3035US2 or similar monthly series). EIA Natural Gas Monthly (free at eia.gov). Monthly frequency, ~2 month lag. Historical data back to 1990s. USDA AMS weekly fertilizer prices (free) for urea/UAN spot.

backtest_approach: Seasonal event study: For each year (2006-2025, CF IPO 2005), check if EIA March-May industrial gas consumption shows >5% MoM decline. If yes, long CF for 30 trading days starting from signal date. Compare spring-turnaround-period CF returns vs non-signal years. Alternative simpler approach: long CF each April 1 for 30 trading days and compare to unconditional returns. Key context: US has ~16M tons/yr ammonia capacity; turnaround season typically March-May with 20-30% of capacity offline.
known_events: Each spring: Major ammonia producers (CF, Nutrien, Koch, OCI) take plants offline for maintenance
2008 spring: Nat gas spike + turnarounds created extreme fertilizer squeeze; urea hit $800/ton
2013 spring: West Texas Fertilizer plant explosion disrupted supply during turnaround season
2022 spring: Russia/Ukraine disruption + turnarounds created global nitrogen fertilizer crisis
2024 spring: Standard turnaround season with moderate pricing impact
implementation_notes: CF Industries is the largest publicly traded nitrogen fertilizer producer in the US. IPO August 2005. LXU (LSB Industries) is smaller with more volatile earnings. CF is preferred for the single-name strategy due to liquidity. The spring turnaround is a well-known seasonal pattern in nitrogen fertilizer markets. EIA industrial gas consumption is a reasonable proxy for ammonia production (gas is both feedstock and fuel for ammonia). bt_feasibility is 4 because FRED/EIA data is clean and the seasonal pattern is well-documented.

name: FAA Airport Operations Record -> Long Airport-Adjacent Hotel REIT
rule: Using yfinance and FAA/DOT data: When TSA checkpoint throughput (proxy for airport operations, available from TSA.gov) shows YoY growth exceeding 5% for 3+ consecutive months AND absolute levels exceed prior-year same-period records, go long APLE (Apple Hospitality REIT) on the next trading day. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. APLE owns 220+ select-service hotels near airports and suburban commercial corridors that directly benefit from record airport traffic. TSA data is the most timely proxy for FAA ATADS.
entry_conditions: TSA daily checkpoint throughput shows YoY growth >5% for 3+ consecutive months
Absolute throughput levels exceed same-period prior-year records
Enter on first trading day after 3rd consecutive qualifying month
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: APLE (Apple Hospitality REIT, IPO Mar 2015), MAR (Marriott International), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: TSA checkpoint throughput (free at tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes, daily data from 2019+). FAA ATADS (free monthly). DOT T-100 enplanement data (free monthly at transtats.bts.gov). For backtest, use DOT monthly enplanement data which goes back to 1990s.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load DOT monthly enplanement data from BTS (1996-2025). Compute monthly YoY growth. Identify 3-consecutive-month periods where growth >5% and absolute levels exceed prior-year records. For each signal (180-day min gap), long APLE for 42 trading days vs SPY. APLE IPO March 2015; pre-2015 use HST or APLE proxy. Key expected signal periods: 2004-2005 (post-9/11 recovery), 2015-2016 (low oil/cheap flights era), 2023 (post-COVID travel boom surpassing 2019). TSA checkpoint data only available from 2019 (COVID tracking); DOT T-100 provides longer history.
known_events: 2004-2006: Air traffic recovered from 9/11 and exceeded 2000 levels; airport hotels thrived
2015-2016: Record US air travel driven by low fares; airport-area hotels saw 80%+ occupancy
2019: Pre-COVID peak air traffic year; record TSA throughput drove strong hotel performance
2023 Q2-Q4: Post-COVID air travel surge exceeded 2019 levels; TSA set daily records multiple times
2024-2025: Continued record-setting air travel volumes
implementation_notes: APLE IPO March 2015; its portfolio is concentrated in select-service hotels (Hilton Garden Inn, Courtyard by Marriott, Hampton Inn) near airports and suburban commercial areas. For pre-2015 backtest, use HST (Host Hotels, more full-service/gateway city focused) as a proxy. DOT BTS T-100 data is the gold standard for US air traffic but has 2-3 month lag. TSA checkpoint data is real-time from 2019+. bt_feasibility is 4 because DOT enplanement data is clean and APLE/HST are liquid.

name: FTC HSR Early Termination Surge -> Long M&A-Levered Investment Banks
rule: Using yfinance data: When the XBD (NYSE Arca Securities Broker-Dealer Index) or IAI (iShares US Broker-Dealers & Securities Exchanges ETF) shows relative strength vs SPY (outperforming by >5% over 20 trading days) AND M&A deal announcement count is elevated (proxy: monthly FTC HSR early termination grants from ftc.gov exceeding 6-month average by 50%+), go long equal-weight EVR + PJT on the next trading day. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. Boutique M&A advisors have higher sensitivity to deal volume than diversified banks.
entry_conditions: FTC HSR monthly early termination grants exceed trailing 6-month average by >50%
Proxy for backtest: IAI outperforms SPY by >5% over trailing 20 days (broker-dealer momentum signals M&A cycle turning)
Enter on first trading day after signal confirmation
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: EVR (Evercore Inc, IPO Aug 2006), PJT (PJT Partners, spin-off from Blackstone Oct 2015), IAI (iShares US Broker-Dealers), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FTC HSR early termination grants (free at ftc.gov). Published irregularly. Data available back to 2002+. FTC/DOJ Annual HSR Report provides annual filing counts (free).

backtest_approach: Event study: Load IAI and SPY daily prices (2006-2025). Compute rolling 20-day IAI excess return vs SPY. Identify dates where excess return crosses +5%. For each signal (90-day min gap), long EVR+PJT basket for 42 trading days vs SPY. PJT IPO Oct 2015; pre-2015 use EVR+LAZ (Lazard). Key expected events: 2006-2007 (pre-GFC M&A boom), 2013-2014 (post-GFC M&A recovery), 2017-2018 (tax reform M&A surge), 2021 (SPAC/M&A record year), 2024-2025 (M&A cycle recovery from 2022-2023 trough). The thesis is that boutique advisors have higher operating leverage to deal volume than diversified banks.
known_events: 2006-2007: Record M&A volume; LBO boom drove advisory fees to record levels
2014-2015: Post-GFC M&A recovery; mega-deal activity returned ($100B+ deals)
2017-2018: Tax reform triggered wave of strategic M&A; repatriation funded deals
2021: Record M&A year ($5.6T global); SPACs added incremental deal volume
2024-2025: M&A cycle recovery after 2022-2023 rate-shock-driven trough
implementation_notes: EVR IPO Aug 2006 provides good history. PJT spun from Blackstone Oct 2015. Pre-2015 substitute with LAZ (Lazard, IPO 2005). Boutique M&A advisors (EVR, PJT, LAZ) have 50-70% of revenue from advisory fees, making them 3-5x more sensitive to M&A volume than diversified banks like GS/MS. IAI (broker-dealer ETF) launched Sep 2006 and includes both trading desks and advisory firms, providing a broader sector signal. bt_feasibility is 4 because yfinance data is clean and the M&A cycle is well-documented.

name: NOAA Severe Weather Season Exceeds Norm -> Long P&C Reinsurers on Rate Hardening
rule: Using yfinance data: When NOAA NCEI confirms 5+ billion-dollar weather disasters in Q1-Q2 (Jan-June) of a given year (indicating above-average severe weather season), go long equal-weight RNR + ACGL on July 1 (after June/July reinsurance renewal season). Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that above-average catastrophe losses in the first half drive reinsurance rate hardening at mid-year renewals, benefiting reinsurers' forward premium adequacy.
entry_conditions: NOAA NCEI billion-dollar weather disasters: 5+ events confirmed in January-June of current year
Enter on July 1 (or first trading day of July) -- after June/July renewal season captures rate hardening
One signal per year maximum
exit_conditions: Hold for 63 trading days (approximately 3 months through September)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: RNR (RenaissanceRe Holdings, IPO 1993), ACGL (Arch Capital Group, IPO 1995), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NOAA NCEI Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters (free at ncei.noaa.gov/access/billions/). Real-time updates. Historical data back to 1980. Counts tornado outbreaks, hail storms, derechos, hurricanes, flooding, wildfires, winter storms.

backtest_approach: Seasonal event study: For each year (2000-2025), count NOAA NCEI billion-dollar disasters in Jan-June. If count >= 5, long RNR+ACGL from July 1 for 63 trading days. Compare signal-year returns vs non-signal-year returns and vs unconditional July-September returns. Key signal years expected: 2004 (Florida hurricane season preview events), 2008 (Midwest floods + Gustav/Ike setup), 2011 (record tornado year -- Joplin, Tuscaloosa), 2017 (Harvey/Irma/Maria buildup), 2020 (derecho + western wildfires), 2023 (severe convective storm record), 2024 (continued elevated SCS losses).
known_events: 2005: Record hurricane season; Katrina/Rita drove massive reinsurance rate hardening
2011: Record $62B severe weather losses including Joplin tornado; reinsurance rates hardened 10-20%
2017: Harvey/Irma/Maria tripled insured losses; largest single-year cat loss in history at the time
2020: Record 22 named storms + western wildfires; reinsurance pricing hardened into 2021
2023: Record severe convective storm losses ($60B+); driven by hail and tornado activity
implementation_notes: RNR (RenaissanceRe) is a pure-play property catastrophe reinsurer with the strongest brand in the space. ACGL (Arch Capital) has significant reinsurance operations plus insurance and mortgage segments. Both have been public since the 1990s, providing 25+ years of backtest data. The NOAA NCEI billion-dollar disaster database is excellent -- well-maintained, regularly updated, and the $1B CPI-adjusted threshold is consistent over time. The July entry timing captures the June/July reinsurance renewal season, when rate changes are set. bt_feasibility is 4 because NCEI data is clean and RNR/ACGL are liquid.

name: Fed SLOOS C&I Loan Demand Inflection -> Long Small-Cap Value (IWN)
rule: Using yfinance and FRED/Fed SLOOS data: When the Fed SLOOS net percentage of banks reporting stronger demand for C&I loans (FRED series DRSDCI or equivalent) turns positive (or improves by >20pp from trough) after being negative for 3+ consecutive quarters, go long IWN (iShares Russell 2000 Value ETF) on the first trading day after the SLOOS release. Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that C&I loan demand inflection signals business confidence recovery that disproportionately benefits bank-dependent small-cap companies.
entry_conditions: Fed SLOOS net % reporting stronger C&I demand turns positive after 3+ negative quarters
OR: SLOOS C&I demand indicator improves by >20 percentage points from its trough
Enter on first trading day after quarterly SLOOS release (typically January, April, July, October)
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals (one per credit cycle)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: IWN (iShares Russell 2000 Value ETF, launched Jul 2000), IWM (iShares Russell 2000, backup), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: DRSDCI (Net Percentage of Domestic Banks Reporting Stronger Demand for C&I Loans, quarterly) or DRTSCIS (tightening counterpart for reference). Fed SLOOS published quarterly (January, April, July, October) at federalreserve.gov/data/sloos. Historical data back to 1990. Also: FRED BUSLOANS (Commercial and Industrial Loans, All Commercial Banks, weekly) for confirmation.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED DRSDCI quarterly data (1990-2025). Identify quarters where the C&I demand indicator turns positive after 3+ negative quarters or improves >20pp from trough. For each signal (180-day min gap), long IWN for 63 trading days vs SPY. IWN inception July 2000; pre-2000 use Russell 2000 Value index returns. Key expected signal dates: (1) 2002 Q1 (post-dot-com demand recovery), (2) 2009 Q3 (post-GFC demand inflection), (3) 2016 Q3 (post-2015 industrial recession), (4) 2020 Q4 (post-COVID credit demand recovery), (5) 2024 Q1 (post-2022-2023 rate-shock tightening cycle). These align with classic credit cycle turning points. Compute per-event IWN excess return vs SPY.
known_events: 2002 Q1: C&I demand bottomed after dot-com bust; small-cap value led recovery 2002-2006
2009 Q3-Q4: SLOOS demand indicators turned sharply less negative; IWN outperformed by 15%+ over next 6mo
2016 Q3: Post-industrial-recession demand recovery; small-cap value rotation (IWN beat SPY by 12% in Q4 2016)
2020 Q4: Post-COVID credit demand recovery; IWN outperformed SPY by 20%+ through March 2021
2024 Q1: C&I demand began recovering after 2022-2023 rate-shock-driven contraction
implementation_notes: FRED DRSDCI (or closely related series) is the key data input. SLOOS is released quarterly, approximately 2-3 weeks after the survey reference date. IWN (Russell 2000 Value) is preferred over IWM (Russell 2000 Growth-blend) because value stocks are more bank-credit-dependent than growth stocks. The 3-quarter negative threshold filters out noise and identifies genuine credit cycle inflections. SLOOS has been published since 1990, providing 35+ years of data with 5-6 clear credit cycle turning points. This is one of the highest-conviction macro signals available with free data. bt_feasibility is 5 because FRED SLOOS data is quarterly, clean, and the signal-to-asset mapping (credit demand -> small-cap performance) is well-established in academic finance.

name: NHTSA Safety Investigation Opening -> OEM Recall -> Auto Parts Aftermarket Long
rule: Using yfinance and NHTSA data: When NHTSA opens a Preliminary Evaluation (PE) or Engineering Analysis (EA) on a vehicle model with > 500K potentially affected units, go long an equal-weight basket of auto parts aftermarket stocks (AZO, ORLY, DORM) on the trading day after the NHTSA investigation announcement. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. NHTSA publishes new investigations in real-time at nhtsa.gov/recalls. The recall-to-parts-demand pipeline takes 3-12 months but the stock market may price in the demand uplift earlier.
entry_conditions: NHTSA opens PE or EA investigation on a model with > 500K potentially affected vehicles
Enter on trading day after NHTSA investigation announcement
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: AZO (AutoZone), ORLY (O'Reilly Automotive), DORM (Dorman Products, replacement parts manufacturer), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NHTSA Office of Defects Investigation (free): https://www.nhtsa.gov/recalls - PE/EA investigation openings with affected vehicle counts. Also NHTSA complaints database for leading indicators.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each NHTSA PE/EA opening (2010-2025) affecting >500K vehicles, compute AZO+ORLY+DORM return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Major investigations: 2014 GM ignition switch (2.6M vehicles), 2014-2020 Takata airbags (tens of millions), 2022 Tesla autopilot (multiple investigations), 2024 various OEM investigations. With 90-day gap, expect 10-20 events. AZO and ORLY are resilient defensive stocks so the excess return may be modest but consistent. DORM is the purer play on recall-driven parts demand.
known_events: 2014: GM ignition switch recall (2.6M vehicles, massive parts demand)
2014-2020: Takata airbag recall (largest ever, 67M US vehicles affected)
2016: Samsung Galaxy Note 7 (not auto, but showed recall mechanics)
2022-2024: Multiple EV-related investigations (battery, autopilot)
implementation_notes: NHTSA data is free, real-time, and well-structured. AZO and ORLY are the largest US auto parts retailers -- they benefit from any increase in car repair activity. DORM (Dorman Products) specifically manufactures replacement OE parts, making it the most direct beneficiary of recall-driven demand. SMP (Standard Motor Products) was in the original idea but DORM is a purer play. AAP (Advance Auto Parts) was excluded due to operational issues. bt_feasibility is 4 because NHTSA data is precise and the affected-vehicle-count threshold is well-defined.

name: USGS Copper Scrap-to-Refined Ratio Peak -> Scrap Exhaustion -> Copper/Copper Miners Long
rule: Using yfinance and USGS copper data: When USGS monthly copper data shows the scrap-to-refined consumption ratio peaked above 42% and has declined for 2 consecutive months, go long an equal-weight basket of copper miners (FCX, COPX) on the first trading day of the following month. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The scrap ratio peaking and declining signals that economically recoverable secondary copper supply is being exhausted, removing a price ceiling and allowing primary supply constraints to drive prices higher.
entry_conditions: USGS monthly copper scrap-to-refined ratio peaked above 42% within prior 3 months
Ratio has declined for 2 consecutive months from the peak
Enter on first trading day of month following confirmation
Minimum 120 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: FCX (Freeport-McMoRan), COPX (Global X Copper Miners ETF), HG=F (COMEX copper futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USGS Mineral Industry Surveys - Copper (free): https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/copper-statistics-and-information - monthly data on refined copper production, scrap consumption, and total apparent consumption. Calculate scrap ratio = scrap consumption / total consumption.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each period (2010-2025) where copper scrap ratio peaked above 42% then declined for 2 months, compute FCX+COPX return over 40 trading days vs SPY. COPX inception April 2010. The scrap ratio thesis is novel -- it captures a supply-side dynamic that most copper analysts discuss qualitatively but few quantify with systematic data. Expected 3-6 events over the period. USGS monthly data may have publication lag of 2-3 months. Calculate average excess return.
known_events: 2011: High copper prices drove record scrap recovery, ratio spiked then collapsed as scrap exhausted
2017-2018: China scrap import ban disrupted global scrap flows, US ratio spiked
2021-2022: Copper price surge drove demolition scrap recovery wave
2024: Elevated copper prices again incentivized secondary supply
implementation_notes: USGS monthly copper surveys are free but have 2-3 month publication lag, which actually helps the backtester avoid look-ahead bias. The 42% threshold (vs idea's 45%) is more realistic based on historical data showing ratios rarely exceed 45%. FCX is the largest publicly traded copper producer. COPX provides diversified copper miner exposure. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) USGS monthly data has lag, (b) scrap ratio calculation requires parsing USGS tables, (c) limited events at 42%+ threshold. The idea's originality (9/10) makes this worth testing despite feasibility challenges.

name: BLS Petroleum Import Price Drop -> Long Consumer Discretionary
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When BLS Import Price Index for petroleum (FRED series IR or similar) shows YoY decline >15% for 3 consecutive months, go long XLY (Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR) on the first trading day after the 3rd consecutive qualifying BLS release. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that sustained petroleum import price declines act as a consumer tax cut, redirecting gasoline savings into discretionary spending.
entry_conditions: BLS Import Price Index petroleum component shows YoY decline >15% for 3 consecutive months
Enter on first trading day after 3rd consecutive qualifying BLS Import Price release
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XLY (Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR, launched Dec 1998), DRI (Darden Restaurants), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: Import Price Index -- petroleum (series like IR10000 or BLS MXP petroleum component). Published monthly by BLS, ~2 weeks after reference month. Historical data back to 1982. EIA weekly gasoline prices (FRED GASREGW) for supplementary confirmation.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED petroleum import price index (1983-2025). Compute monthly YoY change. Identify 3-consecutive-month periods where YoY decline exceeds 15%. For each signal (90-day min gap), long XLY for 42 trading days vs SPY. XLY inception Dec 1998; pre-1998 use equal-weight basket of consumer discretionary stocks or S&P Consumer Discretionary index returns. Key expected signal periods: 2001 Q4 (post-9/11 oil price crash), 2008 Q4-2009 Q1 (GFC oil crash from $147 to $33), 2014 Q4-2015 Q1 (OPEC price war, oil crashed from $100 to $45), 2020 Q2-Q3 (COVID oil crash), 2023 Q1 (oil prices declined from 2022 highs).
known_events: 1998 Q1: Asian crisis drove oil below $10; US consumer spending boomed
2001 Q4: Post-9/11 and recession drove oil down; consumer spending recovered Q1 2002
2008 Q4-2009 Q1: Oil crashed from $147 to $33; gasoline savings massive but offset by recession
2014 Q4-2015 Q1: OPEC price war; oil $100->$45; consumer discretionary outperformed significantly
2020 Q2-Q3: COVID oil crash; gasoline prices plunged but mobility was restricted
2023 Q1: Oil declined from $120 2022 highs; consumer spending remained resilient
implementation_notes: The 2008 and 2020 events are tricky because oil crashed due to recession/crisis, which also hurt consumer spending. The 2014-2015 OPEC price war is the cleanest test because it was a supply-driven oil decline with no US recession. The YoY >15% threshold for 3 months filters out small fluctuations. XLY is Amazon-heavy (23%); for a purer discretionary signal, could use equal-weight DRI+HLT+RCL but these have shorter histories. bt_feasibility is 5 because FRED import price data is monthly, clean, and goes back to 1982. The main risk is that oil crashes often coincide with recessions, making the signal noisy.

name: DOE AFDC EV Registration Rate -> State-Level EV Penetration -> Utility Infrastructure Capex Long
rule: Using yfinance and DOE AFDC data: When DOE quarterly EV registration data shows that EV penetration in a major utility's service territory exceeds 8% of new vehicle registrations (a tipping point for grid investment), go long utilities with significant EV-growth territory exposure (PCG, EVRG, DUK) on the first trading day after DOE data release. Hold for 30 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. DOE AFDC publishes quarterly EV registration data by state (free). The 8% threshold is where utility commissions typically approve accelerated EV infrastructure rate base additions.
entry_conditions: DOE AFDC quarterly data shows EV share of new registrations > 8% in a major utility service territory state
State must have approved or pending utility EV infrastructure program
Enter on first trading day after DOE quarterly data release
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: PCG (PG&E Corp, California utility), DUK (Duke Energy), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: DOE Alternative Fuels Data Center (free): https://afdc.energy.gov/vehicle-registration - quarterly EV registrations by state. Also state PUC filings for EV infrastructure program approvals.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each DOE quarterly release (2020-2025) where key states crossed the 8% EV penetration threshold, compute PCG+DUK return over 30 trading days vs SPY. California crossed 8% early (~2020), other states followed. The signal fires as each new state crosses the threshold. Calculate average excess return. bt_feasibility is 3 due to: (a) quarterly frequency, (b) the EV penetration-to-utility-capex link has significant lag, (c) utilities are affected by many factors beyond EV.
known_events: 2020: California EV share exceeded 8% of new registrations
2023: Several more states crossed 5-8% thresholds
2024-2025: EV adoption accelerating in sunbelt states
implementation_notes: PCG is the most EV-exposed utility (California). EVRG (Evergy) was in the idea but is Kansas/Missouri focused with lower EV penetration. DUK has growing EV programs across Southeast. The 8% threshold is based on industry analysis showing utilities begin receiving PUC approval for EV infrastructure rate base additions at this level. bt_feasibility is 3.

name: BLS Import Price Index Petroleum Drop -> Terms of Trade Improvement -> Consumer Discretionary Long
rule: Using yfinance and BLS/FRED data: When BLS Import Price Index petroleum component (FRED series IR141) shows YoY decline > 15% for 3 consecutive months, go long an equal-weight basket of consumer discretionary stocks (XLY, DRI, RCL) on the BLS release date. Hold for 20 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. BLS Import/Export Price Indexes published monthly, typically around the 14th. Falling petroleum import prices improve US terms of trade, boost consumer purchasing power, and benefit discretionary spending-sensitive businesses.
entry_conditions: BLS Import Price Index petroleum component (FRED IR141) YoY decline > 15% for 3 consecutive months
Enter on BLS Import Price release date (typically 14th of month)
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XLY (Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR), DRI (Darden Restaurants), RCL (Royal Caribbean), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED series IR141 (Import Price Index: Petroleum, Not Seasonally Adjusted) or FRED MCOILBRENTEU (Brent crude). BLS Import/Export Price Indexes (free): https://www.bls.gov/mxp/ - monthly, released ~14th of month.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each month (2000-2025) where petroleum import prices declined >15% YoY for 3 consecutive months, compute XLY+DRI+RCL return over 20 trading days vs SPY. Major petroleum price declines: 2001 (recession), 2008-2009 (GFC), 2014-2015 (shale oversupply), 2020 (COVID), 2023 (normalization). XLY inception Dec 1998, DRI public since 1995, RCL public since 1993. Expected 5-8 event clusters. bt_feasibility is 5 because: (a) FRED data is clean, free, monthly, (b) the petroleum-to-consumer-spending mechanism is well-established, (c) precise thresholds are easily computed.
known_events: 2001-2002: Post-9/11 oil price decline, consumer discretionary outperformed
2008-2009: Oil crashed from $140 to $30, massive terms of trade improvement
2014-2016: Shale oversupply crashed oil, consumer spending benefited
2020: COVID oil crash, consumer stocks recovered strongly
2023: Oil price normalization from 2022 highs
implementation_notes: This is one of the highest bt_feasibility signals (5/5). FRED IR141 is pristine monthly data going back decades. The mechanism is textbook macro: falling oil prices = lower gasoline costs = more disposable income for consumers = discretionary spending uplift. XLY provides broad consumer discretionary exposure. DRI (restaurants) and RCL (cruises) are high-beta consumer discretionary names that benefit disproportionately from lower fuel costs (both as input cost and consumer wallet share). The 3-month consecutive decline filter ensures sustained price improvement, not a one-month blip.

name: USDA PNW Grain Export Inspection Surge -> Long Union Pacific
rule: Using yfinance data: When UNP (Union Pacific) outperforms CSX (CSX Corp) by >5% over trailing 20 trading days during grain export season (September-February), go long UNP for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. This price-based proxy captures the fundamental signal that USDA PNW grain export inspections are surging relative to Gulf, which benefits UNP's PNW franchise. The grain season timing (harvest Sept-Nov, export peak Oct-Feb) provides the seasonal context.
entry_conditions: UNP outperforms CSX by >5% over trailing 20 trading days during September-February
This relative strength proxy captures PNW grain volume advantage over Gulf-focused CSX
Enter on first trading day after condition met
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: UNP (Union Pacific), CSX (CSX Corporation), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USDA AMS FGIS weekly export inspections by port region (free at ams.usda.gov). Data available weekly going back 20+ years. Tracks grain inspections at PNW (Portland/Puget Sound), Gulf (New Orleans/Houston), Great Lakes, and interior ports.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load UNP, CSX, SPY daily prices (2000-2025). During September-February each year, compute rolling 20-day UNP excess return vs CSX. Identify dates where UNP outperforms CSX by >5%. For each signal (90-day min gap), long UNP for 42 trading days vs SPY. Key expected events: 2007-2008 (record China grain imports), 2013-2014 (China soybean demand surge), 2017-2018 (trade war preemptive buying), 2020-2021 (China Phase 1 trade deal grain purchases), 2024 (strong Asian grain demand).
known_events: 2007-2008: Record grain export volumes driven by Chinese demand; PNW grain inspections surged
2013-2014: China soybean demand at record levels; PNW shipments hit capacity constraints
2016 Q4: Post-harvest surge in PNW grain inspections; UNP grain carloads hit records
2020-2021: China Phase 1 trade deal purchases drove massive PNW grain exports
2024: Strong Asian demand for US wheat and corn via PNW ports
implementation_notes: UNP has the dominant rail franchise serving PNW grain export terminals (Portland, Longview, Tacoma). CSX serves the Eastern/Gulf grain corridor. The UNP vs CSX relative performance during grain season is a clean proxy for PNW vs Gulf grain flow dynamics. Both stocks have been public for decades with excellent liquidity. The grain export season (Sept-Feb) provides natural timing for the signal. bt_feasibility is 4 because the price-based proxy eliminates the need for USDA data parsing.

name: Census M3 Core Capex Shipments Acceleration -> Long Industrial Distributors
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When Census M3 nondefense capital goods shipments ex-aircraft (FRED series A34SNO or ANXFS) show YoY growth turning positive after 3+ months negative, OR accelerate by >3 percentage points from trough, go long equal-weight FAST + GWW on the first trading day after the Census M3 release. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that core capex shipment inflection drives MRO consumable demand recovery at industrial distributors.
entry_conditions: Census M3 core capex shipments (A34SNO) YoY growth turns positive after 3+ months negative
OR: YoY growth accelerates by >3 percentage points from trough
Enter on first trading day after Census M3 monthly release (about 5 weeks after reference month)
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: FAST (Fastenal Company, IPO Aug 1987), GWW (W.W. Grainger, IPO decades ago), MSM (MSC Industrial, IPO Nov 1995), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: A34SNO (Manufacturers' New Orders: Nondefense Capital Goods Excluding Aircraft, monthly SA) or ANXFS (Shipments: Nondefense Capital Goods Excluding Aircraft). Published monthly by Census Bureau. ~35 calendar days after reference month. Historical data back to 1992.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED A34SNO monthly data (1992-2025). Compute monthly YoY growth. Identify months where YoY turns positive after 3+ negative months, or accelerates >3pp from trough. For each signal (90-day min gap), long FAST+GWW basket for 42 trading days vs SPY. Key expected events: (1) 2002 Q2 (post-dot-com capex recovery), (2) 2009 Q3 (post-GFC capex bottom), (3) 2013 Q1 (post-fiscal-cliff capex recovery), (4) 2016 Q3 (post-industrial-recession recovery), (5) 2020 Q4 (post-COVID capex rebound), (6) 2024 Q1 (post-rate-shock capex recovery). FAST has data back to 1987, providing 35+ years of backtest.
known_events: 2002-2003: Core capex bottomed Q1 2002; FAST outperformed as MRO demand recovered

FDA NME approval data is clean, publicly available, and easily countable. The 5+ NME/month threshold is roughly the 80th percentile of monthly NME counts since 2006. The backtester should check whether removing December changes results. bt_feasibility is 4.

name: USGS Columbia River Low Flow -> Long Thermal Power IPPs (NRG)
rule: Using yfinance and USGS NWIS data: When USGS real-time streamflow at The Dalles, OR (station 14105700) 30-day average drops below the 25th percentile of historical June-September flows, go long NRG on the next trading day. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. As a backtest proxy: when XLE (energy) outperforms XLU (utilities) by >8% over 30 trading days during June-September, it signals thermal dispatch economics improving (thermal generators earning elevated spark spreads while hydro-dependent utilities underperform).
entry_conditions: USGS Columbia River at The Dalles 30-day avg flow below 25th percentile for June-September
Backtest proxy: XLE outperforms XLU by >8% over 30 trading days during June-September
Enter on next trading day after signal confirmation
One signal per summer season maximum

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: NRG (NRG Energy), XLE, XLU, SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USGS NWIS station 14105700 (Columbia River at The Dalles, OR). Free real-time and historical daily mean flow. Data back to 1878.

backtest_approach: Event study: Identify summers where Columbia River flow was below 25th percentile (drought years). Key events: 2001 (Pacific NW energy crisis), 2005, 2015 (severe drought), 2021 (heat dome), 2024. Long NRG for 42 days per event. Also test XLE/XLU proxy approach for broader backtest.
known_events: 2001: Pacific NW energy crisis from severe drought; wholesale power prices spiked 10x
2015: Record drought reduced Columbia flows; Mid-C prices elevated all summer
2021: Pacific NW heat dome event; record temperatures + low hydro = extreme pricing
implementation_notes: NRG has diversified thermal fleet including WECC exposure. USGS data at The Dalles is the key gauge for Columbia River system hydro generation. bt_feasibility is 4 because USGS streamflow data is excellent.

name: FDA Novel Drug Approval Cluster -> Long Small-Cap Biotech (XBI)
rule: Using yfinance and FDA data: When FDA CDER approves >5 novel molecular entities (NMEs) in a single calendar month (vs historical baseline of ~4/month), go long XBI (SPDR S&P Biotech ETF) on the first trading day of the following month. Hold for 42 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that NME approval clusters signal favorable regulatory environment, which drives big pharma M&A interest in small-cap biotech targets.
entry_conditions: FDA CDER approves >5 novel molecular entities in a single calendar month
Hand-coded from FDA CDER monthly approval lists (free at fda.gov)
Enter on first trading day of the month following the approval cluster
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: XBI (SPDR S&P Biotech ETF, launched Jan 2006), IBB (iShares Biotech, launched 2001), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FDA CDER novel drug approvals database (free at fda.gov/drugs/new-drugs-fda-cders-new-molecular-entities-and-new-therapeutic-biological-products). Annual novel drug approval reports with monthly breakdown. Data back to 1990s.

backtest_approach: Event study: Hand-code months with >5 NME approvals from FDA annual reports (2006-2025 for XBI). Key expected high-approval months: several in 2018 (record 59 NMEs), 2020 (53 NMEs), 2023 (55 NMEs). For each signal month (90-day gap), long XBI for 42 trading days vs SPY. The mechanism: approval clusters reflect FDA staffing increases and productive pipelines, which validates biotech R&D investment and re-rates the sector's risk premium.

2020: 53 NME approvals; COVID-era fast-track approvals boosted biotech sentiment
2023: 55 NME approvals; Alzheimer's drugs (lecanemab, donanemab) drove biotech M&A interest
implementation_notes: XBI (equal-weight biotech) is preferred over IBB (cap-weight) because M&A targets are small/mid-cap. FDA CDER novel drug approval data is clean and well-maintained. The >5 NME threshold in a single month is aggressive; if too few events, relax to >4. bt_feasibility is 4 because FDA data is free and well-documented.

name: S&P GSCI Rebalance Roll Window -> Long Second-Month Energy Futures
rule: Using yfinance data: On the 4th business day of each month (day before GSCI roll begins), go long BNO (United States Brent Oil Fund, which holds second-month futures) and hold for 5 trading days through the roll window. Benchmark vs USO (United States Oil Fund, front-month focused). The thesis is that predictable GSCI index fund selling of front-month contracts during the 5th-9th business day creates temporary calendar spread widening that benefits second-month holders. LONG ONLY -- buying the second-month contract.
entry_conditions: Enter on 4th business day of each month (day before GSCI 5-day roll window)
Position is long BNO (second-month Brent exposure)
Trade occurs every month -- mechanical entry
exit_conditions: Hold for 5 trading days (through end of GSCI roll window)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: BNO (United States Brent Oil Fund), USO (United States Oil Fund), CL=F (WTI front-month), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: S&P GSCI methodology (free at spglobal.com). Roll schedule: 5th through 9th business day of each month. Annual rebalancing weights published November.

backtest_approach: Calendar study: For each month (2010-2025, BNO inception), compute BNO return from 4th to 9th business day vs USO return over same window. Test whether BNO systematically outperforms USO during roll window. If the spread is positive on average, the roll yield capture is working. Based on Mou (2011) research showing ~5-10bps/month roll yield predictability. With 180 monthly observations, this provides strong statistical power despite small per-trade return.
known_events: 2011: Mou Columbia PhD thesis documented predictable GSCI roll effects in energy
2020 Apr: Extreme contango during COVID oil crash made roll effects massive (USO restructured)
2022-2023: Backwardation in crude reduced roll yield capture opportunity
implementation_notes: BNO holds second-month Brent futures. USO was restructured in 2020 to hold a mix of maturities instead of pure front-month, which may reduce the signal since 2020. Pre-2020, USO was pure front-month making the roll effect cleaner. The GSCI roll is one of the most well-documented predictable flows in commodity markets. bt_feasibility is 4 because the roll schedule is mechanical and BNO/USO are liquid ETFs on yfinance. The per-trade return is small (~5-10bps) but occurs every month, making it a high-frequency low-volatility strategy.

name: S&P GSCI Quarterly Rebalance Roll Yield Capture in Energy Futures
rule: Using yfinance and S&P GSCI rebalance schedule: Go long USO (United States Oil Fund) 5 trading days before each S&P GSCI quarterly rebalance date and hold through 5 trading days after the rebalance (total 10 trading days). Benchmark vs SPY. S&P GSCI rebalances occur on the 5th-9th business day of January, April, July, and October. During rebalance, index funds must roll futures contracts, creating predictable demand flows in energy futures. The predictable buying pressure during the roll period creates a short-term excess return opportunity.
entry_conditions: Enter 5 trading days before S&P GSCI quarterly rebalance (5th-9th business day of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct)
Rebalance schedule published by S&P Dow Jones Indices (free)
Signal fires exactly 4 times per year
exit_conditions: Hold for 10 trading days (5 before + 5 after rebalance)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: USO (United States Oil Fund), BNO (United States Brent Oil Fund), CL=F (WTI crude futures), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: S&P GSCI rebalance schedule (free): https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/indices/commodities/sp-gsci/ - annual rebalance calendar published each December for following year. Goldman Roll dates are the 5th-9th business day of each rebalance month.

backtest_approach: Calendar study: For each GSCI rebalance period (2006-2025, USO inception April 2006), compute USO return over the 10-trading-day window centered on the rebalance vs SPY over the same window. 4 events per year = 80 observations over 20 years. The 'Goldman Roll' effect has been studied academically and was strongest pre-2010 when GSCI AUM was larger relative to futures open interest. Calculate average excess return, win rate, and test if the effect has decayed over time.
known_events: 2005-2008: Peak GSCI AUM period, roll effect was strongest
2010: GSCI methodology changes reduced some predictability
2020-2024: Commodity index AUM recovered, potential roll effect restoration
implementation_notes: The Goldman Roll (GSCI rebalance) effect is well-known among commodity traders but may still persist due to the mechanical nature of index fund rolling. USO tracks WTI front-month futures and suffers from contango drag, but for the 10-day window this is minimal. BNO tracks Brent and may show a different pattern. bt_feasibility is 4 because rebalance dates are precisely known in advance and the trading rule is mechanical.

name: FEMA National Risk Index Update -> Insurer Geographic De-Risking -> Specialty Insurer Long
rule: Using yfinance and FEMA NRI data: When FEMA updates the National Risk Index (NRI) and the number of counties rated 'Very High' or 'Relatively High' risk increases by > 10% from the prior version, go long HCI (HCI Group, Florida specialty insurer) on the first trading day after FEMA NRI publication. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. FEMA NRI is published free at hazards.fema.gov. When more counties are rated high-risk, national insurers (Allstate, State Farm) de-risk by non-renewing policies, creating opportunity for specialty carriers like HCI that price the risk more accurately.
entry_conditions: FEMA NRI update shows > 10% increase in counties rated 'Very High' or 'Relatively High' risk
Enter on first trading day after FEMA NRI publication
Signal fires at most once per NRI update cycle (irregular, roughly annual)

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: HCI (HCI Group, Florida property insurer), ALL (Allstate, for comparison), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FEMA National Risk Index (free): https://hazards.fema.gov/nri/ - county-level composite risk scores for 18 natural hazard types. Updated periodically. Downloadable as CSV/GIS data.

backtest_approach: Event study: Limited history as FEMA NRI was first released in 2021 (version 1.0) with updates in 2023 and 2024. For each NRI update where high-risk county count increased >10%, compute HCI return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Supplement with analysis of state DOI Citizens Insurance depopulation events (Florida) which follow similar dynamics. Very limited events (2-3) make statistical testing difficult. bt_feasibility is 3 due to NRI's short history.
known_events: 2021: FEMA NRI v1.0 released, first comprehensive county-level risk assessment
2023: NRI updated with improved flood and wildfire models
2024: NRI update reflected increased wildfire risk in California counties
implementation_notes: HCI Group is a Florida-focused property insurer that specifically targets policies shed by national carriers. When FEMA risk ratings increase, national carriers accelerate non-renewals, expanding HCI's addressable market. ALL (Allstate) is included as a comparison -- it typically underperforms when risk ratings rise (higher loss expectations). UVE (Universal Insurance) was in the idea but appears to have ticker issues. bt_feasibility is 3 due to NRI's very short history (2021+) and irregular update schedule.

name: USDA FAS Protein Export Surge -> Long US Protein Packers (TSN/PPC)

Enter on first trading day after monthly trade data release

name: SEC 13F Activist Cluster Accumulation -> Long Target Mid-Cap
rule: Using yfinance and SEC EDGAR data: When 3+ known activist funds (from a curated ~50-fund CIK list) simultaneously increase positions by >1% of shares outstanding in the same mid-cap stock in a single 13F quarter, go long that stock on the day the 13F filing deadline passes (T+45 days after quarter end). Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. As a simpler backtest proxy: when a 13D filing (>5% activist stake) is made at a company where 2+ activists already held >2% positions per prior quarter's 13F, go long.
entry_conditions: 3+ known activist funds increase positions by >1% of outstanding in same mid-cap stock in one 13F quarter
Simpler proxy: 13D filing at a company with 2+ existing activist 13F holders
Enter on 13F deadline day (Feb 14, May 15, Aug 14, Nov 14) or 13D filing date
Minimum 30 calendar days between signals per target

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: target stock (varies), SPY for benchmark
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: SEC EDGAR 13F-HR filings (free at sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar). 13D filings for >5% stakes. Curated activist CIK list (~50 funds: Icahn, Elliott, Starboard, ValueAct, Third Point, Trian, etc.). Data back to 2004 (EDGAR full-text search).

backtest_approach: Event study: Build curated list of ~50 known activist fund CIKs. For each 13F quarter (2006-2025), parse filings to identify stocks where 3+ activists hold >1% positions. Identify new accumulations (position increase from prior quarter). For each target stock meeting the multi-activist threshold, long for 63 trading days from 13F deadline. Compute average excess return vs SPY. Academic evidence (Brav et al. JF 2008) shows activist targets earn 7-8% CAR over the year following initial accumulation. Multi-activist convergence should amplify the effect.
known_events: Academic literature (Brav et al. 2008) documents 7-8% average CAR for activist targets
Multi-activist situations like Darden Restaurants (2014: Starboard + Barington) saw 40%+ returns
Recent examples: activist clusters in Disney, Salesforce, Illumina (2023) drove significant re-ratings
implementation_notes: This strategy requires a curated CIK list of known activist funds, which is manual but stable (activist funds rarely change CIKs). The 13F filing deadline is 45 days after quarter-end, so the information is stale but still actionable because markets underreact to multi-activist convergence. Target stocks vary each quarter -- this is a portfolio construction strategy rather than a single-ticker trade. bt_feasibility is 4 because SEC EDGAR data is free, but the parsing/cross-referencing is non-trivial.

name: ASCE Water Infrastructure Grade Decline -> Water Utility Capex and Rate Base Growth Long

Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. ASCE Report Card is published every 4 years (2013, 2017, 2021, 2025 expected). A downgrade signals increased urgency for water infrastructure investment, driving utility capex approvals and rate base growth.

Enter on first trading day after ASCE report publication
Signal fires at most once per 4-year cycle

Supplement with state-level drinking water violation data from EPA SDWIS as a more frequent proxy signal. bt_feasibility is 3 due to 4-year frequency.

2017: Drinking water remained D

The 4-year frequency makes traditional backtesting nearly impossible (only 3 data points). The supplementary approach using EPA SDWIS (Safe Drinking Water Information System) violation counts as a higher-frequency proxy could improve testability. bt_feasibility is 3.

Condition sustained for 2 consecutive months

TSN public since 1960s, PPC since 2009 (current form). Calculate average excess return, win rate.

2017-2018: Strong global protein demand, US exports at record levels
2020: African Swine Fever in China drove massive US pork export surge
2022: Strong beef exports to Japan and South Korea
implementation_notes: TSN is the most diversified US protein company (beef, pork, chicken, prepared foods). PPC (Pilgrim's Pride) is a major chicken processor with growing export business. USDA GATS data is free, monthly, and well-structured for automated extraction. The 5-year seasonal average comparison adjusts for normal seasonal patterns in protein exports. The 2-month persistence filter ensures sustained demand, not a one-off trade. bt_feasibility is 4 because GATS data is clean and monthly.

name: USACE Scheduled Lock Closure -> Grain Rail Diversion -> Class I Railroad Revenue Long
rule: Using yfinance and USACE data: When USACE schedules a major lock closure on the Mississippi/Illinois/Ohio River system (affecting >10M tons annual throughput) during grain export season (September-December), go long an equal-weight basket of Class I railroads (UNP, NSC) 30 trading days before the closure start date. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. USACE publishes lock closure schedules months in advance (free at navigationcharts.usace.army.mil). Lock closures force grain shippers to divert to rail, creating surge pricing and volume for railroads.
entry_conditions: USACE schedules lock closure on major grain corridor (Mississippi, Illinois, Ohio River) affecting >10M tons/year
Closure overlaps with September-December grain export season
Enter 30 trading days before scheduled closure start
Minimum 180 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: UNP (Union Pacific, western grain origination), NSC (Norfolk Southern, eastern grain routes), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: USACE lock closure schedules (free): https://www.navigationcharts.usace.army.mil/ - maintenance closure dates published 6-12 months ahead. Also USACE Lock Performance Monitoring System for traffic disruption data.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each major USACE lock closure during grain season (2010-2025), compute UNP+NSC return over 40 trading days vs SPY (starting 30 days pre-closure). Major lock closures: Lock 27 Mississippi River repairs, LaGrange Lock Illinois River closures, various Ohio River lock rehabilitation projects. USACE has been rebuilding aging lock infrastructure under WRDA authorizations, creating multiple events. Calculate average excess return, win rate.
known_events: 2014: Olmsted Lock and Dam construction disrupted Ohio River traffic
2018-2020: Multiple Mississippi River lock rehabilitation closures
2022: Lock 25 closure during harvest season forced rail diversion
implementation_notes: UNP handles the majority of western grain origination (connecting Great Plains to Gulf export terminals). NSC handles eastern grain. When barge routes are closed, grain must move by rail at higher per-ton cost, benefiting railroad revenue per carload. USACE closure schedules are published well in advance, making this a predictable catalyst. bt_feasibility is 4 because closure dates are known months ahead and the grain diversion mechanism is well-documented.

name: NASA FIRMS Active Fire Anomaly in Timber Regions -> Lumber Supply Shock -> Timberland REIT Long
rule: Using yfinance and NASA FIRMS data: When NASA FIRMS (Fire Information for Resource Management System) shows active fire detections in Pacific NW timber regions (Oregon, Washington, Northern California) exceeding the 90th percentile of historical same-month fire pixel counts for 2+ consecutive weeks during fire season (June-October), go long an equal-weight basket of timberland REITs (WY, PCH) on the Monday following the second qualifying week. Hold for 40 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. NASA FIRMS provides free near-real-time active fire data from MODIS and VIIRS satellites.
entry_conditions: NASA FIRMS active fire pixel count in OR+WA+NorCal timber regions exceeds 90th percentile of historical same-month distribution
Condition sustained for 2+ consecutive weeks
Signal window: June through October (fire season)
Enter on Monday following second qualifying week
Minimum 120 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: WY (Weyerhaeuser, largest US timberland REIT), PCH (PotlatchDeltic, timberland + lumber mills), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: NASA FIRMS (free): https://firms.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/ - near-real-time active fire data from MODIS/VIIRS satellites. CSV download with lat/lon, brightness, confidence. Historical archive back to 2000 for MODIS.

backtest_approach: Event study: For each fire season (2005-2025) where Pacific NW fire activity exceeded 90th percentile for 2+ weeks, compute WY+PCH return over 40 trading days vs SPY. Major PNW fire years: 2015 (record fires), 2017 (Eagle Creek), 2020 (Labor Day fires, 1M+ acres in Oregon), 2021 (Bootleg Fire). WY has long public history, PCH formed from Potlatch/Deltic merger 2018 (use PotlatchDeltic pre-2018). Expected 4-6 events. The thesis: major fires destroy competitor timber, tightening future lumber supply and benefiting surviving timberland owners.
known_events: 2015: Pacific NW record fire season, 1.6M acres in OR+WA
2017: Eagle Creek fire, Columbia Gorge fires
2020: Labor Day firestorm in Oregon, 1M+ acres, multiple towns destroyed
2021: Bootleg Fire in Oregon (400K+ acres)
implementation_notes: NASA FIRMS data is excellent: free, near-real-time, global coverage, CSV download with lat/lon coordinates for geofencing. The thesis has a counterintuitive element: wildfire destroys timber supply, which benefits timberland REITs that own unburned forests (rising stumpage prices). WY and PCH own millions of acres in PNW. The risk is that fires could burn WY/PCH's own timberland, which would be negative. The backtester should examine whether the net effect is positive or negative. bt_feasibility is 3 because: (a) the direction of effect is uncertain, (b) fire-to-lumber-price transmission has lag.

name: USACE Lock Closure During Harvest -> Long Class I Railroads (UNP)

Enter on announcement date or first trading day of closure
One signal per harvest season

name: SSA COLA Exceeds Expectations -> Long Retiree-Consumer Exposed Stocks

Hold for 63 trading days (through January when checks arrive). Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that large COLA increases boost 70M+ beneficiaries' income, driving spending at discount retailers and senior housing occupancy.

Enter on first trading day after SSA COLA announcement (mid-October)
One signal per year

SSA COLA is mechanically determined by CPI-W, so it's predictable once Q3 CPI data is known (before the official announcement). bt_feasibility is 4 because the event dates and COLA percentages are public record.

name: TIPS Real Yield Curve Deep Inversion -> Long Gold (GLD)
rule: Using yfinance and FRED data: When the TIPS 2y-10y real yield spread (FRED DFII10 minus DFII2) inverts beyond -50bp (i.e., 2y real yield exceeds 10y real yield by >50bp), go long GLD on the next trading day. Hold for 63 trading days. Benchmark vs SPY. The thesis is that deep real curve inversion signals the market expects aggressive easing and prolonged negative real rates, reducing gold's opportunity cost and driving institutional allocation.
entry_conditions: TIPS 2y-10y real yield spread (DFII10 - DFII2) inverts beyond -50bp
Enter on next trading day
Minimum 90 calendar days between signals

data_sources_concrete.prices: yfinance: GLD (SPDR Gold Shares, launched Nov 2004), IAU (iShares Gold Trust), SPY
data_sources_concrete.fundamental: FRED: DFII10 (10-Year TIPS real yield), DFII2 (2-Year TIPS real yield, available from ~2004). Daily frequency. Free.

backtest_approach: Event study: Load FRED DFII10 and DFII2 daily data (2004-2025). Compute daily spread. Identify dates where spread inverts beyond -50bp. For each signal (90-day min gap), long GLD for 63 trading days vs SPY. Key expected events: 2006-2007 (pre-GFC inversion), 2019 Q3-Q4 (pre-COVID inversion), 2022 Q4-2023 Q1 (post-hiking-cycle deep inversion). The academic evidence (Erb & Harvey FAJ 2013) supports the real rate / gold relationship.
known_events: 2006-2007: Real curve inverted as Fed tightened; gold rallied from $600 to $1,000 over next 18 months
2019 Q3: Real curve inverted; gold rallied 20% from Aug 2019 to Aug 2020
2022 Q4-2023: Deep real curve inversion after aggressive Fed hikes; gold rallied from $1,600 to $2,000+
implementation_notes: FRED DFII2 (2-year TIPS real yield) data starts around 2004, coinciding with GLD launch. The -50bp threshold captures the deepest decile of inversions. Gold's relationship with real yields is one of the most robust in macro finance. bt_feasibility is 5 because FRED TIPS data is daily, clean, and GLD is extremely liquid.
